			- 80/20 Making Money
			  collapsed:: true
			  #80-20-Business
				- Be Good At Something With a Demand/Have a Good Product With a Demand = Make Money
				- _Simplified process_
					- ONE
						- Identify a group of customers
						- Look for something they need
						- Develop a high quality item that meets their needs
						- Connect with the audience and lure them to your sales pitch
						- Pitch your product so well that the deal closes itself
					- OR
						- 1. Find a lucrative market and product,
						- 2. Make a place to sell it and advertise, and
						- 3. Sell it and get paid.
					- Create a landing page and buy traffic

			- Business 101
			  collapsed:: true
				- First-time ((6497031b-5e06-451d-b593-021e3ec1965f)) should sell existing products
					- First time entrepreneurs aren't best equipped to get prototypes built in china or outsource the creation of software or an info product
						- Easiest way to get started = sell people something that already exists e.g. affiliate marketing or eCommerce
						- #1 danger of starting any business is creating something that no-one wants
						- Until you understand a market deeply it's lower risk to be the middle man and sell existing products
						- Then all you have to learn is #MarketingStrategy, which is in itself a huge task
						- If you instead try to become a product creator too and you'd have to learn a ton more
				- This tells me you first need to read Think and Grow Rich by Napoleon Hill.
				- Then read a general red pill business book like The Millionaire Fastlane to get an overview of entrepreneurship fundamentals.
				- After that read some articles on Minimum Viable Products and case studies using MVPs and get started building a business. You can learn the rest as you go along.
				-
				- 4 hour workweek
					- The 4-Hour Workweek, Expanded and Updated: Expanded and Updated, With Over 100 New Pa
					- The business building stuff made it seem very easy when in reality it's far more difficult to find a winning idea.
					- But that's the value in this book. It makes everything seem a lot simpler which ensures you actually take action.
					- Most people who read books never take action on it so by virtue of him writing the book this way he's made millions of people become more successful than they would have otherwise.
					- His non-business ideas on becoming location independent were revolutionary as well.
					- No-one else put together such a concise guide to quitting your job, leaving your country and finding new places and things to do.
					- Then you add in fantastic chapters on goal-setting, productivity, comfort challenges, lifestyle design etc and you realise that this book has far more value than the haters would like you to think.
					- +1 on it being a must read.
				- For anybody who is not sure where to start, Y Combinator's How to Start a Startup course is free and has tons of useful info http://startupclass.samaltman.com/
				- RE: The Entrepreneur / Business Owner's / Self Employed Lounge
					- A few thoughts:
					- Read The Lean Startup and learn all about Minimum Viable Products
					- Don't quit your day job til you've got >1 year savings
					- Plan how you will automate the business even before you start it. A business you can't automate and sell is a career (not necessarily a bad thing but just know what you're getting into)
					- Assume that your business idea will fail to be profitable. But it won't matter because you should only pursue business ideas which will give you some form of long term value even if they do fail eg a useful skill like sales, high value contacts, niche market knowledge etc
					- Bootstrap abroad if your business allows you to be location independent eg many places in SEA are cheap and have fast internet
					- Read less. Do more. Don't get caught in the trap of thinking you need more knowledge - just try new things and see what sticks.

												- http://centralitymedia.com/google-alerts-affiliate-method/ hootsuite Make sure I'm getting at least 100 alerts/day, update with new keywords periodically http://www.rosshudgens.com/link-building-with-google-alerts-a-guide/ Use good anchor text but mix it up so it's natural Instead of searching for the product itself, search for places where people might need a product like that. So, for example, if you are reviewing toasters, don’t search for “toasters” as you’ll just get generic results. Search for places where people might need toasters! For example, search for sites related to kitchens or groups talking about breakfast. You will need a “Home” page, a simple “About” page, and a “Contact” page where people can contact you.
											- Here is a brief overview of this method before I go into more depth:
											- Pick an evergreen niche (eg making money online, weight loss, dating) Create a review blog Use Google search & BuzzBundle for prospecting Respond to all opportunities Build long-term traffic sources Repeat consistently
											- 1. Identify a Niche
											- 2. Build Your Website
											- Also, it is a good idea to get a custom logo made, but don’t worry about breaking the bank. There are several great options on fiverr.com — you can’t beat a logo for just $5!
											- Create Review Posts You will want to start off with between 3 and 5 products. I suggest that you start with products that are popular and have high consumer interest and demand — you will see why this is important when I move onto the next step below. For the best results, you should either buy the product or request a review copy. You also don’t want the review to sound like an advertisement. Write it from a first-person point of view — review posts that have the “I” influence makes them appear genuine. For example, “After using this product for two week I already noticed a change in my body, and according to my scale I lost 5 pounds, so I was very impressed with the results” makes a much bigger impact than, “This product does a great job and leads to weight loss.” Put some effort into this part, because your reviews will be directly responsible for converting your website traffic into sales. The more in-depth and convincing your reviews are, the more sales you are going to make.
											- Insert Your Affiliate Links
											- Pro Tip 1 – Commenting Strategy
											  collapsed:: true
												- Unique, non-spam comments
												  collapsed:: true
													- eg: “I was in the same boat as you about six months ago. I had no idea what to believe online because everything seemed to be an advertisement. I actually read a personal review about [your product] and it seemed like the person had great success using the product so I gave it a try. I couldn’t have been more excited about the results, as I am now 50 pounds lighter than when I first started using it. The article was very helpful to me — if you want to check it out yourself you can read it here” [link to your review article].”
													- Remember, the more products you review, the more traffic you can generate. When you get rolling this directly translates into more sales and profit for your business. With regards to commenting/posting, just be intelligent and not overly commercial when posting. Things like “hey check out this link” won’t work. But if you join in conversations and add value with your links, you will be fine (and it will convert much better). I personally don’t cloak links on forums but on blogs/social media it can be sensible to avoid getting your site banned. "I always check out this website before buying any products in [niche name] - [website link]. My personal favorite is [product X - a random product from the review site] because [whatever, whatever, whatever]."
												- Facebook The Facebook commenting system allows “top commenters” to display links as hyperlinks rather than text. Getting the top commenter status will therefore increase traffic considerably. What criteria you need to meet to get this status in comments is variable but the more likes your comment gets, the higher your chances.
												- Disqus Websites that use the Disqus commenting system will ban users who constantly post the same link even if you change the comment surrounding it. To get around this, consider using proxy servers to change up your IP address and different accounts on a regular basis. Other users of this method have started to see accounts banned within 10 posts, so for blog commenting to be effective you need to be discrete.
												- Blogging The key thing to remember when you are commenting on blogs is to always add value to the post you are contributing to. Failing to do this is the quickest way to get your comment deleted. It’s this consistent commenting and adding value that will create steadily increasing, profitable traffic.
												- Other things to note: Don’t constantly link back to the same page on your site. Keep a healthy domain by linking to various inner pages too. On most blogs, inputting your website will change your name to a hyperlink. Vary up linking from your name and from within the comment itself. Don’t assume high quality news sites will delete your link. Some don’t even check.

								- $3k Create video-course membership sites in a JV with social media inflencers
								  collapsed:: true
									- How to
									- How to make money in affiliate marketing without having a list, product, or startup money
									- Ok, I know that’s a big claim. It’s the ol’ “No list, no product, and little or no investment” claim that is truly the Holy Grail in the on-line marketing space. But in the next few minutes, I’m going to give you the blueprint that I used to generate $674,900 in revenue in 2012.
									- Step 1: Find people with a big following or list, but aren’t currently monetizing it (particularly with continuity).
									- Now this may sound like a hard task, but it’s actually very easy. I started off by doing a search in LinkedIn under that heading of “groups”. Go to the search bar at the top of the page in LinkedIn, and click the dropdown menu to search “groups”, then type in anything that people are interesting in. For example, when I just typed in “Fitness” in there, dozens and dozens of groups pop up that have thousands and thousands of members.
									- When you do this, the information about the group owner is public information. I simply call the owner of the group and use a script that is in essence a conversation that asks him (her) if they are currently monetizing their list. The most common answer is “what do you mean by that?”, and that answer is music to my ears!
									- The same thing can be done with FaceBook fan pages. There are thousands of fan pages where the leader is just doing the fan page for “fun” and have large followings of “ready to take action” people.
									- The cool part of Facebook fan pages is, you can actually see how “engaged” the fans are by comparing how many fans there are to the number right beside that which is how many of the fans are “talking about this”.
									- For example, as I write this today, there is a FB fan page called “Juicing Vegetables”. It currently has 291,541 fans with 224,313 “talking about this”!!! That’s an insanely high engagement factor of 76.9%. (no need to call him as I’m already working with this very cool guy that is helping hundreds of thousands of people J )
									- As you do these searches, you will see there are thousands of groups and fan pages with millions of people on them, and the leader is not currently monetizing their following. So these people are masters of building a list, and many of them just don’t have the time to focus on the monetization of them. Their amazing gift is building a “herd”.
									- So then what I do is contact them using my scripts, then those that are interested in helping their crowd by making cool “no brainer” offers, I then go on to step 2.
									- Step 2: Ask Their Crowd What They Want To Buy….
									- I then give this message to the group owner and have them send it to their groups via postings in the groups, and via Facebook messaging / LinkedIn messaging / and emails (if they have their email addresses).
									- Here’s the message I give them to send out:
									- “What is the one thing that you need the most help with now?”
									- That’s it! Basically it’s just asking them “What do you want to buy?”
									- Ok, so now we have your list and we have their response on the topics of what they want help with, now we move on to Step 3.
									- Step 3: Go To Google And Search For Pre-Done Courses On The Same Topics They Said They Wanted Help With
									- For instance, we have one group that we have worked with that was on the topic of “Sewing”. Their group said they wanted to learn how to make pants. So I simple went on Google, found a course on How To Make Pants, paid the owner of that course a single one time $25 for the rights to sell it a thousand times, and me and the list owner get to keep 100% of the profits. These are called “PLR Courses”. Which stands for Private Label Rights Courses. There are thousands of them on almost any topic you can think of. You can Google PLR to get more info on them.
									- Another good site to get “free courses” that we then package up and sell is on E-How.com There are thousands of free courses there where you can contact the creator of the course to get permission to distribute it to your group and you and the group owner keep virtually 100% of the profits. (I just use a good script when contacting the person who made the course).
									- I find the best courses are ones that are done as video, and again, there are thousands of them on just about any topic you can think of.
									- Step 4: Create A Continuity Membership Website For Each Group
									- About 3 years ago I took a course that changed my life and thousands of people that are in these groups. It was a course offered by Ryan Deiss called Continuity BluePrint. That course gave me the ability to run multiple continuity sites and generate millions of dollars (literally) by focusing on helping thousand upon thousands of people.
									- So in the example that we are talking about today, you can get these PLR courses for about $15 to $30 each, then put them in a membership site, that you then offer to these large groups and you and the group owner shares the profits.
									- Step 5: Get A Project Manager To Help You Do All The Work
									- So the next step is to get somebody to help you find the PLR courses, set up the landing pages, set up the shopping carts, and set up the membership websites. There are some great sites on the web that are full of these Project Managers that are looking for somebody just like you to lead a project.
									- The typical split is 45% to the group leader, 45% to me, and 10% to the project manager that does most of the actual work. I typically do not pay any “upfront” money to the project manager. They get paid their % when the funds start coming
									- in.
									- Step 6: Find Cool Ways To Frivolously Spend Your New Found Fortune
									- Ok, just kidding about the “Frivolously” thing, but you get the idea here. This is a very simple way to get:
										- An Instant List
										- An Instant Product
										- Somebody Else Does All The Work
										- Make An Impressive Income
									- Imagine what it will look like when you have 3 or 4 of these membership sites that are helping people and are generating cash coming to you automatically like an assembly line. Let me tell you, it’s really really cool!
									- As stated in the title to this article, you can then use these groups to make ongoing affiliate offers to which provides another great source of income for you and the group owner.
									- So this is meant as a quick overview of what I have been doing and what you can copy. Due to space limitations, we can’t go into all the details here. I’m going to be doing an on-line workshop on just this subject. You are welcome to join me on that by simply emailing me atCarl@TheMarketingAnimals.com
									- Please be sure to put Ryan’s name in the subject line so I can invite you to the correct on-line class.
									- Here’s to your success!!
									- -Carl White
								- $4k Find cheap places to buy ad space THEN create video-course membership sites
								  collapsed:: true
									- Instead of looking at the keywords for niche ideas. Here is one idea... look at the ad space. Look at where you can buy media at good prices and then design a product or campaign around that. The above campaign was on a site that had a great community, I'm not talking about a site like facebook, but a niched community. In the above example I used a site calledwww.goodreads.com , the site is a great place for readers and writers to learn about new books that are not simply main stream NYtimes best sellers... 
									  
									  So.. looking at the traffic source you can now say... "What can I offer to Readers and Writers?" 
									  
									  A better question is what do readers and writers want? or need? 
									  
									  Our answer to the above question was " to be published". From that point we now create a product offering, it could be a standalone product or like we did a monthly membership. I personally like the recurring income of a membership which is how you really get those 7X+ multiples on your investment. The best thing about this strategy is that with RTB (real time bidding) you can test an ad space for $5-$15 to see how it's impressions are converting. 
									  
									  Overall Strategy:
									  Use places like:
									  
									  to find places to advertise. 
									  
									  Develop a product specifically for that ad block, and multiple your investment. Just to make good on my title of this thread. The above campaign we scaled up and turned that $1000 into $6,000/M in recurring payments. After member retention rates kick in and all other expenses aside, we'll make around a $56,000 yearly profit. 
									  
									  The key now is to be fresh and not fall into banner blindness..

									- Why/how
									  collapsed:: true
									  This is the sort of thing that causes me to think about incubators. Have a staff of guys just to work out these ideas and put them into play. But then most guys seriously lack usable skills (I’m totally ready! – just isn’t enough in the real world), or they’re on top of things and likewise already busy. Then you’ve got a bunch of big picture sharks that just add a layer that doesn’t benefit me in any way. The human element is always the biggest snag in business.
									  And also the thing that mainly fucks you over is spreading yourself too thin. One idea, it doesn’t have to be a mind bender like the yoga app, just done right can make you all the money you need, and happy with your business life.
									  But there it is. If you’re worried about your ideas getting ripped off, don’t be. We might as well share thoughts and see if there’s enough smart ones here to benefit all participants.
									  
									  
									  months ago, i had a brilliant, genius revolutionary idea to totally flip that whole thing on its head. yoga and apps and whatnot. or maybe just sort of genius and just barely maybe not quite revolutionary. still, good. better. propositioned it to some broke and bored and talented friends, offering free room and board and equity and whatnot. like a magical unicorn landing on their front porch, offering them salvation and gold nugglets.
									  they all elected to instead get minimum wage jobs or just sit at home and complain about life. while wanting to participate, without making any actual commitment. and you wonder why some people are always broke and complaining about their lives. mysterious, really.
									  what do you do when you get that kind of offer from a dude who’s got it figured and has money, and a complete business plan, and willing to pay your way, living on the beach? but whatever. not like it really mattered. and yet i’m always keeping an eye out for interesting potential players for new ventures, but nothing’s turned up so far.
									  
									  
									  live rent free at the beach in paradise, a bedroom in my house is yours. grab one of the motorbikes, too. i’m buying all the market food (as long as you’re not on a regimen to gain weight for a bodybuilding competition). i’m paying for coffees, and gas. basic life is free. you are point dude on the project and various other marketing bits, to keep you busy. it’s not a vacation, but an internship. bootcamp, but with shared winnings, whatever they might be. if you’re at the start of a guru empire, then pat yourself on the back for having taken the chance.
									  or maybe you’re just rich and bored, and could use more hot girls too. either way.
									  idle talk though, really. though, all that internshippiness is theoretically possible as well. job #1 would be to compel me to make above offer not just idle talk. exactly the sort of amazing ability of intuition paired with persistence that it’d take to make this project culminate in hot girls and money.

								- $12k 'Your Perfect Teacher' Platform

									- Jake's Yoga App idea
									  “Your Perfect Teacher” aka. the Yoga App
									  You notice how all the yoga apps on all platforms are sort of meh?
									  There are some decent ones. Consistently done video, attractive hosts, separate audio tracks for instruction and music, ways to mix and match postures to make little lesson plans. Not too bad.
									  But the whole thing totally misses the boat on the big picture.
									  Because, what should a Yoga app really be? Joe Bob’s online Yoga studio? Meh, all that.
									  The real kicker would be a Yoga app instructor platform. You create the infrastructure, the rules, the things that make a consistent experience for the user, no matter who they choose as instructor. Set it up that whoever yoga studio queen can take the template for each posture (which is exactly defined as to length, visual style, etc), and upload her own version of the 30 second warrior pose and the 1 minute warrior pose, and fill in all the other pose options if she wants.
									  And then the consumer can go on the yoga platform app and pick his favorite instructor, his favorite voice over (you could just do hummmm shanti shanti pose instruction voices since everything is specifically scripted that everything matches no matter who the instructor is). Pay a buck for your favorite yoga thing, we keep a cut, they get a cut.
									  Now you have every yoga studio on earth that can expand their audience past their studio. And everybody who wants to do yoga but not be confined to their local places, pick whatever they want. Live in Kansas but want an Indonesian yoga chick? Easy! Live in France but want the black yoga girl but french voiceovers? Done! A buck here or there, way cheaper than a studio, but everybody wins.
									  
									  
									  That’s what’s missing, on a massive scale.
									  And it’s not just Yoga. Take any sort of instruction like that, and specifically apply this format of scripting the experience 100%, and letting just individual personalities do the delivery. Because that is the beauty and point of having any teacher, having somebody you relate to. And that’s mostly just tone, facial expression, body language. You can make the perfect lesson in terms of mechanics, and then “skin” it with instructors.
									  That’s a zillion dollar industry right there and it’s not being done yet. You could have the exact same language lesson from a thousand different instructors, who get paid if you choose that particular one.
									  Build the Yoga app since it’s a big market, first. Then use the same platform, staff, infrastructure to expand it to other learning concepts. Get it all built and cash flowing on Yoga, learn the marketing lessons, make the PR friends, and then go after Chinese kids wanting to learn English from a hot blonde German chick. Or whatever … it’s the mega niche machine.
									  And why am I not doing it?
									  One lazy pirate can only do so much. Above, just one of a dozen ideas that would potentially totally define a new market. The thing really takes a group of at least half a dozen highly skilled and motivated people dealing with individual areas to even think about starting to pull off (coding, talent management, video production, scripting, PR, and biz admin stuff).
									  
									  
									  Why not turn it into a paid membership for yoga studios that by being a nominal fee of say 99$/month, they can get exposure to students around the world, 24/7? You could also offer these students access to recorded videos from hot yoga teachers of their choice. If they want to, you could up sell them to get highly personalized and customized lessons tailored to their needs? Even offer them an even higher personal service of getting live lessons from their favorite yoga master for a measly 79$/30 minutes. Or something alone those lines. Just throwing some ideas out there.

									  Brilliant concept, quite similar to what J referred to in his initial post about that yoga app. Learning language from sexy natives, be it women or guys (to target the female gringas) can be not only very lucrative but also not that difficult to pull off. It can easily be scaled into an empire fast! Sexyspanish, sexyportuugese, sexyfrench, sexyswedish, sexythai, sexyviet, sexypinay, sexyjapanese, sexybahasa etc...you get the idea. 
									  
									  Post up in a country for say 6-12 months to get it off the ground. Hire a few hot girls and have them teach in a seductive and sexy way. Market the crap out of it and I can easily see that little project take off.

								- Cold calling with affiliate offers
								  collapsed:: true
									- know its a “no-no” to give away freebies but f-k it. I was once a lost soul on this blog.
									  If you follow these steps to a tee you can turn 200 bucks into 100k (minimum) in 2016:
									  
									  Phase 1
									  1) Buy the software ‘scrapebox’
									  2) Watch youtube tutorial on how to scrape phone numbers / start scraping for google search [city][business]
									  3) Locate legitimate affiliate offer that you, yourself would buy (perfect example “evoice”)
									  
									  Notes:
									  a) FTC’s “do not call” list does not protect B2B calls
									  b) Use small-med biz like lawyer, carwash, strip club, dentist, construction etc. When you factor in additional permutations like dui, divorce, worker comp (lawyers) you will see just how many options you have
									  c) Execute. Do not train, do not write a script, just do it (do study the product your selling, however). I would listen to gangster rap and then start burning through numbers
									  
									  You have 30k+ numbers, do not waste time trying to win over someone who doesn’t want the product. Also, don’t waste time on the secretaries. The time:reward ratio is infinitively better if you’re a prick.
									  
									  Tried and true line for getting past secretaries:
									  
									  “This is [X]. Can i speak to [boss] please.” Sound annoyed, make it sound like an order. When she asks the reason for the call just say its urgent and repeat line one. After doing this a few dozen times you’ll actually start getting mad when they inconvenience you. The power trip is real.
									  
									  Its like picking up chicks – don’t waste time on high-maint when there are single/horny ones around the corner. Call, get past the gate keeper, then say whats up to the decision maker tell him what your selling and ask if he wants it.
									  
									  If you have a sweet offer, thousands of people on your list have thought about it at one time or another. I would buy the offer I mentioned above if you called me and sounded confident.
									  
									  Phase 2
									  Repeat process with your own offer. Keep it all in house. Hire additional callers using craigslist or PUA forums because those guys are hungry as F-K.
									  
									  Bonus points for rebills.
									  
									  Notes:
									  a) Use 1099-misc forms for any caller you pay over $600
									  b) Your service needs to be somewhat expensive with good margins cause its hard to find callers for anything less than $100/conversion because people hate cold calling (except PUAs, seriously those guys stopped caring a long time ago)
									  c) If you don’t have at least an LLC by this point GET ONE
									  
									  If you do exactly what I just said you will be rich, I promise. Good luck.

												  this guy, he is on fiver and elance and odesk (or whatever it’s called now) and he hires minions. he doesn’t need minions. he hires them for micro gigs that are just the same thing over and over again. five bucks. ten bucks. same gig. hundreds and hundreds of times.
												  he’s got a spreadsheet.
												  on it, he keeps track of timeliness, outcome, contact information, reliability, skill set.
												  if you ever tried to find a good resource minion online, you know where this is going.
												  you can go to this guy and pay a 10% premium over what you’d pay otherwise, and actually get the very thing done that you need. so you can either go on odesk yourself and waste days and weeks and months and moneys of your time, till you find somebody good. or you pay this guy 10% more, and get the right guy the first time.
												  also i gave him this idea. he’s one out of about a hundred guys who (shockingly) actually followed through. worked in a call center when i met him. now he makes an average 15k a month from his spreadsheet.

												- Improve first meeting
													- Use mini projector (needs dark room) + PowerPoint for a more persuasive initial meeting
													- Meeting script
														- On laptop open TripAdvisor to bottom ranked of seminyak.
														- Then cold walk in each hotel with iPhone showing me their rank on TA
														- SCRIPT
														- *Concerned look on face like possible annoyed customer* "Hello, I'm looking to speak to the manager here."
														- Okay thanks, what's their name by the way?
														- Dodge the "What's it about?" Question for as long as possible, vaguely say "it's about occupant/customer reviews"
														- If manager comes:
														- Hello X, I'm wondering is now a bad time?
														- Okay is there somewhere we can sit and talk?
														- Meeting:
														- I was quite concerned because I've recommended friends to come to this hotel but going online I see its ranked X!
														- I don't know if you're aware how serious this is *present White Paper*
													- > Speak slower
													- add interactive questions in script with forced answers
													- > Make it more interactive - personal questions ie banter
													- > Ask them at the beginning "Is now a bad time?" for the investment by them inviting me to sit OR reschedule
													- > Ask them how many customers they've had and calculate their conversion to review ratio.
													- > Say I found hotel by searching up the hotels with the worst reputation. Psychologically push them off the cliff but then offer them a hand
													- eg encryption = have you heard of NSA? They pay businesses called intercept partners to weaken their security. For example, Microsoft was named in PRISM. This means not only is your security weak vs NSA, but also weaker vs hackers and viruses, and your data is being read by intercept partners who sell it to advertisers
													- Have a diagram with PRISM companies to NSA/GCHQ/ other foreign intelligence. Flow arrow to PRISM with money, in exchange for data. Then you at the top?
													- > Reduce size of report and tailor it so I know it deeply and intuitively (manager was eating out of my hand the whole time but English was poor)
													- > Ask their occupancy rate at peak and currently. Emphasise the pain
													- Add questions into pitch - investigation is very important for determining my pitch
													- THEN followup and demonstrate my credibility - what value I can provide (rather than not linking my features to a perceived benefit)
													- Copy notes from SPIN selling
													- > Emphasise I do recurring billing because it means I have to keep providing high quality results to clients, or they can stop working with me. Others take your money and go. Also means enables me to focus on providing long-term value to clients, so once you get a taste of the results you consider other services
													- > Bring tablet or laptop to show them what people are saying about them online, their ratio of bad:good reviews on tripadvisor, my review site sample and possibly sample website design
													- Spend money on appearance
													- clothes could be sharper, bag nicer, busines card better etc

														- Create page for each individual package

													- Show them their mobile site immediately

													- Gatekeepers
														- Make friends with everyone you meet. If there is a receptionist, smile like you just won the lottery. Try to be cheery and humorous. People like to laugh and feel better when you walk out than they did when you walked in. You need the receptionist to tell the owner "There was a really friendly guy here today and he left this for you to take a look at"
														- A little charm goes a long way, too. Piola spends a few minutes with each one he meets, tossing a compliment or two, cracking a few jokes. He smiles a lot, which comes naturally.
														- If you leave a business and the owner is not in, be sure to ask "When is the best time of the day to speak with him? Will he be in tomorrow? Now you can call the business when you know he's in and say that you were speaking to "Mary". You have the owner's name so you can ask for him directly. Very important, especially if its not "Mary" who answers the phone.
														- "I wonder if you could help me out?" opening line. Everybody likes to help," says Piola. "Put people in that posture right away."
														- "I represent Systematic. We perfect your business systems to increase profits and adaptability. Our clients include Bell Atlantic, a lot of doctors and hospitals, and even the Philadelphia 76ers. I happened to be in your building, and I wondered if I might see whichever partner handles your finances." His tone is upbeat and cordial.
														- The best time to reach a decision maker is early in the morning or late in the day. "Try to catch people on their way into the office, before the secretary arrives. That minimizes interference."
														- ask for the appointment right away: 'Would Tuesday be OK, or would Wednesday be better?' Go on the assumptive."
														- Is there anywhere we can sit down - anywhere we can go discuss this?
													- Do you have a 'quick minute' > asking for 10 mins
													- The more qualified the prospect, the more likely the sale. leads generated by telemarketing, direct mail, and trade shows.
														- cold calling just to set the appt. This is a huge time saver.
														- Outsource - Pay per lead booking appointments
														- Though if you're already in the area it makes to cold walk in addition

													- Find out their name + repeat it

													- Good morning, please put me through to Mary Smith /
														- When the gatekeeper answers the phone simply say the person’s name you are trying to get through to, followed by ‘thank you’. For example, “John Smith, thank you”.
														- When they ask your name, a simple “Joe Bloggs, thank you” will do.
														- hey will then likely ask for the name of your company, again simply say “Punch!”
														- Some gatekeepers may put you through at this point, but the more confident among them may push a third question and ask the purpose of the call, again give the minimum information, “marketing strategy”
														- maybe not this curt, be short and assertive so they don't make the judgement call.
													- Get the name from LinkedIn, receptionist or gatekeeper
													- The one who asks the questions is the one who controls the call (redirect with a question).
													- Good morning, would you put me through to Dave Gadlay's office
													- Who else is involved with you on this decision?
													- Do you have a budget for this project?
													- Script your original message in under 30 seconds.
													- My name is Aaron Silva and calling regarding increasing profits through business systems.
														- Who in your company is responsible for that? By the way, who am I speaking with?
														- Thank you, Anne. I really need your help. Who in your company would I talk to regarding the telephone system?"
														- “Hi, it’s John Austin, is Paul in?” (His first name + my full name, but no company name — that makes it obvious that he doesn’t know you and you should be denied.) / This is my name and I am calling for Mr. Johnson,"
														- They may ask you to leave a message, trying to get more information about why you're calling-in essence, sizing you up. Don't go into a great amount of detail. You're not trying to sell the assistant on your business, just on yourself.
														- When you finish your initial call, take a quick moment to write a note to Anne and mail it off:
														- Dear Anne,    I enjoyed talking with you on the telephone. I hope the company realizes how important the first image is when someone answers the phone. You do a wonderful job. Thank you in advance for all your help.

Phone Burner automates all of this, and can make 85 - 90 calls in an hour.

												- Affiliate Proposal call
												  **Script**
												  "Good morning/afternoon, I'm wondering if you can help me... _(can you speak English_) 
												  _choices:_
												  I'd like an introduction to the owner/manager
												  **OR** Can you tell me the owner's name > Are they in/I'd like an introduction
												  
												  **Contingencies:**
												  **<i>Asked name:</i>**
												  Aaron Silva
												  **<i>Asked purpose</i>**
												  Pitch Trailer - "I'm calling regarding increasing profits through expanding your services."
												  **<i>Asked for proposal</i>**
												  I'm from Systematic Scale and we're a business services company.  
												  We noticed that currently you only sell web designs, but your customers are often looking for much more like digital marketing, data security, online infrastructure, productivity management software, etc.
												  Offering these services as well would make you more money, but would require a lot of time and money to get started when you're already busy with your web design clients.
												  So we at Systematic Scale would like to partner up and we'll fulfill these new services for you, without having to invest any of your own time or money.
												  So would you like to make more money from your clients?
												  
												  Great, if you give me your email address I'll send you some further information. 
												  
												  **<i>Transferred blind with no name intro:
												  </i>**Hello, who am I speaking with?
												  **<i>Told to leave a message:</i>**
												  I'm  usually out most of the day and I'm sure he is very busy. Rather than 
												  having him waste time trying to reach me, I'll just call back. When is 
												  the best time to reach him?
												  **<i>She doesn't know when he'll be available:</i>**
												  That's ok, please leave a message for him that Aaron Silva called regarding 
												  increasing profit for the company and that I'll call back later today.
												- Affiliate Proposal email
												  Hello **X**,
												  
												  Just to refresh your memory on our phone call earlier my name is Aaron Silva, I'm from Systematic Scale and we're a business services company that wants to help you make more money by allowing you to expand your range of services.
												  
												  We noticed that currently you sell a few services, but your customers are often looking for much more like data security, online reputation management, productivity software, etc.
												  
												  Offering these services in addition to your current services would make you more money, but of course would require a lot of time and money to get started when you're already busy with existing clients.
												  
												  So we at Systematic Scale would like to partner up and we'll fulfill these services for you.
												  
												  This means you can make more money from existing, previous and new clients without investing any extra time or money.
												  
												  **Your New Range of Business Services**
												  
												  We primarily work with the hospitality industry in Bali (e.g. restaurants, hotels, villa management agencies) but these services are useful to almost any kind of business today (especially if they already have a website).
												  
												  Attached to this email is a copy of our full services list which you can promote on your website, emails, inperson, etc.
												  
												  We also highly recommend that you:
												  
												      Email all previous & existing customers offering your new range of services
												      And make sure to inform every new customer whilst negotiating their first project 
												  
												  **Getting Paid**
												  
												  Commissions for each successful referral are Rp 400,000 and are paid through monthly standing order.
												  
												  We ask every new client who they were referred by so you can rest assured you will be properly credited for each referral (and if you doubt us you can always ask your own customers whether they've signed up with us or not). We're here to be partners for long-term mutual benefit, not like some scamming idiots who are in for short-term gain.
												  
												  Once you have your first commission we'll collect your full contact info and payment details so we can pay you and send monthly earnings reports.
												  
												  **And that's all there is to it!**
												  
												  You're now free to start promoting your new services and earn your first commission!
												  
												  We know that this will massively increase your average customer spending and thus increase business profits so make sure to promote your new offerings wherever you can!
												  
												  We're looking forward to working with you :)
												  
												  If you have any questions feel free to reply to this email or you can contact me 0812 2029 9785.
												  
												  Regards,
												  -- 
												  Aaron Silva
												  Business Developer
												  Systematic Scale

												- Consultation Proposal call
												  **Open LibreOffice Calc doc**
												  
												  **Mindset:**
													- Do not give the gatekeeper your pitch. Don’t let them be the judge. You're not trying to sell the assistant on your business, just on yourself. Never take a “no” from someone who doesn’t have the authority to give you a “yes.”
													- Aim high - whoever you end meeting up with you'll get more respect depending on who you started with
													- A good cold caller knows that you only divulge the minimum amount of information with each question
													- Take ownership of the next contact - He’s never going to call you back so instead find out when he’ll be back and then call back.
													  
													  													  **Script**
													  													  "Good morning/afternoon, I'm wondering if you can help me... _(can you speak English_) 
													  													  _choices:_
													  													  I'd like an introduction to the owner/general manager
													  													  **OR** Can you tell me the owner's name > Are they in/I'd like an introduction
													  													  **OR **"I'm calling regarding increasing profits through business systems. I'm a little unsure of who I should be speaking to, who is in charge of your operations systems - like customer service, IT, marketing, that sort of thing.
													  
													  													  **Contingencies:**
													  													  **<i>Asked name:</i>**
													  													  Aaron Silva
													  													  **<i>Asked purpose</i>**
													  													  Pitch Trailer - "I'm calling regarding increasing profits through business systems.
													  													  **<i>Asked to clarify</i>**
													  													  "As in, enhancing your operations systems - like customer service, IT, marketing, that sort of thing
													  													  **<i>Asked for proposal</i>**
													  													  "I'm from Systematic Scale, a business automation company that helps you make more money.
													  													  We want to offer a free consultation to point out the weaknesses of your business which are costing you time and money. We've already identified several and would like to share them with you in this meeting."
													  													  **<i>Transferred blind with no name intro:
													  													  </i>**Hello, who am I speaking with?
													  													  **<i>Asked 'what are the weaknesses':</i>**
													  													  There are too many and it would take too long to explain over the phone. We're happy to share them with the decision maker inperson though.
													  													  **<i>Told to leave a message:</i>**
													  													  I'm usually out most of the day and I'm sure he is very busy. Rather than having him waste time trying to reach me, I'll just call back. When is the best time to reach him?
													  													  **<i>She doesn't know when he'll be available:</i>**
													  													  That's ok, please leave a message for him that Aaron Silva called regarding increasing profit for the company and that I'll call back later today.

												  Hello** X**,
												  
												  Thanks for your call back,
												  
												  My name is Aaron Silva, I'm from Systematic Scale and we increase your profit and save you time through improving your business systems e.g. your marketing, IT and human resources procedures.
												  
												  _The reason I'm contacting you is because I read reviews about your business which pointed out several alarming inefficiencies in your systems which are harming your customer experience and when I visited your website I found even more.
												  To fix these problems I immediately thought, well our _**<i>x system could fix Y</i>**<i> easily and I realised that there must many more blind spots where your systems are weak. So</i>
												  I live near your Surya Sumantri ZEN spa and since going there a number of times I've noticed a few things missing from your system which is wasting time and money.
												  For example: 1) Systems you are missing https://workflowy.com/#/50e35188cd44
												  
												  I could go on all day but I think you get the point - your systems are far from perfect and there are clearly many blind spots where things haven't been fixed. Which is where we come in.
												  
												  I'm from Systematic Scale, a business automation company that can increase your profit and save you time through improving your business systems e.g. your marketing, IT and human resources procedures.
												  
												  We're offering you a quick free consultation on your current business systems. You'll learn your strengths and weaknesses in the 5 core business systems which determine profit and be shown the blind spots where you may be at risk. If we're a good fit as well we can implement solutions to make you more money without needing to waste any of your time and energy. You can learn more about our free consultation here: http://systematicscale.com/free-consultation
												  
												  I live nearby in Surya Sumantri so let me know what times you are available for a meeting and I'll get back to you.
												  
												  Kind regards,
												  
												  Aaron Silva
												  Systematic Scale

														  <u>Description:</u> Staff just have to type in a customer's name and it will bring up all information about them on their dashboard. This helps them to see special requests, past conversations and other individual data about the customer in order to personalise the customer experience so that nobody feels like 'just another customer'. Allows quick record 
														  
														  
														  This contains:

														      c

														      Specialised CRM tools (meeting scheduling, product catalogs, workflows, activity management, graphical reporting etc

														  This price is £39.97/month and you can pay here.
														- Online Ranking Review Filter System
															- No system to improve online ranking - Currently there's no system in place which filters out negative reviews from being published online and encouraging lots more positive reviews. This is one of your biggest potential customer sources so it's vital that your online ranking is improved.
															- _Purpose_: To increase ranking on booking websites to gain more customers.
															- _Summary_: This system prompts more customers to write positive reviews on booking websites such as TripAdvisor, Agoda, [Hotels.com](http://Hotels.com) etc whilst also eliminating their ability to leave negative reviews, which pushes you towards the #1 hotel ranking.
															- Description: Customers who login to your Wi-Fi will be redirected to the Review Filter website and on checkout customers will be given a postcard which tells them to go to leave their review there(rather than on Hotels/Agoda etc directly). The system pushes all negative reviews to your email account (eliminating negative reviews being posted online where it harms your business permanently) and encourages much more positive reviews, which all go to the review site of your choosing.
															- **Review Filter System** - Right now your online ranking on review sites like TripAdvisor, Agoda, etc isn't good. Your online ranking is what brings you new customers. Our proprietary Review Filter System boosts your ranking by eliminating negative reviews and getting lots more positive reviews.
														- Automatic Marketing System
														  Having lots of emails for previous paying customers, yet nothing being done to retain customer loyalty and increase customer lifetime value
														  
														  **Automated Marketing System**
														  _Purpose_: To provide more value to customers and keep in regular contact with customers, thereby increasing reviews+referrals+repeat customers and thus profit. 
														  <u>Summary</u>:  Social media followers are nice to have but they don't convert to customers - they're there to browse, not buy. The most valuable thing is getting an email address because it allows you to directly communicate with customers wherever they are in the world and your marketing emails stay in their inbox until they're opened. This system involves setup of an infinitely scalable newsletter system to automatically deliver emails in a scheduled sequence e.g. day 1, then day 2, then day 4 etc. You'll also get detailed feedback statistics on % of people who open each email, if they clicked your web links inside, etc to further optimise the value of your automatic marketing system over time.
														  
														  <u>Description</u>: This system uses email newsletters with automated delivery sequences.

															- Proposal email
																- Hello Ayu,
																- When we spoke in person the other day the main thing you said you wanted was 'easy booking' to properly reflect the brand name 'Easy Booking Bali Villas' so this Phase 2 will focus primarily on creating your Easy Booking System.
																- Currently you have a very time-consuming manual booking process:
																	- Prospects have to submit a reservation.
																	- If dates not available customer service must request new dates from prospect.
																	- Customer service emails villa details and payment info.
																	- Customer service must confirm payment has been received.
																	- Customer service must email confirmation to customer.
																- This is only my guess of your process, it may even be longer than this!
																- To permanently eliminate this huge time suck (and headache) from both customers and employees, as well as live up to the name of 'Easy Booking' we'll totally automate this process as previously discussed.
																- The Unlimited Easy Booking System will include five components:
																	- Site-wide eCommerce software integration
																		- All villas will have a new 'product' format which is essential to place bookings on each page.
																		- It also allows the integration of automatic currency conversion, setting variable prices on dates, easy import/export etc.
																		- Will have baskets, checkout, account logins, etc.
																	- Software to automatically handle every aspect of their accommodation booking for unlimited villas & customers
																		- Offering discounts/coupons, capturing contact details, calculating fees and taxes, etc.
																		- Customers will be able to complete the whole process on your website quickly and easily through picking off their desired dates off a simple calendar and checking off the amenities they want.
																		- Back-end interface for staff will be a simple user-friendly calendar-based system (as that's what you're used to) and colour-coded so you can quickly see what dates are booked etc.
																	- SSL website security encryption (the padlock symbol you see on many websites) to protect customers financial data and increase trust.
																	- Payment gateway integration with MOLPay.
																	- Email newsletter software for unlimited emails & recipients
																		- This allows automatic email sequences to be sent to all customers with booking confirmation email
																		- Once you have their email address, you can automatically have everyone receive emails before their stay (reminders of what to bring on holiday), during their stay (things to do, see - from your affiliated partners) and after (prompting for reviews, referrals, repeat purchases etc)
																		- You'll also get access to detailed reports & visualisations of all stats (open rate, clicks, bounces, etc) to further optimise your email marketing
																		- This software can automatically personalise emails with their names, birthdays, send followup email sequences every week, every few months, yearly or whatever frequency you desire
																- By having this Unlimited Easy Booking System you'll gain a number of advantages:
																	- Huge time savings - Countless hours will be saved for employees by eliminating the time-consuming booking process. The free time gained can be used for marketing and post-sale customer service to actually make more money (or downsizing human resources to save more money), rather than unnecessary taking bookings manually which is vital time lost.
																	- Massively increased profits - A long-winded booking process turns off potential customers. They want it to be quick and easy to give you money. If it's not they'll use other booking sites instead or just choose your competitors, both of which decreases profit.
																	- A scalable business - Right now if you suddenly got tonnes of bookings it would be overwhelming. Everyone would be too busy to do any real work because they would be too preoccupied with just the booking process. "Be prepared for success" is what they say, but currently your business model is fragile to increased bookings. The Unlimited Easy Booking System instead allows you to automatically handle 1000s of bookings without any stress or time wasted.
																- This will take up a significant amount of time but I can also implement a few of your most requested features in this Phase also:
																	- Automatic currency converter
																	- Setting up social media accounts
																	- TripAdvisor integrated into homepage
																- The Unlimited Easy Booking System is £33 per month flat rate for unlimited villas, customers, emails & recipients with a separate setup fee. Installing this system and as well as your most requested features is a one-time payment of only £697 which can be made here by clicking here.

														  **Inefficient training system** - At least 40 hours of productive time just used for training receptionists even (20 hours for new employee and 20 hours from the trainer), plus there is no way for employees to check the Standard Operating Procedures. 
														  
														  No efficient combined digital communication+project management system in order to give you and your employees more free time, by eliminating unnecessary followup and micromanaging so that you can focus on higher quality communication together
														  
														  Digital Standard Operating Procedures - Right now there's a lot of 1:1 training for certain job roles which is time-consuming and costs money vs automated training. These Standard Operating Procedures can be recorded once to eliminate the training period. We can teach your staff how to create & update these SOPs themselves as well rather than having management do all the work. These SOPs can then be accessed by new and existing employees at any time through their mobile phones or PC with a secure individual login.
														  
														  Digitising your Standard Operating Procedures - This saves massive time as it allows all employees to perform their tasks in the expected desire of your brand without you having to remind them constantly. It also allows you to capture the 'little tricks' that each employee learns about how to perform their tasks effectively, so this invaluable knowledge is recorded for the benefit of current & future employees. A further benefit is that this eliminates the training period - staff just log in and know how to do their job instantly.
														  
														  I'll setup up organised software which will contain all your future SOPs. Your employees will be trained using my existing tools for how to create these detailed SOPs, use them for all their tasks and maintain them independently.
														  
														  This is of course 2-for-1 because you get free project management software with it too - this means you can easily assign tasks to your employees from the dashboard and massively improve productivity. You'll be able to log into the software and see your employees progress on every single task 24/7 through the project view, Gantt chart or calendar overview.
														  
														  This price is £29.97/month and you can pay here.
														  
														  Digital Standard Operating Procedure System
														  _Purpose_: First this eliminates the training period, which gives your staff more free time to focus on their job and allows rapid training of new employees. Secondly this ensures that employees actually stick to using your SOPs - because they're accessible from anywhere and employees can check off the steps as they complete their tasks.
														  <u>Summary</u>: This system saves massive time as it allows all employees to perform their tasks in the expected desire of your brand without you having to remind them constantly. It also allows you to capture the 'little tricks' that each employee learns about how to perform their tasks effectively, so this invaluable knowledge is recorded for the benefit of current & future employees. A further benefit is that this eliminates the training period - staff just log in and know how to do their job instantly.
														  <u>Description</u>: This will be a browser and mobile app-based dashboard, so wherever employees are they will be able to access their SOPs. Your employees will also be trained using our private SOPs for how to create SOPs and update them where necessary, so they are able to use them for all their tasks and maintain them independently.

														  Inefficient management system - Right now there's no online dashboard where you can always view project progress, key performance numbers or have quick conversations related to a task. Instead the management process relies upon waiting until the scheduled meeting, hoping that task progress is communicated accurately through the various management levels, primarily focusing on day-to-day operations over long-term goals etc.

														  _Purpose_: To give you and your employees more free time, so you can focus on doing your respective tasks rather than worrying about deadlines, following up etc.

													- Security Systems
													  Unknown security systems - It is unknown what kind of data protection is currently in place.
													  Unknown backup systems - It is unknown if there is any automated data backup system is currently in place.
													  
													  PC Security - It appears you don't have protection against viruses, malware, trackers, spam, malicious scripts, hackers, etc etc. Basically anyone with a web connection (or a USB stick) can steal or destroy your files, and you don't even have backups.
													  
													  Data Encryption - Without encryption your files can be stolen or destroyed by hackers, viruses or a malicious employee. To solve this, encryption makes it impossible for others to access your passwords, files/folders, disks, servers, web connection etc.
													          Automatic Backups - If your files are destroyed by viruses, hackers, physical or digital accidents then you've lost irreplaceable data. We can setup automatic backups of all essential files in an encrypted form so that you don't lose vital parts of your business systems.
													  
													  
													  
													      First we would set you up with password, file and disk encryption so nobody can see your PC files. 										- $2k Security Alarm biz
										  source:: http://cedonulli.com/how-rich-do-you-wanna-be/
										  collapsed:: true
											- Jake's idea
											  collapsed:: true
												- ever notice how most of asia is literally something from the past? it’s a previous step in development. things we take for granted are just barely starting to happen in lots of other places. you want to be rich? start the next silicon valley, on some nice beach, in a college town. or just open a proper western style gym. go start an insurance company.
												- let’s use a more tangible, easy-to-do, specific hands on example. take ADT. the stupid alarm company. the whole scheme, rent the system, pay a monthly fee for “monitoring”. doesn’t exist where i’m staying at, right now. imagine that. it’s the past, the residential security scheme hasn’t yet been invented.
												- lots of nice houses here, and the police is unarmed and doesn’t even answer the phone at night. businesses with lots of inventory and things that just sit there at night, behind a few locks. and people with money come to live here. and there literally is not a single, singular, solitary secure-fuzzy-feelings provider. take that in for a moment. it’s unreal!
												- so what you do, so simple, and if you’ve got a grand to your name, you’re all set for startup costs.
												- you go online buy a hundred sim-card enabled alarm systems from china, you hire one big dude and one cute girl. you copy the marketing model and general scheme of ADT. and next week, you’re in business.
												- cute girl sells fear and doom door to door, with some burglary stats and whatnot. “monitoring central” big dude gets a phone and rides his motorbike to any place the alarm goes off. cost of your staff, hundred bucks a month each. you get a “deposit” for each alarm so that your inventory overhead goes to basically zero. you sell these fucking monitoring contracts to everybody. you slap the “protected by” on their gates. now the neighbor gets the pitch about, which house is the burglar going to pick on this street? the alarm warning one, or yours?
												- and there you go. next year you’re one of the really rich dudes in town.
												- have her find out the local thoughts on personal safety. go talk to a bunch of people with nice houses, float the idea of a managed security solution. have her sort out what sells, what people are willing to spend.
												- if that goes nowhere, whatever. salary is cheap.
												- if it does, have her figure out what decent price points might be. have her translate sales brochures from american security companies. make flyers. have her run to the post office when a shipment of security things arrive. have her go interview night guards to act as the guys driving around to locations where the alarm goes off. have her set up a monitoring office, deal with all the sim cards and setting up schedules and whatnot.
												- you can spend a hundred bucks a month to pay a really smart critter to take any number of business schemes that were successful in the west, and apply them elsewhere. NVO cell service? loan sharking? a tuner motorbike shop? you name it, they’ll figure it out for you. all you need is a copy of a successful playbook, and have them work the feasibility study you make up, little steps at a time.
										- Private Gym Ownership

											- Becoming a physical trainer is generally a losing battle. You’re trading your time for money. Starting an actual gym can be outstanding. Private trainers make $100+ per hour and if your knowledge exceeds theirs… You can monetize your physical health knowledge to create a business. (notice you’re combining many of the 10 skills here)
										- $4k Niche Rating Sites of Local Businesses
										  RE: Online Reputation Management hustle
										  
										  I was thinking about this method (setting this up, as we speak). Later on you could add another (evil) twist. If you really think about it, what are credit rating agencies? (Standard & Poor's, Fitch, Moody's, etc.). They are private companies that have other companies and even whole countries by their balls. Just because they started the rating and now everybody needs those damn ratings. Even the famous Michelin Guide. Michelin is a fucking tire manufacturer that took the noble approach to give restaurants & hotels some stars. Nowadays getting or losing a star can make or break your business.
										  
										  Why not use all the same arguments that have been presented here (thanks fistor and roi300x I look up to you) and also create a local rating site. If you don't have enough ratings for local businesses, than create one yourself. You don't have to tell the business owner that this is your site. Don't post fake reviews though. Let the card system fill the site with reviews. This could take some time to build up, but it could be well worth in some countries/cities. We could create "niche rating sites". Mechanical shops + city, Hair salons + city, etc. Some industries might not pay in the hundreds monthly, but it could become a profitable business venture.

												- i’ve been researching some stuff on international banking. keeping little piles in a dozen countries is all fine and good, but lately i’m feeling like i should get myself a bit better educated on how the real players do it. moving a little 10k USD around and getting hit with 600 USD in fees just isn’t so much awesome. so. digging on the google. stumbling accross sovereignman.com and internationalman.com. seen one of them previously somewhere, good reading anyway. the more i’m looking at their stuff, the more something totally and obviously brilliant dawns on me.
												- first though, take a look at this: The blatant theft of depositor money in Cypriot banks was at first peddled as a one-off emergency measure. Then a Freudian slip by the head of the Eurogroup finance ministers, Mr. Dijsselbloem, suggested this would be the new pattern for similar future events. Much back peddling and “clarification” ensued.
												  collapsed:: true
													- But don’t bother squinting as you try to read the lips of mumbling bureaucrats. Just follow what they’re doing, and you won’t get blind-sided. What have they been up to? In October 2011, the Financial Stability Board (FSB) – a tentacle of the Bank for International Settlements, the central bank for central bankers –released a report that proposed a new regime to resolve financial-institution instability. In the report, the FSB calls for solvency support for banks without taxpayer exposure and the allocation of losses to shareholders and unsecured and uninsured creditors. Deposits at a bank are considered a loan, and if a bank fails, its depositors become unsecured creditors for amounts that exceed the insurable limit. The whole sordid thing is here: http://www.internationalman.com/78-global-perspectives/920-the-tenuous-nature-of-borderless-money
												- You might get sucked into some more of the articles and whatnot. And sure. It’s interesting stuffs. If you play online enough, you might see what template all this is. A healthy dose of facts. Relevant facts. Facts that hit close to home, somewhere near a man’s balls. There’s the whole survivalist genre, conspiracy theory niche, etc etc. It’s easy to get sucked in. Because of the combination of how it’s put together. Facts that trigger the flight alerts in the brain. Mixed with a healthy dose of engaging narrative, a dash of fiction, and … analysis. Prediction of the future, logical in the way they set up the facts and narrative. The future? DOOM.
												- Let me tell you something. Your house will fall down. Eventually. You will get old and frail, and then your bones will turn to dust in the ground. Your children’s children will forget your name. The sun will melt the earth. All that.
												- But what’s the timeline? And relevance? What will happen in the interim, unexpected, that will actually affect you? So some shitty island takes 10% of the savings of a handful of block headed retirees. Wow. The end is nigh!
												- Look though, how internationalman spins that into an ever expanding circle of viciousness that inevitably ends up at … your balls. Or your bank account, in this case. And … soon. Soon, son! Run, hide, sign up for my newsletter, buy my ebook, come to my seminar!!!!!!!!! Heh. Did I just give away the ending? It’s an ultimately compelling format, tested by the oldest sales pitch around: religion. Mix facts with fiction, healthy dose of awesome narrative, get close to your balls, soul, bank account, whatever. Repent! Sign up for my newsletter! Buy my ebook! Come to my church! And yea, I do keep money in a handful of countries and a few currencies. I like contingency planning as much as the next guy.
												- Here’s the thing though, when it comes to that racket, and making money. If you aren’t trying to make money because of some passion project, you might as well not start out with barriers that will forever haunt you. Barriers like location dependence. Like inventory dependence. Like dependence on skilled labor. Or a limited prospective audience. Or 50% in taxes. You could start making carry bags for high end folding bikes, sell them at a shop in Portland. Over time you’d make some good money. Great, if it’s a passion project, and you love Portland. But if you are born to be an international pirate, forever in the next shadow of some faraway country, you don’t want any of that.
												- No inventory. No location dependence. No taxes. No currency limitations. And most of all, a RICH TARGET AUDIENCE.
												- Sovereignman and his peers got this figured out. A lot of guys I know start out with Internet marketing. Good start. But that builds no brand, no customer base, no residual, no safety net. It’s a good skill, though. But then you have to take that somewhere where you play on your own playing field, no Google’s. A built-in user base that expands your reach. Social media, whatever, stuff and stuff. Quite a few dudes do travel blogs. So short sighted. How much money do you make selling that dream? Guys that can afford it don’t need your blog. People do mom blogs and Etsy shops, and advertise travel insurance. No big picture perspective, there. If I had nothing else going on, here’s what I’d do: Take both those sites. Do you standard Internet marketing research. Alexa, keywords, reach, inbound links, whatever Internet marketing monkeys like to do. Get the full picture of the size of the thing, competitors, the lot. Use it to copy the delivery model. You already got the other key piece – the content.
												- Then, niche-ify the fuck out of that whole gambit. Hire a broke ass translator from any of the elance type sites, have him rewrite every one of those posts in German. Make a .de site, with some German-centric design details. Pay the translator to paraphrase, make it unique enough. Stock photo the fuck out of that thing. Use WordPress and the available host of newsletter, squeezy page tools, SEO crap, whatever membership component to have a quick, low cost system in place. And then copy those two dude’s spread, connect with blogs, PR, guest post, whatever. Use an entirely different, German speaking zomboloid Internet lakey to do the German bidding for you. Seaparate the labor force, so nobody knows nothing but their own task. Copy, copy, copy. The whole sales pitch, squeeze, the materials themselves. Rewritten to where plagiarism is murky, and besides who gives a shit. Translate it into French, copy paste the whole same approach onto a new .fr site. Get a bunch of affiliates. Now you’re going after the two richest demographics on earth, in the most disaster ready places. It’s like taking a chain saw to the fruit tree orchard. You want low hanging? *smell of oil burned exhaust fumes, a thousand chainsaws singing your song of cash piling up*
												- Translate it into Spanish. Japanese. Chinese. Have a small army of content minions mine content, locale specific news, prune your army, find the most creative little minions to write your posts. It’ll never end. However much money you lust for, is however much work you’ll put in. The racket of haves vs. have-nots has been going on since the dawn of time. The world isn’t ending. The rich will continue to fleece the poor, the occasional uprising will happen creating new rich, fleecing the rest of the poor. Rinse, repeat. Sit on the sideline, scare the rich a little, take some of their money to tell them bed time stories about how their money is safe

							- 4 types of concept generation
							  collapsed:: true
								- 1. Original concept. That is when a whole new product space is developed. The Telegraph for communication would be an example. This brings to mind your example of Shark Tank people creating a whole new product/market space where one did not previously exist.
								- 2. Iterative concept. That is when existing technology or a business concept is updated. For instance, landline telephones to cell phones. Dumb cell phones to smartphones.
								- 3. Efficiency concept. That is where existing technology is taken but modified to be much cheaper or more efficient, even if the underlying tech didn't change that much. Uber is the prime example of taking existing infrastructure that was being under-utilized and put those cars/drivers to work.
								- 4. Copycat concept. This is where you launch a coffee shop because you know people like drinking coffee. There's no total creativity because you know people will want comfy chairs, fast wifi and good coffee. You can pattern your store off successful coffee shops. Taking this concept even further, if you don't want any creativity, you open a franchise, as you discard all notions of creativity instead of having guaranteed corporate mandates on what you should and shouldn't do. You've outsourced your creativity.
							- B2B: What are they already buying?

								- Hotels - Web design, SEO, POS systems, check-in systems, employees, catering

							- Build an audience instead of a product if you can't think of the latter
							  collapsed:: true
								- If you can't think of a product, then do the next best thing...build an audience.
								- Start a blog about golfing. Grow readership by providing quality content on a consistent basis (something new every one to three days).
								- Build that audience and even if you don't have product ideas, you can sell other people's product ideas because you now have something even better....a marketing channel.
								- That's actually the hardest part of business. Not the creating (which is hard), but rather the marketing. No point in having a million dollar idea if no one knows about it.
								- If you have 3000 visitors a day, people will be throwing money at you to review their newest golf club innovation.
							- look for something you do in your day job that your company could sell, but it would cost them too much to develop
