							  collapsed:: true
								- Rather than focus on the gloom and doom, maybe try to think how we can turn this to our advantage, and make some money.
								- How about this:
									- 1. Invest in the stock of companies that produce birth control pills? (Pharmaceutical companies). Women are going to have more options than ever before, and will want to have more sex with multiple partners.
									- 2. Invest in companies that make things that guys like, such as good beer. You know, I was reading the other day about a new popular trend in Korea: craft breweries. They are taking off like wildfire. Studies show that as nations grow in wealth, they consume more beer.
									- http://craftbeerasia.com/korea/
								- 3. Invest in companies that cater to male activities, pursuits, and things like that. Security firms, clothing, military and armaments, etc.
								- When you get down and discouraged by long-term trends, think of ways to turn them to your advantage. Guys like to make money, and you might as well be the one making it.
							- If I was looking for a small company to invest in, personally I would prowl Reddit or crowdfunding websites for interesting start ups
							- Copy WSO apps and make them slightly better
							  collapsed:: true
								- Here’s an idea for you. Instead of (or better yet, in addition to) blogging, have you thought about making small software apps (Adobe Air apps or plugins) and then selling those apps via a WSO on the Warrior Forum?
								- People that I have interviewed typically spend about $500 to build an app and it takes about 2-3 weeks to build it.
								- To get ideas, they study what is already selling on the forum…which is pretty easy to do if you get a WSO pro subscription for about $4/month. Once you have a handle on what is selling, you can then figure out the type of product that you should make.
								- For example, anything that helps people with SEO (get traffic, build links) sells like HOT CAKES, so all you would need to do is to have a look at the top selling apps from the last few weeks, and you are going to get a ton of ideas. Then, figure out one or two additional features to add and hire a developer to make it for you.
								- To find a developer, you could use oDesk, elance, etc… OR (and I like this better), you could go onto meetup.com and join the local wordpress developer meetup. When you do, you will meet all sorts of local coders that will be terrific and making stuff, but they won’t have the first clue about how to sell it. Partner up with one of them, and you’ve now eliminated your up front development cost. Instead, get them to code, and you handle marketing, you then split the profits 50/50.
								- To get affiliate support (which you will need to ensure sales are decent), I would suggest you give away 75% yo 100% of the front end. If you do that, and your sales page is decent, affiliates will find and promote you. You should have some kind of “back end” offer that will allow you, via the upsell, to generate some actual profit that you can keep a larger percentage of.
								- What most people don’t understand is that WSOs are for building your list of buyers, so you don’t actually need to make any profits up front (hence why I say to give it away to affiliates to get them to mail for you).
								- Once you have a buyer on your list, you can EASILY get your money back by either promoting your own products (that you make in the future), or by promoting other people’s products (where you become their affiliate).
								- This is by far the lowest risk, fastest payback strategy that I’ve ever come across, and I have interviewed many people who’ve tried many different approaches. I have also tried many myself and they all take longer than the “software as a WSO” approach.

										- localethereum leverages the new Web Crypto API, a secure interface to cryptographic operations now available in the major web browsers. The native API — developed in part by Google, Mozilla and Netflix — is at the center of localethereum’s secure messaging protocol.
										- Every account has a public and private key pair for reputation and security purposes — the public key is public and the private key never touches the internet. Signing in to your account involves decrypting an encrypted private key using a stretched password as the key — somewhat akin to Blockchain.info’s web wallet for Bitcoin.

									- DNS alternative
									  collapsed:: true
									  https://www.rooshvforum.com/thread-64533-post-1640347.html#pid1640347
										- [quote='Valentine' pid='1640347' dateline='1504194087']
										- Great thread idea.
										- [quote='John Michael Kane' pid='1639518' dateline='1504032915']
										- 2. DNS access
										- Domain services, especially if pressured by left-wing groups are already under attack. Don't like someone? Call their domain registrar and tell them they are hosting a Nazi. Now their domain is locked and can't get transferred. We need legal action to be taken against domain registrars who block transfer or freeze accounts of domains that are NOT BEING USED FOR ILLEGAL PURPOSES.
										- Free-speech advocates must start suing registrars who freeze accounts and hold domains hostage for political reasons. Counter.fund might be an excellent place to start such a campaign once launched. Registrars need to defend any domain freeze as based in breaking the law, not offending SJWs.
										- While I think it certainly doesn't hurt to consider "alternative web" solutions, there's still plenty of internet freedom out there in the United States that needs defending. Easy of access of the current system is what brings a message to a mass audience.
										- We need to keep that access open and fight for every inch of it, and only use "alternative web" as a fallback in case we lose the first fight. "Alternative web" should be a secondary consideration, because it also makes it easier to brand it as "Dark Web 2.0" where only the mean, scary people hang out. We need the Web As We Know It to stay intact.
										- [/quote]
										- The problem is that the 'Web As We Know It' (clearnet/world wide web) is inherently centralised and thus compromised.
										- Instead of legal action against domain registrars we need technological solutions which can't be censored. ICANN control all Top-Level Domains (TLDs) on the clearnet and the registrars who are licensed by them are another centralised body of control.
										- Daily Stormer for example now have little chance of being on the clearnet as they've been kicked off domain registrars like GoDaddy, Google, Namecheap and more.
										- [b]Several alt-tech solutions exist:[/b]
										- Namecoin (.bit TLD)
										- Emercoin (.emc, .coin, .lib and .bazar TLDs)
										- Blockstack (.id TLD)
										- Ethereum Name System (.eth TLD)
										- These domains however are still on the "dark web" as they are not normally accessible from a web browser. There are some solutions to this (from easiest to hardest):
										- 1) Promote browser extensions like [url=https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/peername/kkdihlopcnkjinfjhbeopjfmnfpcoaop?hl=en]PeerName[/url] which are able to resolve these TLDs.
										- 2) Promote decentralised apps (dApps) such as those run on Ethereum. If everyone started using MetaMask in their web browser or Mist browser on PC or Status on mobile then that's another way to access the decentralised web.
										- 3) Push for changes in Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Brave browser etc to integrate Peername or similar tech into their core stack so that web browsers can resolve these TLDs without needing to add browser extensions.
										- 4) Push for changes in popular PC/mobile/router operating systems to make it easier to change the DNS IPs to more free public DNS services like OpenNIC which can resolve decentralised TLDs like Namecoin's.
										- None of these are easy solutions but when the clearnet is fundamentally simple to monitor and censor then we have to get creative.
										- [/quote]

									- Pros and Cons
										- Pros
											- Passion
												- Stronger Brand
													- Voice, my image, stories
													- Own loyal fans rather than constantly find new ones (maximise lifetime value)
														- Though with any business you need to market wide to acquire new customers
												- Utilises SMP so I'll be motivated to work on this long-term, as well as be passionately driven and creative in short-term
											- Being a self-experimenter is a core and unique trait of mine
											  #SelfExperiments https://workflowy.com/#/1621a644a3de
											- In 5 years time
												- I'd have 500k+ monthly pageviews (like D&P/ROK/Rational Male)
												- Be making between $1-3k/mo online
											- I'm suited for this
												- Looks and knowledgebase make me ideal for content marketing this
												- Uses ideal audience + solution, rather than just SMP
												- My intellectual skills make me suited for content marketing, probably video more due to my face/physique/body language
												- Even if I had passive income, I'd want to create a blog to share my lifehacking knowledge - might as well forget going the roundabout way and just do it
											- All big players turn to this content marketing funnel
												- Drop Ship Lifestyle, Internet Business Mastery, big internet marketers, MJ DeMarco, Seth Godin, Tim Ferriss, etc
													- Info is available to everyone, they have however curated it and built a personal brand
											- Cheap money-wise for learning process at start (first traffic buys to test demand add up)
											- Though expensive time-wise to create a blog
											- Typical  businesses create stuff like software like plugins. Or software platforms such as shopify for recurring profit. Or consumables
												- Problem with all of these is that the skill of niche discovery is developed and skills for that particular niche - not necessarily widely applicable
											- Auto-Screening
												- Screens out incompatible time-wasters
												- This fame also makes it easier for picking up + maintaining relationships
												- Draws in cool, like-minded individuals who will share with me info I like, activities to do, etc
												- eFame may attract groupies or a wifey too
											- Makes Me Grow
												- Teaching + persuading others consolidates my own beliefs e.g. discussing ideas with friends
												- Learn and think better - now whenever I learn something my own notes look like a blog post
													- Advantages/disadvantages at top, then a sequential explanation of approach
													- Rational and scientific reasoning which strengthens beliefs and self-confidence whilst simultaneously challenging and removing weak beliefs
												- improves my self-control and persistence through both fan accountability + self-concept/authority building me as a self-control king
												- Helps me achieve other goals and learning
											- Express My Core Self
												- Uses my Unique Gift which enables me to make a contribution to the world
												- Using my Signature Strength (teaching others) gives me flow (happiness) and meaning
												- I owe my ideal audience my unique insights
										- Cons
											- Time: High mandatory requirement for short-term (min 2 years)
											  #Fastlane-NECST https://workflowy.com/#/2be7e447e524
												- Yes for first few years minimum
												  #DelegatingContentCreation https://workflowy.com/#/8d2ce4189242
												- Lots of other business don't have the limiting mandatory content creation requirement, thus are better suited to being attempted before Habit Laboratory
												- Not just content marketing + product creation
													- Also reading other peoples’ posts, responding to emails/comments, dealing with technical issues etc
											- Creating a Minimum Viable Product is far quicker than creating a Minimum Viable Audience
												- I have to spend months/years to find out if a blogging niche will produce enough income for me
												- With product-businesses you just send $500-$2k of traffic + initial time discovering/creating product
												- Selling to an existing group is easier than bringing customers to you
												- Makes more sense to Build a Product for a Hungry Audience, rather than try to attract an audience
												- ie you need to invest more time compared to e-commerce to test whether your business model is viable
											- Profitability is relatively low on average for time investment
												- margin = low price point for products
												- Low average sale profit - ebooks, affiliate sales etc
												- Average men tend to buy less than women
												- Digital information products are generally priced less than anything else
											- Easy to get too invested - sunk costs
												- Ego investment - makes it more difficult to quit if not at desired success
												- However investing so much time into 1 business means it's more difficult to switch over to others?
											- Trade-off spectrum: between audience of high-paying customers or highly-intelligent discussions
												- Some attract an audience for networking/self-reflection (like Mike Cernovich, cedonulli)
												- Most attract somewhat inferior audience to milk cash from (like Victor Pride, Christian McQueen, etc)
													- Unclear - Good Looking Loser, Ramit Sethi, How to Beast, Return of Kings, Tropical MBA
												- Elevate minds/create value vs getting purchases?
												- Salient opt-ins, calls to action, squeeze pages, popups, and high readability content draws in a more average/stupid audience? for higher profits
												- Most very intelligent people don't read much blogs?
											- Entry: Low barrier to Entry (anyone can enter the blogging game which makes it exponentially more difficult)
											  #Fastlane-NECST https://workflowy.com/#/2be7e447e524
											- Or is this better suited to 'hobby once I've made my first million'
											- Have to hide much of true knowledge, especially about controversial topics
											  #HabitLab-ContentStrategy https://workflowy.com/#/ff0c23a70259
											- ...
											- it's much harder to sell an "authority content" type business compared to e-commerce. It's more similar to a job with multiple income streams than a typical business asset
											- I personally would rather avoid fame and that's much harder in a content business
										- Unclear
											- Content creation requirement decreases dramatically after several years
											  #DelegatingContentCreation For me to be 100% comfortable with it, I want to be able to delegate content creation
												- **FOR delegating content creation / not necessary (No Time burden)**
													- Victor Pride only produces articles monthly (if that) and still (apparently) makes good money
													- Get ghost and guest writers to not burn out
														- Ramit Sethi looks like he's hiring ghost writers, people who can write in the brand 'voice'

													- Hire staff to handle other areas of business
														- Ramit Sethi, Dave Asprey and Derek Halpern have been hiring conversion optimisers, product managers, outreach, R&D and project managers

												- **AGAINST delegating content creation (Yes Time burden)**

														- And then you may require (expensive) skilled content marketers/copywriters i.e. linchpins
													- Better to focus on building a successful Fastlane business first
														- Many content creators only become so once they've got a successful Fastlane business
															- e.g. MJ DeMarco and Jake Cedonulli have time freedom
														- Once You Start You Can't Stop - If it's your only income stream then you have to keep content marketing and can't pursue other Fastlane businesses
															- People  who aren't reliant on it (like cedonulli) instead can take their time because they've created PASSIVE INCOME i.e. a Fastlane business with no time requirement)

																					- i learned this over a few decades, too, rather than when i was 19.  it’s normal.  you don’t have the balls to do stuff till you can afford to.  you don’t want to get into a fight till you know that you can win a fight.  you don’t tell a customer to fuck off, till you have ten more waiting.  all that slowing down your learning, in exchange for not paying up front for being you.
																					- and i know, i say this a lot.  it’s important.  i wish i’d known earlier.
																					- you’re way better off just being you.  business or personal.  yes up front it’ll cost you.  but you learn faster and more, and you get the chance to become a bigger, real-er you if you’re willing to put more of that out there.  maybe not all, because that’s not necessary, and because you don’t really know what’s real till you put it in the frame of the real world anyway.
																					- i’m 40.  i’m still learning this, today.  everybody starts off lightweight, but most never evolve past that.
																					- i’d probably be having drug fueled orgies right now instead of writing blog posts, if i wasn’t holding back.  maybe i’d have a better relationship with my parents, would have told them by now what somebody probably should.   maybe lots of things.
																					- point is, you don’t actually win in the long run, calculating the impact of some modified, fake persona, half-truth sales pitches, and de-balled levels of entitlement about your personal space.  and sure nobody is just going to put it all out there all at once.  that could be foolish and up front cost bankrupting your life.   just be aware that they may be more to be gained by incrementally at least letting the real you off the leash a little bit.
																					- [source] http://cedonulli.com/the-up-front-cost-of-being-you/

																	- Face internet outrage head on
																	  collapsed:: true
																		- I think there's an additional important point to note here with regards to the importance on confronting those attacking you in contexts like this... I suspect being as savvy as Mike is about internet dynamics he already knows this even if it may be subconscious/a product of his worldview as opposed to something he's independently recognised.
																		- In today's world of clickbait journalism we've seen the rise of "outrage journalists". These aren't people out there looking for something that offends THEM... they're more cynical than that. They're looking for stuff that they know (when presented in the right way) will offend OTHERS. They write an article on it, they give it a suitably sensationalist headline, they remove any context that might make the original thing more reasonable, they stick a link up on twitter and off they go. The twitterverse explodes with more links, other writers stick up other blogs discussing it (and linking to that first article), the mainstream media (who these days tend to decide what to write about by seeing what twitter is saying and then copying and pasting as much as possible) get involved and put up a link to that original article themselves (or at least mention it by name) etc etc. Before you know it that original article suddenly has hundreds of thousands of views... and that first outrage journalist is laughing all the way to the bank with lots more money, interest and name value.
																		- Why does that make it so important to attack?
																		- Because if you don't they have no reason to stop till they've squeezed you dry. There's no downside for them. They'll keep digging, keep writing and keep making sure they present anything they find in a way that will outrage others. An weak apology is even worse... it just gives them an excuse to write more. On and on, day after day, week after week. And even if it dies down eventually all it takes is for you to raise your head above the parapet again and
																		- you'll be under the cosh again.
																		- But if you attack?
																			- If you attack then suddenly there's a cost to them. Suddenly you're not a help, you're a hindrance. You're not helpless, you're fighting back. You're turning the light back on them and even the biggest narcissist rarely likes to see their misdeeds in bright lights. So they'll stop... and stop fairly quickly. Because it's no longer worth it. There are a lot of things one can find online that will get others outraged... they'll just move on to a new, softer target. As Mike mentions here and in the previous podcast, he was under the sort of attack than can bring down the CEO's of major companies. Now? Now most of those who were attacking him are desperate NOT to talk about him because they're aware of the cost. They know they can find someone else who'll say something that will offend people... they'll go for that instead. No risk, no cost. Remember, they don't really care about whatever was actually said... it's not a cause for them. They just care that others care and others will click to read their article. If they don't really care and they're taking this heat then there's no reason for them to continue and they'll move on, their egos bruised and their reputation damaged.
																			- All because you attacked.
																		- Without trying to be too cynical about it, think about the opportunity RSD had here if they'd followed what Mike had said here and in the previous podcast?
																			- They had the eyes of the world on them, the number of eyeballs that companies would pay millions to a PR firm to arrange. They were going to have to take some punches on the chin sure, and some people were going to hate them... but most of those people would hate them anyway if they ever heard about them even without this. But if they'd owned their mistakes, if they'd used it as a chance to not only explain their approach and mindset in a positive way (well, as positive as RSD can be presented...) but also to DEMONSTRATE it in a real world, high pressure, crisis situation? That's a huge opportunity... and they combine it with making sure that the outrage journalists (who you know are currently watching them like a hawk waiting for their next chance to strike) now think twice before going after them again, instead aiming for lower hanging fruit. Hell, they can even turn the outrage journalists tactics against them (something I note Mike has been effective at himself recently) publicising their misdeeds to draw attention to RSD. Looked at cynically this could have been a huge opportunity for them... look at how Mike's own traffic on both twitter and (I assume) here has spiked.
																		- But what have RSD actually done?
																			- The people who are outraged are still outraged... nothing was going to change that. The outrage journalists know they're a soft target. And anyone who may have been interested in their product (giving RSD more credit then they deserve there...) will look at the weak, spineless reaction of these supposed "alpha males" and immediately dismiss them. I can't say I've ever paid attention to RSD but from what I understand a lot of what they do is about presenting oneself as a supposed "alpha", being confident, being in control, being a leader, being the sort of person one would want to be.
																		- Who wants to be them now?
																			- Even if one disagreed with Mike utterly on everything he said... even if you considered him immoral or unethical and representing everything you disliked... if you were under attack I think almost everyone would want to respond like he did; taking control of the narrative, using your skills and experience to dictate the story, not be dictated to. Keeping positive, keeping moving forward, picking the battleground and punching back till the opponent broke. No-one would say the same about RSD.
																		- If you're a boxer do you want to be the boxer who gets hit on the chin and, even though not knocked out, immediately cowers away begging for the ref to stop it? No. Do you want to be the boxer who immediately lashes out wildly at anything in sight in desperation? It's preferable to the first option but still not great. But the boxer who takes his hit, owns it, understands his strengths, keeps positive, picks his spot when the opponent thinks everything's going for him and then counters with accuracy and power? Yes, I think that is the perfect option.
																		- Despite liking Mike's work I'm not a particularly huge fan of the animal-to-human metaphors and similes that are so frequently used. But there's some truth in saying that it's the weaker of a herd that get circled and picked off. The ones who can't or won't fight back. The ones who cower. They don't got for the ones who hit back. The ones who aren't worth the trouble.
																		- Go on offence.
																		- Don't go blindly. Go smartly. Know what your strengths are and keep yourself strong. Pick your
																		- moment.
																		- But go on offence.
																		- And go hard.

																				- Blue Pill > ((65f1fc6a-fa6e-4e90-9390-db92e5736abf))
																				  Because it increases retention of customers + most consumers are women
																					- Why
																						- men are largely producers and women overwhelmingly are consumers. Savvy marketers know who's going to be spending the money, no matter who earned it in the first place.
																						- their fanbases are predominantly female. Women are doing most of the subscribing/following of their social media accounts, buying most of the products they advertise, buying most of the tickets to their shows/movies, and generally providing most of the vocal support that drives their celebrity. Modern celebrity culture is highly feminized by necessity – it is built to cater to the people who fuel it most, and those people are overwhelmingly women.
																						- Women create a mental "relationship" with these celebrities
																							- They imagine everything they say and do is to them personally
																							- They HATE anything that might suggest they can't get this celebrity, ESPECIALLY when it's by the celebrity themselves
																							- he critiques them or sets some sort personal preference or standard, they feel obligated to meet it
																							- They're too insecure to handle any criticism, or to use it as fuel to become better. It just makes them depressed
																						- A large part of the job as a male celebrity is to sell a dream and a fantasy to millions of young women out there. He has to walk on eggshells in order to keep that fantasy intact and keep the money rolling in.
																						- This is why celebrity males so often cannot voice legitimate standards of even the most basic variety. The risk of a majority of their fanbase taking it personally is simply too great because so many girls are insecure about so many things.
																						- Thus, when these guys get the chance or are prompted to talk of standards, they keep it real simple:
																							- “I like strong, independent women.”
																							  This is a great go-to. All of his female fans, regardless of what they look like or where they’re from, can convince themselves that they are strong and independent. He can’t offend anyone.
																							- “Just be yourself.”
																							  Even safer. He is basically saying to every female out there that she’s perfect for him the way she is. The statement implies absolutely no obligation on her part to change anything about herself in order to be able to get an extremely high value male interested in her. That total lack of responsibility brings great mental comfort to these young females.
																							- “I love women who are beautiful on the inside.”
																							  Another great go-to. Any female can convince herself that she fits this bill even if she’s actually a bitch to most of the people she comes into contact with. Low risk of offending anyone.
																							- “Looks aren’t everything”
																							  This is also an effective statement for a high value male looking to remain in the good graces of female fans. It is just another variation of the “beauty on the inside” mantra mentioned above. It works well because it doesn’t offend anyone: the girls who have the looks can easily convince themselves that they’ve also got the beauty on the inside (rendering the fact that “looks aren’t everything” inoffensive to them), while the girls who lack good looks can use this statement to convince themselves that they can completely make up for it with their “beauty on the inside” (which, again, they can all convince themselves they possess). This statement is also effective for placating the attractive girls who have convinced themselves that they’re ugly.
																						- What isn’t okay for high value men to say?
																							- “I like athletic girls”
																							  This will build fans among the girls who’ve played sports or enjoy them, but it will awaken the insecurities of the many who were never particularly inclined to athletics and who hated gym class (or who are out of shape). If a man’s fanbase consists of many of these women (and it almost certainly will if he’s a typical celebrity), he’d best stay away from this one.
																							- “I love freckles”
																							  Any girl who doesn’t have freckles will begin to wonder if you like her.
																							- “I love big tits”
																							  Even girls with sizable breasts are often insecure about their bust size and convinced that they’re too small. Thus, such a comment would have a high risk of offending just about all of his female fanbase.
																							- “Girls need to shave”
																							  Lots of girls don’t do it and many who do have neglected it at times. This kind of statement has the potential to offend every girl in a given high-value male’s fanbase, as Nash Grier has learned.
																							- “I love fit girls”
																							  Obviously a high risk of offending the fat/chubby chicks, but also a high risk of offending the fit chicks who THINK they’re fat. These two groups can often make up the entirety of a celebrity male’s fanbase.
																						- From this article
																						  source:: http://www.returnofkings.com/26986/why-famous-men-become-outspoken-feminists / https://archive.is/46GOC
																					- Examples
																						- Popular YouTuber received massive hate for video on "what men like in girls"
																							- What they said
																								- Caylen criticizes girls with “fake tits,” while Grier dislikes girls who are “obnoxious and loud.” Then the boys talk about physical appearance, favoring girls who are short and petite, with “natural” looks and “really good smiles.” They criticize girls who don’t shave their facial and body hair, implying that “peach fuzz” and other types of natural hair on women is “gross.”
																								- Grier also tells girls that “the chase is such a big part” of what makes a girl appealing, encouraging them to “play hard to get.”
																								- “If you play too hard to get, then it’s just like, ‘oh, she doesn’t even like me,’ but if you play easy, then it’s just like, oh, she’s a whore,” Grier states. “Find a balance.”
																								- “You can’t be better than me” at playing video games, Dallas adds. “I mean I don’t even play video games. You can’t be better than me.”

														- Good topics
															- Evergreen content that will be relevant even years from now
															- Things that haven’t been done before - otherwise link to it instead
															- Things that are hidden or unspoken - only write about the obvious if you have a unique angle or insider information
															- Hot topics
															  #DraftingTechnique https://workflowy.com/#/1ec3e10e250e
																- e.g. Scott Adams writes about Donald Trump's presidential campaign to massively boost book sales
																  source:: http://www.dangerandplay.com/2016/01/27/two-games-scott-adams-says-and-donald-trump/
														- WRITE EPIC SHIT.
															- “What can I give my readers today that would blow their minds? How can I turn their life upside down? What can I say that they couldn’t help but share?”
															- Provide so much value that they can't ignore you. (Useful, Inspiring, Innovative, Unique)
															- Do you plan to educate, entertain or enlighten your readers with your content?
															- triple your personality - actionable, research-proven,
															- If not enough impact - write as if the whole world is watching.
															- "How vain is it to sit down and write when one has not stood up to live."
															- "To be like no-one else you have to live like no-one else."
															- Am I failing enough? i.e. am I taking risks, going for opportunities, learning etc. If you're not risk-seeking then by playing safe you cant be remarkable by being like everyone else
																- - look for risks that dont have a high cost of failure i.e. not trying new stuff as a neurosurgeon or pilot, but fine posting blog posts
															- Be Controversial - behaviour, beliefs and belongings (opinions + research)
																- /Polarising
																- controversy - behaviour, beliefs and belongings (opinions + research)
																- Be polarising - make them love or hate you for better conversions
																- Use contrast to keep attention. Humor is born out of ridiculous contrast. So is shock. Fear. Surprise. Pattern interrupts.
																- people share stuff that confirms their self-image e.g. as smart, having good taste. eg. why x group/skill/place is awesome
																- News
																	- Agree with it and provide more facts
																	- Disagree and put-down a high profile influencer
																	- Make a prediction based on the news event
																	- Interview someone related to the story
																- Controversy
																	- - you need scientific evidence opposing your opinion or to truly believe in it
																	- - don't have to necessarily put your own opinion on it so they dont hate you, simply take others opinion/evidence
																	- - not too crazy otherwise people feel embarassed to share it
																	- - reach out to people who'd like your articles (google your title keyword) e.g. personal dev fanatics, etc
																	- + reach out to people who'd hate it - get them to comment and argue on it with your audience
																	- - email people back and forth to fuel the controversy e.g. how do you feel about that
																- Linkbaiting is the process where you write content purely focused on getting links to your site. You can do this by writing articles that push people’s buttons (but doesn't turn off your readers), running competitions, providing top 10 lists or making a simple and useful tool.
																- This can be a bit hit and miss, but when it hits, it can give you a tremendous boost in traffic.
																- Problogger gives an excellent 101 on linking baiting here including 20 linkbaiting techniques.
																	- http://www.problogger.net/archives/2006/09/19/an-introduction-to-linkbaiting/%20/t%20_blank
																	- http://www.problogger.net/archives/2006/09/21/20-linkbaiting-techniqes/%20/t%20_blank
														- Treat blog posts as MVPs - testing demand
														- Highlight audience
															- e.g. Brought to you by valued commenter deti "quote"
															- Reader Mailbag /reader's questions as headline/intro
														- Keep each article relatively independent from each other
															- Why
																- Pros
																	- Organised into landing pages
																	  #LandingPage https://workflowy.com/#/5f19d15d7caa
																	- Organised into free resources further along funnel eg newsletter, Lead Magnets
																	- Can organise content into paid products eg Tripwires, Core Offering, Money Maximiser
																- Cons

															- How to Give Value
															  Mike was discussing how he only links to guys that links out
															  And I thought of course, that's only natural.
															  
															  We give value back to those who give value to us
															  
															  For example he mentioned in a recent blog post that he was having difficulties getting his newsletters setup.
															  
															  I have a newsletter myself but we use different tools for that. No matter, I just did a quick google search and found a guide that would help him get set up
															  
															  The reason why I put my real name and face out there is because I provide massive value in my job. No matter what kind of 'offensive' or 'trigger warning' stuff I share doesn't matter because
															  Besides, even if my current clients didn't like it I have enough value to get other clients.
															  
															  http://habitlaboratory.com/project-world-domination-update-1/
															  
															  -----
															  Great tips. It really all comes down to having a great answer to the question "Why should I read your site?". If you have valuable insights then you will have a valuable blog.
															  
															  And to do this you need to invest in yourself. Pay for courses to learn how write, market and becoming skilled in your niche. Spend time taking on whatever habits are necessary for you to have a useful viewpoint for others.
															  
															  The only way you won't succeed is if you haven't put in the work whether it be your 10,000 hours to mastery or 1000 days http://www.tropicalmba.com/liv... you need to put in your time in the trenches or your site will simply fade into the ether.
															  
															  "Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value." - Albert Einstein
															  
															  You'll only receive value back once you can say for certain you're giving out a shit ton of value. Go change 1 million lives for the better and and tell me that you're not rich beyond your wildest dreams.

																- Law of Karma
																  Catch-all term for when people harm others also have habits which are likely to result in negative consequences in the future 
																  e.g. they are stimulation seeking or 
																  
																  
																  So what's the takeaway?
																  
																  Consider all your habits which may have unintended negative consequences on others.
																  
																  From the top of my head, I can immediately see how my tendency to focus on productivity with less focus on social ties can harm my relationships. At the extreme, this can result in being rich with no-one to share it with.
																  
																  How to prevent this from happening?
																  
																  Seek balance. Do the 80/20 of every activitity to keep it all on the burner at the same time. Blast and cruise.

															- Mysteries
																- Look closely at your topic and uncover some old-fashioned mysteries. Now write a post that presents the mystery and leads your reader through the investigation to its incredibly satisfying conclusion.
															- Finding out your value
															  On one hand we have more people than ever being proud of (nothing much)
															  Female empowrment, bullshit degrees
															  
															  Disclaimer: this'd isn't an excuse to feel proud over the non existent value your have. 
															  
															  Who your are inside doesn't matter - all that matters to other people is how valuable your are to them. 
															  
															  But rather than bitch and cry at the world over how unfair this all is, suck it up and realise this is what you need to achieve any of your goals in regards to attracting others both sexually (people to date or your current partner) and financially (customers, business partners, employees, etc). 
															  
															  ----
															  
															  On the other hand, more people than ever have become a slave to the system
															  Not realising the true value they have
															  
															  Note: find statistics on % of entrepreneurs over history
															  Or just use anecdotes - there was no stable jobs back then - you had to create value or you and your family starved
															  
															  How can these problems coexist?
															  
															  By people placing value on the wrong things. 
															  
															  So how do you find out what value your have?
															  
															  The easiest way is to
															  Compare yourself to Joe Average. 
															  
															  [Picture]
															  Joe Average had 2.3 kids, x amount of debt, is Y years old etc
															  
															  What do you know that he doesn't? 
															  
															  The world pays you when you solve problems. 
															  
															  If you're having difficulty pinpointing any thing you're probably being too hard on yourself. 
															  
															  Lighten up. It doesn't matter if other people know it - it's whether the average person does. 
															  
															  What can you do better than top 20% of other people?
															  Or wall street playboy's would ask for 1-5%
															  
															  And finally, if you're struggling still
															  
															  Discover what you're uniquely suited for and go learn that!

																- They don't rush. They commit at least 30 minutes a day and realize that starting a
																- business won't happen overnight.
																- 3. They've all failed at starting. You will too and it'll be a great learning experience.
																- 4. They have fun. This should be a good time, don't ever forget that.
																- 5. They persist. You will face tough times and we'll help you prepare for it. They don't
																- refund the first moment they are struggling, they click on the stuck button (top right)
																- to ask for help or use the private Facebook community (you'll be invited shortly).

																- of starting that business.

																- more success ideas
																  e best moment is when I fall. Huh? Yea, it’s when I got to the part I didn’t think I could do. 
																  Over time, that new point becomes my baseline, and I progress further and further.
																  That’s the same thing in business and in kicking ass in general—getting comfortable with 
																  being uncomfortable. Once you get past your comfort zone you’ll be further ahead than ever 
																  before.
																  Most of our fears and failures are self-created. Once you start focusing your thoughts more 
																  positively on succeeding and what you actually want, then those ideas will start becoming 
																  true.
																  Just a friendly reminder, it's good when you fail. You may have gone further than you thought 
																  AND you get to learn to become even better for the next experience.
																- Trite positivity/success stuff
																	- Follow your dreams
																	- Listen to your spirit
																	- Change the world
																	- Make your mark
																	- Find your inner voice and make it sing

															- Cult/indoctrination beliefs
																- Talk about getting rid of friends/family
																	- Cults are designed to minimize the ability of followers to experience such epiphanical moments of clarity. Consider how specific tenets of a cult-like memeplex can wall off avenues of likely exit. For example: friends and family are a common vector of doubt, thus the frequent exhortations to be cautious of their negativity as memetic vaccination. Debbie’s Success Cult warns members to resist the pernicious influence of low-consciousness friends and family who might try to lead them astray.
																	  This helps with indoctrination
																- Positive thinking
																	- general lionization of Positive Thinking as a broad-spectrum antibiotic against any loss of faith. If you doubt Debbie for any reason, you’re merely trapped in a self-limiting belief, which you must learn to overcome, no matter how many Webinars it takes.
																- Make monetary investments in yourself
																	- you must demonstrate your faith in yourself, by making hefty investments in your self-development (i.e, purchasing expensive motivational conferences and ebooks) or else you don’t have a Success Mindset, you don’t truly believe that you’re destined for wealth, and you will remain poor indefinitely.
																- Put yourself into bad situations because good stuff is coming
																	- anchored so effectively by Debbie’s story of debt and redemption, is that It’s Always Darkest Right Before The Dawn, and you really should max out your personal line of credit at 20% APR for all of this.
															- Prioritising - values
															  Few activities for me are inherently 'bad'. If I've been writing all day, then relaxing watching some YouTube videos isn't 'distracting'. It's more about doing the right activities first then if there's time left then I can spend it however I like.
															  
															  
															  I' Part 2 - How to define your activities/life The Best Way to /How to Invest Your Valuable Time/Life As for what to do with your time, this will have to be for another post. Okay, now you've got your device/tool sorted out. So how will you define your activities? The Ancient Greeks believed that a balanced life consisted of one-third social pursuits, one-third intellectual, and onethird physical. Ancient Greeks - 5 hours exercise, 5 hours social, 5 hours leisure/creation/learning, (physical/social/mental) Here's some you should consider tracking 1) Sleep - are you getting enough? Too much? There's a fine balance Beware that time-tracking doesn't measure exactly how much you sleep because it may take you a while to fall asleep, wake up and become conscious etc. Other devices can measure this but this can give a fairly accurate measure of sleep 2) TV Work - everyone wants to be more productive, but they never measure just how productive they are. And include commuting. This will help you evaluate whether your current job is giving you exactly what you want, which will help plan out your next moves. 3) Exercise Advanced Productive Work - your 80/20 analysis of your highest priority tasks. Not in terms of urgency, but importance. What is the long-term effect of these tasks? Will these significantly improve your life, or is it just a temporary burden? From there you can think about streamlining or automating the task to make your life easier so you can focus on the most important things. Reading Socialising Relaxing Procrastination/Entertainment
															  
															  
															  How to Spend Your Time and Maximise Life
															  ow to live - time tracking 
															  According to x - focus on hedonism ie live for the moment 
															  
															  It's a balance between delayed and instant gratification. 
															  As delayed affects your whole life rather than one moment, you should spend the majority of your time delaying gratification at least until you have the foundations of life in place eg healthy lean muscular body, financial independence etc 
															  Then you can afford to take it easy. Of course it will take a very long time to get there, so define your goal very specifically ao you do dedicate more time. 
															  Though old habits die hard and I've found you learn to love growth. 
															  I combine delayed and immediate gratification together (use simpler words) future and present focus 
															  By finding the joy in growth. I also focused on the part I love most - teaching this knowledge, which has enabled me to grow loads. 
															  By acting on my passion to coach others, this is what I find engaging plus it helps grow my life long term. 
															  
															  Use your signature strengths, find flow and challenge

															  100% agree. You HAVE to remove the negative before adding in the positive.
															  
															  "Perfection is achieved, not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away." - Antoine de Saint-Exupery
															  
															  Due to the principle of Loss Aversion, negatives hurt us much more than positive. We adapt to positive circumstances easily due to Hedonic Adaptation.

															- - Why Punishment Doesn't Work
																- - explain simply what it is (adding negatives eg physical punishment, fines, detention, arguments etc). It's better to look for the desired behaviour and reward that. It's far easier to punish because all you have to it let your anger take control and unleash it on someone. Telling someone off even if you explicitly tell them what it is they did wrong doesn't work. Instead be smart and wait for them to do the nearest thing to what you like, then you can shape (link) it closer and closer to what you want
																- - I punish Khalil rather than use reinforcers because it's easier, but way less effective eg he learns to face forward by me shining laser pen at walls, but doesn't learn to keep eyes focused on teacher. My best reinforcers are the vibrating device though it keeps slipping out of his pocket and he inspects it sometimes.

															- Approach or Avoidance
															  http://happierhuman.com/approach-or-avoidance/
															  mastery vs performance etc
															  
															  IMO, all of these "what if" mental changes relate to something called Regulatory Focus Theory in psychology. According to RFT, people choose to approach goals in one of two ways;
															  
															  1) prevention-focused - being concerned about safety, vigilance, and "not losing".
															  2) promotion-focused - being motivated by hopes, achievement, and "gaining".
															  
															  Essentially then, the "what if" questions Mike describes above could be seen as a mental exercise in trying on a general promotion-focused mindset. This is particularly helpful for "gifted" men, as higher IQ (and life difficulties) can lead to a vigilant and overly prevention-focused mindset. To truly experience social freedom, creativity, and life satisfaction, however, the ability to shift to a promotion-focused mindset is key.
															  
															  Personally, I have a tendency to get stuck in "analysis paralysis", which is often a function of a prevention-focused mindset (along with frustration, perfectionism, OCD behaviors, etc.). When I feel "stuck", I realize I am over-thinking and dwelling in an effort to avoid pain, fear, and failure. So, I start asking myself promotion-focused questions, like Mike's above. Mine are usually specific to the situation/goal though...
															  
															  What do I hope to accomplish with this situation?
															  What am I looking to gain or get out of it?
															  Where are the good and positive aspects of this situation that I can build from?
															  How can I resolve or complete things in a way that increase my pleasure and satisfaction?
															  If others are involved, how can I create a win-win?
															  
															  Questions like this help me to reduce the negativity or anxiety that I'm feeling and focus on creative, winning, solutions instead. It takes practice (I'm still working to make it a habit). When I manage it though, my life is a lot more productive and pleasurable.

																  time-how-to-manage-your-most-valuable-currency
																  
																  The first step in changing any habit is awareness, after all you need to know how you're spending your time before you can start optimising it. As the great management consultant said,
																  And finally simply tracking your time improves how you spend it due to the observer effect. Or as the famous business management motto says:
																  "What gets measured, gets managed." - Peter Drucker

															- Using Instant Gratification for Habits
															  **Instant Gratification**
															  Whenever you reinforce a positive habit, complete a daily task, or take care of an old to-do, HabitRPG immediately rewards you with experience points and gold. As you gain experience, you can level up, increasing your stats and unlocking more features, like classes and pets. Gold can be spent on in-game items that change your experience or personalized rewards you've created for motivation. When even the smallest successes provide you with an immediate reward, you're less likely to procrastinate.
															  **Consequences**
															  Whenever you indulge in a bad habit or fail to complete one of your daily tasks, you lose health. If your health drops too low, you die and lose some of the progress you've made. By providing immediate consequences, HabitRPG can help break bad habits and procrastination cycles before they cause real-world problems.
															  **Accountability**
															  With an active community, HabitRPG provides the accountability you need to stay on task. With the party system, you can bring in a group of your closest friends to cheer you on. The guild system allows you to find people with similar interests or obstacles, so you can share your goals and swap tips on how to tackle your problems. On HabitRPG, the community means that you have both the support and the accountability you need to succeed.

															  Foundational Habits - - this means that improving this habit also aids other habits. After all, if you can't manage your everyday life then how can you expect to go to the gym, work, read, socialise AND get in a good night's sleep?

															- Monthly Review (how to create your own)
															- My Productivity System
																- eg to-do list/daily habits, time planner, Next Action list, life goals overview (from tactical daily Next Actions to strategical lifetime goals overview). Plus Time reminders eg LATER, calendar, gTasks. And time tracking app.
																- Back in June 2013 I had no such system, just mindomo which was like a whiteboard full of things I wanted to get done at a strategical overview but no tactical stuff like HabitRPG, routine checklists, recurring tasks etc. I had a next action list, don't break the chain, monthly review, calendar schedule where I tried to slot in tasks but no real accountability and logging all my time was very manual. Now I've got strategical SOP checklists for writing blog posts, meeting influencers, Day Plan (all routines) and making way more progress now. Every day goes mostly in the way I want it to in terms of where my time is spent if I look at it in a monthly Review.
																- Overview
																	- OLD OVERVIEW - early 2014 at latest (as I stopped using Mindomo around that time)
																		- For the big picture overview I use a combination of Asana, Evernote and Mindomo.
																		- Also the amount of detail you can fit in a checklist is limited. There isn't enough room if you want to add a lot of notes or many options, so for detailed checklists I use Asana.
																		- Motivation doesn't last, you need habits and systems.
																		- I've used all kinds of motivational reminders - motivational posters, daily changing desktop wallpapers, popup reminders etc. None of them keep working.
																		- Instead you need:
																		- 1) A system which keeps you killing it daily
																		- Whatever tools you use, you need systems so that whether you're 'motivated' on a day or not it doesn't matter because you still get shit done.
																		- I love using HabitRPG because unlike other to-do apps it also has a motivational component to it where you can get a little avatar of yourself to level up, do quests, gain items etc.
																		- 2)
																		- Besides, do you think your best self would rely on 'motivation'? Hell no, you show up and do the work. See
																		- Motivation doesn't last and I've tried posters, wallpapers, reminders, affirmations etc
																		- You need a system that keeps you doing stuff even when you're not motivated eg habit RPG, routines, checklists and mini goals. Eventually habits make this even easier
																		- Second you need to chose things you enjoy eg fine your single motivating purpose, outsource, say no, chose work you enjoy etc

																	- TagSpaces > evernote

																- RVF Draft
																	- https://www.rooshvforum.com/thread-30113.html
																	- Been using Evernote for 6 years now.
																	- Evernote is no longer the mainstay of my productivity system and I only use it for quick jotting down notes or priority items (down to 60 notes from 3000+).
																	- Now I use TagSpaces for my knowledgebase of multimedia notes e.g. saved webpages, videos, audiobooks etc (better than Evernote for this as I don't have to upload my private data to the cloud and you can view more filetypes from within it).
																	- For my text-only notes I use WorkFlowy - lightning-fast, super easy interlinking between notes and easy export for backups or if you ever want to stop using it (simply copy and paste - it's based on hierarchical bullet points which you can zoom into).
																	- Lots of programs available now these days for GTD, but if I had to choose one it would be WorkFlowy. It's sheer speed in taking notes and being able to handle vast quantities of data make it 5* in my books.
																	- **However**
																		- Don't recommend this - instead sell them my own program?
																		- E.g. WorkFlowy copy which allows attachments in Asana-style (puts it onto Amazon AWS)
																-
															- Overcoming Obstacles system
															- TagSpaces: Evernote alternative
																- http://habitlaboratory.com/wp-admin/post.php?post=993&action=edit
																- TagSpaces - Recently I replaced Evernote with this app, and the advantages I'll explain in separate post. Basically this tool is a fast, simple dashboard from which you can manage and edit all your data - whether it be notes, pictures, videos, saved webpages, PDFs, EPUBs, etc.
															- Daily Routines
															  All my systems which keep me oriented everyday to what would be the most effective actions to take to be happy and improve my future simultaneously
															  prioritise my life well and moves me closer towards all my goals without any purposeful goal-setting (though I'm not taking advantage of Parkinson's law)
															  
															  The daily routines I've created which keep me busy and productive towards progress in my goals. It also is a defence against depression or break ups because it keeps me busy and doing positive things.
															- What's Your Single Motivating Purpose
															  I learned about this brilliant concept in the IBM Academy and really changed the motivation in a fundamental way.
															  
															  Link to podcast then explain 6 steps
															  
															  Inside the Academy they explain a few different you can do to help find your SMP, but you can just use some introspection.
															  
															  What do you find yourself naturally doing? What are you drawn to doing, it's a part of you?
															  
															  When I especially focused on the things which I can't stop talking about and do even most people would find it too long to do, I knew I had found my SMP.
															  
															  For me when I use it I take huge satisfaction in the opportunity to teach others about habits.
															- Hardest Task First
															  http://habitrpg.wikia.com/wiki/Do_Your_Hardest_Task_First
															- Life focus
															  It also gives you an overview of your current life focus - are you putting in adequate time into the most important areas of your life to you, be it work, family or your health? If you only spend 1% of your time on family-related activities, it's much more difficult to build a strong relationship with them.
															  
															  This lights a fire underneath your ass to make sure that you're spend more time on the things you truly desire in your life - whether it be more time to spend with loved ones, on your hobbies or just to sit and meditate. On the flip side it sparks your creativity to streamline or stop doing activities you dislike, which will make it far easier to roll out of bed in the morning.

																- Why and How to Start using Virtual Assistants
																  You can't afford to Not have a (virtual )assistant
																  
																  Benefits
																  
																  Long term it's best to have full time employees
																  
																  Short term get started with freelance marketplaces
																  I started with Elance
																  Hire for a specific research task and explain if they are suitable they'll be kept on as a long-term employee
																  Eg 5 hours/week part time
																  
																  You can use them for one-off or recurring tasks:
																  web development
																  Web Design
																  Social media outreach
																  Content marketing
																  Copywriting etc
																  
																  You'd try your hand at all of these but eventually want these delegated to employees. Whether this happens in the first year or not depends on speed of growth and determination
																  
																  You often get better results by doing the job role yourself before delegating
																  However there will come a time when you have to hire people to do things you don't even know how to 
																  
																  Work the system by Sam Carpenter is a great book for the mindset behind this
																  
																  "Work ON the business not IN the business" - ASAP at least, not suitable for MVPs always 
																  
																  
																  **Learn how to be a manager**
																  I'd say the hierarchy for learning this skill is like this: Level 1) outsource anything to learn how to manage employees. 2) OS time-consuming tasks so you have more time for higher ROI activities. 3) OS tasks which you get paid more than what they cost eg producing content, apps, products etc

																- Learn valuable skills
																	- ^ Such an important point. Learn valuable skills.
																	- You might not know right now what business idea to pursue, it doesn't matter, learn a foundational entrepreneurial skill. Wall Street Playboys has some good posts on the kinds of skills that are valuable.
																	- Case in point, I spent the last 5 years teaching myself a valuable skill. I've had lots of little successes along the way, then an opportunity has come along in the last month which is likely to lead to me earning 5 figures within the next 12 months
																	- This is an opportunity I could never have planned for, but I had thoroughly prepared for.
																	- http://wallstreetplayboys.com/follow-your-skills-not-your-dreams/

																	-
														- Long Tail Keywords
															- So far SEO value (money potential) of top 15 habit keywords is very low (~$5k) and competition is very strong. Difficult niche with low rewards, but potentially great rewards offline in terms of speaking/job opportunities. Plus in app market there's HUGE demand
															- Long-Tail KW Research
																- - Only need: PBR = 15, SEOT = 5 (normally 50+), SEOC = 30,000 (low lvl)
																- > Target low SEOC, exact match/high PBR first kws first
																- Match type data:
																- At first I'll only get 'Exact match' traffic, then 'Phrase match', then 'Broad match' as G grants authority
																- i.e. Exact match = short-term traffic potential
																- Low PBR = word missing? Generate KWs from it to determine the best phrase
																- - derivative keywords (use theme keyword phrase)
																- - semantic long-tail keywords (related by meaning)
																- -** Get web content studio for finding extra keywords and KeywordTool Dominator for hundreds of kw's**
															- *lots of competitors keywords amassed on Dropbox
																- 1.) I sort the keywords I already have by number of searches per month. I then take the 50 keywords with the most searches per month and add them into the 'get keywords' tool. Brings up hundreds of extra keywords.
																- Go through yahoo answers, wikipedia, alexa, own analytics, wiki answers blogs, websites, tv and then put the keywords through both Google Adwords Keyword Tool and Get Keywords Function in the tool.
																- Questions tend to be good candidates. What is the best way to..., How often should you..., etc.
																- i then sort by searches and start with the low traffic one['s first.
																- 2.) In Google I type the following search (without quotes) "keyword site:answers.com" and feed the resulting questions through the keyword tool.
																- 3.) I export every keyword I have in the tool into excel, and add 'facts' to the end or 'facts about' to the beginning of each keyword (you can automate this). Add every single one of the newly combined keywords back into the keyword tool. It rejects 90%+ of them as being too few searches, but the 10% are gold keywords, and easy to rank for. Same principle for keywords like "2011".     "
																- And converting keywords Evernote
																- click 'get keywords' on any kw in KEYWORDSTRATEGY
															- AFTER KEYWORD LIST AMASSED
																- First go for vol 1000+ and  KEI >1000, comp <100, then <1k <10k <50k
																- then comp <100 <1k only
																- then vol 140+
																- Then multi-word KWs e.g. {8,} comp <10k, then 50k
																- Sort passion keyword tree.mm into branches and leaves (long tails)
																- High KEI > Long tail > 10k comp or less
																- 2% vol x 25% CPC
																- It rejects 90%+ of them as being too few searches, but the 10% are gold keywords, and easy to rank for.
																- - Only need: PBR = 15, SEOT = 5 (normally 50+), SEOC = 30,000 (low lvl)
																- > Target low SEOC, exact match/high PBR first kws first
																- SEOT is a skewed figure as it assumes 42% go for no.1 ranked, instead use 25% of total searches as a figure for potency
															- Notes
																- do not just copy-paste your competitors’ keywords, use it as additional help for your keyword research;
																- compare which keywords your competitors optimized their pages (i.e. tried to rank for) and which keywords they ended up ranking for. Thus you’ll be able to avoid their mistakes.

												- Guest Posts
													- The idea is simply that you want to create a bit of a buzz towards your eBook without really directly mentioning it. For example, my eBook is all about how to increase your subscriber rate so what I did was write a lot of guest posts about why email subscribers can make you a lot of money. People get really interested in the idea and then visit your site and see that you have an eBook about it. I call it the guest post funnel and it works well.

																- losing weight and building muscle
																	- Changing your diet is one the most difficult habits to change as it is rooted in our DNA
																	- In evolution if you didn't eat, you died so a powerful motivation to eat is rooted in our DNA and it especially goes for the high-calorie items e.g. chocolates,
																	- The effectiveness of informational messages varies massively from person-to-person but it still worth saying here: every little counts. If you make a mistake or slip up on your diet that's fine. Just don't then say "this diet is ruined!" and proceed to devour an entire box of Oreos.
																	- Every little extra adds extra fat you'll have to work harder to burn off and more importantly weakens your long-term habit of healthy eating.

														- **Ideas**
															- eCourses
																- 365 days newsletter/back-end course
																	- I'm thinking of creating a niche market product/service. Essentially like Copyhour, where you get a lesson per day which will take you from zero to hero over the course of a year.
																	- In terms of a funnel, I was thinking;
																		- 7 day trial free.
																		- 30 day trial for like $60 or $2 per lesson.
																		- 365 lessons for $365.
																		- (Price subject to change.)
																	- And then once the course is done and dusted, I can both buy traffic, get SEO love by doing a blog where I allude to what's in the course and write about why other programs in the market are missing the one key ingredient subtly. Maybe write some cheap books to stick on amazon for $2.99 with a few random exercises from the course.
																	- The niche is high interest (basically everyone wishes they could do it.) Competition in the form of youtube videos and books and whatnot, but like Copyhour, my USP is that you don't have to use your brain, just follow the instructions in the email every day and you'll be awesome.
																	- Probably going to use aWeber and set the whole thing as a drip-feed auto-respond. Then have a private member forum for the people on the course to post their updates and be accountable and stuff.

																			- 90% accurate
																			  British Journal of Sports Medicine, July 2010
																			- Ki Fit 90% accurate
																				- The Ki Fit System has been clinically proven to measure calorie burn and levels of physical activity accurately. It is a medical device and as such, features some of the most cutting edge technology on the market.
																				- A recent independent study tested the armband in real-life situations. Participants engaged in a broad spectrum of pursuits from brisk walking, running, cycling, ball games and strength training through to sedentary, home and work-based activities.
																				- The study compared the armband to a '£25,000 portable oxygen analyser ' recognised as the 'gold standard' for assessing calories. The results showed a mean error of less than 10% in measuring total calories for free living activities, and less than 5% in total minutes of exercise.
																			- Source: British Journal of Sports Medicine, July 2008.

																	- [The AirGradient DIY Air Quality Sensor (Pro Version PCB Version 3.7)](https://www.airgradient.com/documentation/diy-pro-v37/) open-source
																	- I would recommend checking out the AQ-SPEC 
																	  program [1]. They do quite comprehensive tests on low-cost air quality 
																	  sensors.  My side project with a few friends [2] chose to use the 
																	  Sensirion SPS30 [3] for our portable PM2.5 monitor.  As mentioned by 
																	  others, it's a bit more expensive than the PMS5003 used by AirGradient, 
																	  but we preferred its documentation, company reputation, and form factor 
																	  at the time of choosing (2019).  The PMS5003 is perfectly capable, and 
																	  has demonstrated good success with many projects/companies using the 
																	  sensor (e.g. PurpleAir). However, a quick search suggests there are some
																	  issues with a recent batch of these sensors [4].

											- All Potential Job Tasks
												- My Job Role
													- DESIGN MY VISION BEFORE CREATING IT
													- Stockpile blog content, podcasts, videos, emails, products for years ahead. Do loads of press + syndication + guest posts
													- Business Creation SOP
														- avoid markets with a perfect measurable score or limited ceiling eg bowling best you can do is 300 therefore no way to become a linchpin.
													- Promotional Strategy (internet marketing)
													- $10k/hour tasks
														- Improving my USP > Planning and prioritising my day > solving a problem for a prospective or existing customer
														- Creating new and better offers > Negotiating with a qualified prospect > talking to a qualified prospect
														- Repositioning > Building sales funnel > writing an email
														- Discovering brilliant ideas > Judging marketing tests and experiments > creating marketing tests/experiments
														- Negotiating major deals > creating PPC campaigns
														- Selling to high value customers and groups > doing social media with extreme competence
														- Selecting team members > delegating complex tasks > outsourcing simple tasks
														- Public speaking > writing sales copy > customer followup
														- Establishing values and culture > going to meetings
													- Doing Non-CVO Tasks
														- Tasks that should be delegated
														- Note: not applicable to MVPs
														- 1) What work was I doing?
														- 2) What job role is the work I'm doing meant for?
														- 3) How will I prevent doing it or similar work again? eg Systemise with SOP, automate, eliminate etc
														- 4) What is the cause of this current firefighting/urgent issue (it's due to a particular inefficient subsystem)?
