														- Hiring for Freelance Programming Projects
															- 1. Identify some activity in your day to day work that seems repetitive
																- For example, you know how you go through those spreadsheets line by line checking to see if a keyword is green or not? A good programmer could probably whip up a program to do that. Or something that merges CSV files together. If you're doing repetitive work, you could probably automate it and save yourself time.
															- 2. Describe the activity in detail in a way that a programmer could give you exactly what you want
																- Imagine you're creating a new recipe in the kitchen. Open up the spreadsheet containing the keywords. For each keyword in the spreadsheet, check the pagerank of the top 4 search results. If any of the top 4 have a pagerank of higher than 4, mark them as no; otherwise, mark them as yes. Output a file containing all the keywords and their yes/no values.
															- 3. Find an outsourcing agency and post that you're looking for a programmer
																- Provide a high level overview of the program, and go into as much detail as you need to help programmers give you accurate quotes. This is the most important point. The more accurate you are with your description of your need, the better programmers will be able to provide you a detailed and accurate quote. If you're not precise, good programmers will steer clear of you, and bad programmers will promise you the moon. As I've mentioned in the past, I use oDesk, but you can use Elance, Scriptlance, or even find a programmer though Craigslist.
															- 4. Choose a programmer and give them the smallest possible thing you can imagine
																- For example, I would give the programmer a single keyword, and ask them to create a script that checks whether that keyword is green or red. That's it, don't worry, they can make it more complicated later. Although you want the final product (the script), you're really assessing personality here. How well do they explain technical issues to you. How well do they keep you appraised of what they're working on. Do you feel like they're talking down, or dismissing you? A good programmer is respectful of the fact that they have magical knowledge and don't abuse that power. The programmer will also have to go through the process of giving you a way to interact with the program. Maybe it's a website that you access, or a script you run on your local computer. Once you've got this going for the tiny example, it'll still be the same when they add additional functionality to the script.
															- 5. Test the program extensively
																- Use it normally to make sure you're happy, and then try to break it. Give it non-standard characters, disconnect your Internet part way through its process, and see how it responds. Once again, you're trying to figure out how careful the programmer is. A bad programmer will write very brittle code that breaks if you go off the tracks, and provide you baffling errors. A good programmer's code will fail gracefully. Again, you're judging character here.
															- 6. Extend your application one feature at a time
																- So now it works checking a single keyword, ask the programmer to extend it so that it can check a text file of keywords. Then ask for it to check in a more complicated way, to determine if any of the top sites have an exact title match. Then have it include the exact match search results as well. Test each feature to make sure that you're happy with the new functionality, and that none of the old functionality broke. The point is that you want to avoid providing a programmer with a laundry list of features and ask them to implement them all. That increases the risk of failure exponentially.
															- 7. Keep doing #6 until you run out of features that you need implemented.
														- DOs and DON'Ts
															- Do plan very carefully
																- The more time you spend detailing exactly what you want, the less money you'll waste.
															- Do take responsibility for the outcome
																- You're the project manager, they're your contractor. It's their job to make you happy, but you need to clear about the outcome, and continually reinforce it.
															- Don't feature creep
																- Tell the programmer that you only want them to implement one feature at a time. Have them tell you when you're asking for too many things at the same time. If you only do one thing at a time, you'll never get caught out of money with a half-finished application.
															- Don't let the programmer build for the future.
																- A classic mistake is that a programmer will spend time building an infrastructure, or code library, to make things better in the future. It's always a mistake. Stick to 1 feature at a time, and they can revise their code as they go.
															- Don't be afraid
																- I know programmers can seem like dark wizards working a mystical black art, but they're just people with specialized knowledge. You would be amazed at how many great programmers there are out there who love to solve problems and save people time.
															- Don't procrastinate
																- It's easy to do the same easy thing over and over without learning how to automate it, but that's a huge mistake. You need to spend your time on the valuable complicated things that will build your revenue over the long term. Let the computers do the scut work.
													- Graphic & Web Designer
														- Must know photoshop
														- How to produce new web designs, custom post images etc
														- For post images:
														  Digital Artist: OK, I get the message, the stock images on my site suck. Even the ones that I didn’t steal still suck.
														  One idea that I’ve batted around on and off for a long time, but never taken seriously until now, was having someone develop accompanying images, graphs and artwork to correspond with the ideas in each of my articles. Some of these images would be dictated by me, but others would be left up to the artist (i.e., “Come up with something that signifies desperation in relationships,”) and then let them go crazy with it. I’m very flexible with this position. I don’t have a specific type of artist in mind. I’m interested in anybody that I feel can add a lot of power and value to my written content.
														  I want to give this a shot. I think it could add a lot to my content. There have been a couple blogs lately that have succeeded in adding interesting images to their posts (examples here and here). And it’s something that some magazines and periodicals such as The New Yorker and The Economist have been doing for decades. The direction the internet is going is of greater interactivity and a great variety of media within content. This is a direction I’d like to play with and perhaps spearhead.
														  What I’m looking for in a digital artist is basically a mutual fanship. They are a fan of my work and are excited to create art for it and I’m a fan of their work and am excited to have their work be paired up with mine.
														- ROLE: GRAPHIC / WEB DESIGNER
														- Tinkering around in Photoshop is a massive waste of time for every entrepreneur that
														- doesn’t know what he / she is doing. We do it though, right? We love the idea of letting our
														- Superhero Syndrome take over and we believe that we can do everything – including
														- graphic design.
														- Please – c’mon people… Pay those that truly DO know what they’re doing, to do it right
														- the first time – not only will it look better, but you’ll be able to spend time on a marketing
														- plan, or putting together your next info-product!
														- ■ 89. Designing Logos, Banners, Icons, eBook Covers and Headers
														- ■ 90. Designing Infographics Images (Content Provided)
														- ■ 91. Designing Websites, Creating Mock-Ups
														- ■ 92. Designing Landing / Sales / Opt-In Pages
														- ■ 93. Basic Video Editing (Splicing Intros & Outros with Raw Footage)
													- Web Developer
													  When I launched my first blog in 2009 it was a mess. And here’s the kicker – I had
													  purchased a perfectly good looking premium theme, and then spent the best part of 3-
													  days ‘customizing’ it (and I use that term as loosely as possible, trust me!). The result was
													  what you could probably imagine from someone with no coding experience – a
													  catastrophe!
													  While you’re putting together content plans and a publishing schedule for your site, get a
													  super-skilled Web Developer VA to handle the coding and customizing side of things for
													  you. It’s just plain smart!
														- ■ 94. Support and Develop WordPress (PHP) Websites
														- ■ 95. Install WordPress PlugIns and Themes
														- ■ 96. WordPress Theme Customization
														- ■ 97. WordPress Functionality and PlugIn Enhancement
														- ■ 98. Site Maintenance / Security and Troubleshooting
														- ■ 99. CRM Integration & Social Media Integration
														- ■ 100. Payment Gateway Integration
														- ■ 101. Install and Support an Email Ticketing System (eg. ZenDesk)

												- Content Creator

													- Editor (for GrowthLab.com)
														- At I Will Teach You To Be Rich, each month over 1 million millennials read our material on living a Rich Life, including automating their finances, making more money, or finding their Dream Job.
														- Recently, we launched a new site, GrowthLab.com. To our readers, launching and growing an online business is one of their biggest goals.
															- We believe everyone can create a thriving online business that lets you share your passion and skills with the world — even if you don’t have an MBA.
															- We believe there’s a limit to how much you can cut, but no limit to how much you can earn. And for millions of our readers, an online business is an important part of that Rich Life.
															- We believe in creating world-class products, then teaching others exactly how we did it.
														- GrowthLab is designed to give our readers every strategy, tactic, and tool they need to launch an online business. Because of our business and relationships, we can share the latest techniques and proven strategies in exhaustive detail.
														- We’ve created some of the most respected programs on the market — Zero to Launch, Accelerator, and Call to Action — and we’re eager to share these programs with more millennials. When you think of creating an online business, there should be only one logical source: GrowthLab.
														- We’re seeking an exceptional editor to help us build GrowthLab into a powerhouse authority site for online businesses. As Editor, you’ll work closely with our CEO, Ramit Sethi, and our Editorial team to craft a content strategy and set the brand and voice guidelines for GrowthLab that will achieve our ambitious traffic and subscriber goals.
														- This position will be responsible for creating and editing content, planning content production, and building an editorial engine.
														- Create and edit public-facing content for the GrowthLab site:  You should have an intuitive grasp of which content will engage readers, and back this intuition up with analytics and tracking. This content includes original content, guest posts, thought leadership pieces, case studies, contests, infographics, ebooks, case studies, student narratives, video scripts and site copy. Material may be authored by you, Ramit, in-house copywriting staff, in-house subject matter experts, guest experts, top students or freelance writers.
														- Plan content production: You'll be responsible for creating an Editorial Calendar, and will keep the brand voice consistent across published content. You'll recommend strategies for how to use the blog to generate qualified traffic and leads.
														- Build an editorial engine: The ideal Editor is tapped into a network of writers and is capable of recruiting a pool of rockstar content creators inside and outside the company that can meet the production goals and quality standards established for GrowthLab.
														- Who are you? Most likely a recognized blogger/content writer in your own right, you’ve successfully scaled a blog or online content site and know what it takes to captivate and cultivate an audience of thousands.  You’re a talented writer who knows the distinction between good, great, and extraordinary copy. You have the instincts and editorial eye for trending topics and addictive content that will convert traffic into subscribers and keep them coming back for more. You have a comprehensive understanding of online business and growth marketing and are obsessed with staying at the forefront of trends.
														- If you think you’ve got what we’re looking for, we want to hear from you! This is a once-in-a-career opportunity to use your talents and do what you love doing at a larger scale while enjoying the support of IWT’s brand, reputation and backing.
														- We are a 100% virtual company - you can work from anywhere around the country!
														- Responsibilities
															- 1. Create & edit content
																- Translate the editorial vision for GrowthLab into a content strategy and plan that meets targeted traffic and lead generation goals
																- Edit and manage the GrowthLab blog and site, including maintaining an editorial calendar and a consistent pipeline of content
																- Manage the production of a broad portfolio of deep and distinctive content, including:
																	- Original content pieces and SEO posts
																	- Ultimate Guides and other posts derived from our popular course content
																	- SME articles on specific online business topics.
																	- Behind-the-scenes insights built on Ramit's experience in running IWT
																	- Business journalism pieces - Interviews, profiles or features on specific business owners or events
																	- Student case studies
																- Manage to KPIs including subscriber growth, lead conversions and expanding the overall reach of GrowthLab.com
																- Coordinate with other teams to ensure interdepartmental tasks (growth, design, video, marketing automation) are completed effectively and efficiently
															- 2. Plan content production
																- Write articles and posts on a regular basis on a wide range of topics relevant to our audience’s needs and wants
																- Provide editorial guidance to other contributors to maintain and ensure a consistent brand voice
																- Implement standards and policies to ensure that all content is original, factual and current
																- Oversee the creation and delivery of content for our partners (e.g. guest posts)
																- Optimize content for search engines and lead generation
																- Generate new ideas for content, taking inspiration from inside and outside the company, and plan and deliver long-form content pieces
																- Coordinate editorial, design and production processes and workflow to produce high quality content in tight timelines
															- 3. Build an editorial engine
																- Recruit and onboard high caliber copywriting talent — both in-house and freelance — to support current and future GrowthLab editorial needs
																- Guide editorial staff to maintain brand and quality standards for all published content
																- Manage employee and contracted copywriters and editors who contribute content to the GrowthLab site — including scheduling, mentoring, training, contracts, invoices, performance evaluations, salary/title negotiations, and other managerial duties
																- Build systems, trainings, tools, and resources to streamline and improve the development of high-quality, on-brand content
														- Education & Experience
															- 3-4+ years experience in blog writing, blog management and/or content marketing, preferably with a high traffic site
															- Proven ability to manage an editorial calendar and regular development of content

															- A passion for and strong understanding of online business
															- Superb writing and editing skills, as well as the ability to adopt the style, tone and voice of our brand
															- Excellent organizational skills to manage a team and projects with many moving parts
															- Familiarity with our Zero to Launch and Call to Action programs a strong plus
															- Able to work as part of a remote team

													- ROLE: GENERAL VIRTUAL ASSISTANT (GVA)
														- Email & Schedule Management Tasks
															- One of the biggest time-suckers in the land of entrepreneurship is email. Before I actively
															- took it upon myself to remove myself from my business and become a Virtual CEO (listen
															- to the full story in episode 16 of The New Business Podcast), I was trapped in Outlook for
															- up to 7-hours a day. It was madness.
															- Working with a GVA to handle your email, as well as things like your calendar is essential
															- to becoming more productive as a modern-age entrepreneur.
															- ■ 1. Filtering Emails / Managing Spam
															- ■ 2. Database Building / Updating Contacts or CRM
															- ■ 3. Answering Customer Service Emails /Tickets / Chat Support
															- ■ 4. Sending of Greetings eCards, Event Invitations, etc.
															- ■ 5. Calendar Management
															- ■ 6. Appointment Scheduling
															- ■ 7. Travel Arrangement and Planning
															- ■ 8. Reminder Services
														- File Storage & Organization Tasks
															- We live in a world of flashing lights, buttons and other things that distract us consistently
															- throughout the course of our working day. Being organized is about as important as being
															- productive. If not more. VA’s can help you systematize and put processes in place to help
															- your business become more organized.
															- ■ 9. Dropbox / Google Drive Organization
															- ■ 10. Data Entry in Word, or Google Docs
															- ■ 11. Creating / Managing Spreadsheets
															- ■ 12. Preparing Powerpoint / keynote Presentations
															- ■ 13. PDF Conversion, Splitting and Merging
														- Administrative & Blogging Tasks
															- I’ve talked about how VA’s can manage your blog, as well as how they can help with the
															- marketing of your blog before – however, there is so, so much more your GVA can do for
															- you when it comes to additional admin tasks, and generally helping you, day to day on the
															- managing of projects, and your marketing efforts.

														- Email Marketing
															- All this work is pointless if you’re not a) growing your email marketing list, and b) engaging
															- with that list on a regular basis. It’s been said that for every name on your list, you should
															- be making an average of $1 a month. So, working with a VA to help grow and market to
															- your list is a complete no-brainer in my book! This takes the concept of choosing tasks to
															- outsource to virtual staff to the next level – a level that equals money!

													- TASKS
														- RESEARCH
															- Making list of topic ideas in any niche using Google Keyword tool
															- Group similar topics and figure out if they can be turned into a seriesof posts, or even more!
															- Figure out what TYPE of content should be created to serve the topic best. Written, audio, video, etc.
															- Produce an outline for posts andpodcasts, or a rough storyboard foronline video content.
															- Find similar online content and create a ‘Likewise List’ to use later on, when promoting.
															- Identify Facebook and LinkedIn groups which can be used to promote and share content when published
														- CREATION
															- Edit video, including intro and
															- outro bumpers and lower thirds.
															- Transcribe the entire video file into Word, to use as blog posts,YouTube description, and more!
															- Edit and finalize podcast session, including intro, outro, CTA’s and list episode mentionable’s.
															- Transcribe the podcast episode for use as additional SEO content.
															- Transcription into a free PDF download (i.e. SmartPassiveIncome)
															- Create ‘Draft Post’ in Wordpress, adding bold, italics, etc., to your pre-written content.
															- Format the Post: Add H2 and H3 tags to sub-headings and sub-sections and bold, as needed.
															- Find and add an image to thepost, if needed. Best to use your own images whenever possible.
															- Add image title, alt-text & caption into Wordpress
															- Embed related video content.
															- Capture video screenshots if required, then resize and insert them into the post if needed.
															- Embed any audio, or podcast sessions if required.
															- Put the post in the correct categories, and be sure to also include relevant tags
															- Optimize the post for SEO using the correct plugin’s.
															- Create ‘Tweetable’ images to use on Facebook, etc.
														- PUBLISHING
															- Upload video to YouTube, with
															- title, links, keywords, categories,
															- as well as transcribed text.
															- Add video to relevant playlist.
															- Upload audio file to server, tagged and with podcast image attached.
															- Final proof read for spelling, punctuation and grammar errors
															- Draft and schedule ‘Broadcast’ email in Aweber
															- Schedule post, or simply hit ‘Publish’
														- PROMOTION
															- Share your content on your personal
															- social media accounts, as well as
															- your blog’s Facebook page.
															- Share your content on related Facebook and LinkedIn groups.
															- Schedule Tweets to go out every 6-hours for the next 48-hours after publishing content.
															- Social bookmark the content on StumbleUpon, Reddit, Digg, etc.
															- Contact anyone you mentioned in the content with a pre-written email, as they might like to share it.
															- Comment on the Top 5 posts from your ‘Likewise List’.
															- Wake up your Email List by sending that drafted ‘Broadcast’ message.
															- Social bookmark any content commented on, helping to gain more traffic to the post.
															- Share the featured image of your post on Pinterest, Flickr, etc., including a link back!
															- Share infographic’s on the top distribution sites.
															- Share PDF transcripts of your video, or podcast content to Docstoc, SlideShare, etc.
															- Promote your SlideShare content on LinkedIn, Twitter & Facebook in order to get homepage exposure.
															- Post your ‘Tweetable’ images to all your social media profiles and pages, to promote clickthru’s.
														- MARKETING
															- Add this recent content to your ‘Blog Bank’, for easy access when creating fresh content and linking.
															- Build internal links to new content from your archive
															- Create a summary of the content and include it on Tumblr. Add media, and a ‘Read More’ link to original
															- Social bookmark the summarized content on Tumblr, as well as StumbleUpon, Reddit, Digg, etc
															- VA’s can also create your PowerPoint presentations!
												- Research Assistant/Content Curator
													- learning/summarising,
													- research assistant for posts
													- often academic research
													- Description
														- Content Curator: I spend a large amount of time reading and researching for each of the articles I write. One of the biggest time sinks is for me to double-back and check on the research and many of the statements I’m making. Often it’s been weeks or months since I’ve read the relevant book or article that I want to write about.
														- The content curator will research certain topics and they will help me cull the information together in a simple and consumable way. They will write summaries, find interest anecdotes or examples and generally keep me on my A-game by making sure my writing doesn’t suck. Their responsibilities will also spread into social media, finding interesting articles or new article ideas, and proofreading and occasionally contributing to articles.
														- The content curator needs to be creative but also adept with processing large amounts of information in accurate and meaningful ways. What I want is a nerd. Someone who loves to read, to learn, to understand complex and difficult ideas. And then they must have the ability to condense and express those complex ideas.
														- Research And Data Gathering On Entrepreneurial Topics, Books And Courses
														- You will be responsible for pinpointing and summarising key content applicable to our audience of entrepreneurs.
														- i have an e-mail address that she also has a login for. and a phone number and voice mail. i give out both to people that i don’t really want to talk to. she fields all the messages, and forwards me solutions and summaries of things i actually want to know about.
															- piles of research on the latest marketing best practices,
														- The Role: Freedom Content Manager
														- You will be working semi-remotely and need to be in or relocate to Melbourne, Australia.
														- It has no set hours – just deliverables and accountability.
														- Therefore you will need to be extremely self motivated and self sufficient – having the ability to work alone to consistently and cheerfully achieve weekly targets and deadlines.
														- YOUR TASKS WILL PRIMARILY INVOLVE BUT NOT BE LIMITED TO:
														- Social Media Management
														- You will be managing the content and conversations on social media networks such as Facebook, Twitter and Instagram
														- Managing Our Teams Content Schedule
														- You will be responsible for managing and holding accountable our small team of content providers including our executive instructors and virtual assistants.
														- Building Accountability Reports And Summaries
														- You will be responsible for managing the weekly reporting and summaries of our targets met throughout the week.
														- YOU NEED TO BE:
														- You’ll ideally need to be familiar with WordPress and HTML/websites. You won’t be expected to code or anything, but knowledge of these systems is desirable.
														- More SYSTEMS and LOGICALLY minded rather than visionary and creative
														- Whilst there is room for creativity in this role, as a manager/executor you will need to be evaluating your tasks and day from a systems mindset. Using and developing spreadsheets and tools to drive targets and results.
														- A great communicator
														- You’ll be dealing with many different people on a daily basis both online and offline. You need to be great at communicating in both written and interpersonal form.
														- General positivity and good vibes are a must.
														- Extremely self motivated
														- This isn’t an internship. It’s a paid role where you will be compensated and held accountable for RESULTS and PRODUCTION.
														- We have no office, so you’ll be working 90% from home (or a cafe, or wherever you are most productive). Most people can’t handle work without a boss breathing down their neck. It’s essential you can.
														- You may be called upon from time to time to shoot a video or be somewhere at weird hours, for example 8pm on a Saturday night. You will need to show up on time ready and motivated when called upon.
														- You need to pride yourself in being an ruthless executor of tasks.
														- You need to love getting shit done, alone. Your internal motivation should be seeing the to-do items for that day crossed off and will not stop until the mission is complete.
														- Underneath all the glamor of being able to help guys like Tommy achieve freedom through their business – is a whole heap of grunt work and tasks to be completed that needs unquestionable grit and military-like execution.
														- This means you LOVE seeing our business achieve it’s goals and targets.
														- Interested in a Full Time position with a massive room for growth
														- We have a very popular blog with hundreds of people signed up to our training program. As we grow this year and the next, so will your compensation and responsibility. Our aim is to be the number one entrepreneur training platform in the world. We are a looking for someone who will want to be a part of this for the next few years at least. Don’t apply if you’re looking for something short term.
														- Love Social Media and Entrepreneurial Content
														- You will be responsible for Social Media and Entrepreneurial style content all day. You need to love and be highly interested in these two things.
														- As a part of this role, you will be expected to come to our twice-annual freedom events and meet ups held at exotic locations all around the world.
														- Whilst this role will be 99% in Melbourne – you have to love a bit of travel too.
														- Deadlines and Details:
														- Applications close Monday 24th February.
														- You will need to be available for an interview on the 25th or 26th.
														- Start date: Monday 3rd March.
														- Compensation: Whilst I wont discuss exact figures on the blog , this role will compensate based on an average marketing graduates salary and be enough for you to comfortably live in the best city in the world: Melbourne, Australia.
														- If you’re from overseas you will need to have a full current working visa or be able to stay in Australia permanently without any issues.
														- Finally before you apply – you need to be someone who can “carry a message to Garcia”.
														- If this still excites the hell out of you and you can see yourself growing and doing this for the next several years… this might be the role for you…
														- To Apply:
														- 1. Record a 1:00 minute (max) video of yourself talking about why this role is for you. Upload to YouTube, set as unlisted and forward the link to:
														- tim@freedombusinessblog.com
														- Subject: I’m the one
														- Hint. There is video editing component to the role. Show me your stuff.
														- 2. Fill out the application form located here.
														- 3. Be ready for an interview from the 25th onwards.
														- PLEASE SHARE WITH FRIENDS AND FAMILY IF YOU THINK THEY ARE MORE SUITABLE.
														- Any questions please ask below in the comment section.
														- Looking forward to reviewing the applications and entering a new big phase for our amazing community.
														- Timothy Marc

													  teach 'my voice' and vision - westerners
													  
													  The main danger I see in this biz model is believing that you can be the only content marketer, which means it’s a career you can never take time off from vs being a business.
													  Even if the brand is centered around you, over time start working with ghost writers and guest writers. Blogging can be a big time suck when you’re trying to build the sales funnel. People who’ve done that part right: Copyblogger, Tom Ewer, etc
													  It also allows you to avoid the second problem which is: most money comes in selling to the masses. Not that it’s bad thing but have to connect with low-quality people all day everyday may get tiring.
													  
													  
													  We’re expanding our reach, and we’re looking for a copywriter to help us communicate our key messages via partner articles, sales promotions and internal messaging. Your copy will be seen by millions of people.
													  This position will require three key skills:
													  The ability to quickly grasp the “voice” of I Will Teach You To Be Rich
													  •A rapid ability to take large amounts of information and distill them into engaging copy
													  •Absolute requirement to hit deadlines
													  •Superb communication and project management skills
													  •The ability to craft engaging and effective narrative and sales copy
													  You’ll work with our Content Manager to draft and edit copy. You should be comfortable with direct feedback and rapid iteration.
													  We anticipate hundreds of applicants, and we’re looking for the best. You should expect a small writing assignment as part of the application process.
													  Tasks and Responsibilities:
													  •Research, collect, outline, draft and edit pieces for use on an IWT channel (blog, email, course, etc) or via one of IWT's strategic partners
													  •Produce on-brand copy that maintains the unique voice, talking points, and high quality associated with IWT
													  •Maintain flexibility to turn around rush assignments on short notice
													  •Edit a variety of IWT copy. Attention to detail is critical
													  •Load, proof, test, and update blog posts and pages in Wordpress
													  •Draft, load, proof and test social media content
													  •Ability to work independently
													  •Ability to manage and report your progress for on-going writing projects
													  •Ability to write effective sales copy
													  •Writes clearly and informatively
													  •Able to vary writing style to meet audience and medium needs
													  •High attention to quality, accuracy and thoroughness
													  •Must have a portfolio that demonstrates strong abilities, standout creative thinking, and exceptional writing skills
													  •Must have experience writing content for the web
													  •Ideal candidate is very familiar with the IWT brand and products
													  •Experience with WordPress is a plus
													  
													  
													  To apply, please complete this writing test: Copywriter Writing Test
													  
													  Draft partner article on “How to Network with VIPs”
													  This test will cover your ability to compile existing IWT material into engaging and useful articles and write in the IWT “voice.”
													  Step 1: Review sample partner articles
													  
													  5 things never to say in a job interview
													  Worst Career Advice Ever
													  Secret perks of credit cards
													  Master your interview with these preparation secrets
													  Money Secrets the Pros Won’t Tell You
													  Step 2: Outline new article
													  Use the blog to find talking points and scripts.
													  Step 3: Draft a new article
													  Pick a compelling title and positioning for an article (about networking with VIPs) that a partner would run. You can reuse key phrases, but it should be mostly original writing. Use scripts verbatim. ~850 words
													  Step 4: Submit outline & article

												- Assorted
													- A graphics guy or someone who can use Photoshop. I say, “Hey, I want an image of X for an upcoming post,” and he delivers.
													- Copyediting.
													- Moderate the comments.
													- Turn posts into mini eBooks.
													- Talk to me about post concepts. Keep track of these concepts, create a spreadsheet or checklist, and follow-up to ensure we are getting through them.
													- Organize my email box. Ensure that every email (that is not parasitic or asking for something for nothing or otherwise is stupid and time wasting) gets a response from me.
												- SEO
												- Products
													- Product Developer for Online Courses
														- If you love to research new topics, know how to distill your findings into actionable strategies, and take pleasure in becoming a domain expert in a variety of fields…
														- …then I want you as my online training course product developer.
														- This is a full-time, virtual position, meaning you can work from your home, the coffee shop, or wherever it is you work best.
														- (This is not for people looking for part-time positions or consulting contracts).
														- Details About This Position…
															- At Social Triggers, we create online content (both free and paid) and cover a lot of different topic areas (like negotiation, persuasion, salesmanship, social skills, marketing, and productivity)…
															- …And I’m looking for someone who can help research new domain areas and curate the information they find into usable frameworks for entrepreneurs and executives.
															- These frameworks will then be turned into in-depth online courses, webinar presentations, ebooks, videos, podcasts, and other formats that help us share our knowledge and expertise with the world.
															- If you’ve never created online courses before, that’s no matter. I can teach you how to do that. What I can’t teach you is to love research, love reading, and love working in a situation where it’s both qualitative and quantitative.
														- Here Are Your Required Responsibilities
															- Conduct research (both internal and competitive) to come up with new product recommendations – and see these recommendations through from start to finish.
															- Review current products, videos, and content, and see what else we can cover (or create) to help out millions of entrepreneurs and executives.
															- Be comfortable knowing that you’re working with a lean organization – and may have to work on other projects that may fall outside the scope of training course development.
															- Come up with a training course idea, flesh out the entire curriculum (knowing what to include… and what NOT to include is vital), and see the course from idea to completion.
															- Be comfortable turning conversations into content. If you come up with a product idea, you may be required to interview people — people like me, other experts, and the like. It’s up to you to turn those conversations into possible video ideas or training material for entrepreneurs and executives.
														- This position is PERFECT for you if:
															- You’re the type of person who reads one or two books about business, self growth, or biographies each week.
															- You’re GREAT at finding the 20% that yields 80% of results in a variety of domain areas (This means, if you read a book about productivity, you can flesh out the 2 big ideas that people can implement to get maximum results fast).
															- You’re familiar with product positioning – and how to ensure we create products that stand out (not blend in) in the market place.
															- You’re comfortable reading through blog comments, online surveys, and other forms of data to figure out what it is people want (What they say they want is different then what they really want. It’s up to you to find the difference).
															- You can go through existing Social Triggers training courses (and content) and make strategic recommendations on how to make it better (You’ll have to back up your suggestions with data and possibly comments from existing customers). Also, you’ll be able to recommend how to package up existing content into new forms (like ebooks) that can be given away for free.
															- You’re happy working with ambiguity. This is a creative role that’s inspired by data and research.
															- You’re a GREAT communicator. There are different methods of communication (writing, creating presentations, writing video scripts, and etc) and you should be comfortable with all of them.
															- You’re savvy when it comes to researching competitors and finding product development opportunities for Social Triggers.
														- This position is NOT for you if:
															- If the last time you read a non-fiction book about business, marketing, or self growth was a 3 months (or more) ago, then this position is NOT for you. This position requires a lot of reading and research.
															- If you’re looking for a “side job” while you’re growing your own business, then this isn’t to for you. This is a contract position for 30 days. And if it works out during this 30 day trial period, we’ll move you over to full-time employee status.
															- If you’re not willing to sign a non-disclosure agreement, then this is not for you.
															- If you’re looking to be a front-facing content creator, this position is not for you. This is a behind the scenes role.
														- Here’s Why You’ll Love Working for Social Triggers…
															- This position has high expectations, but here’s the deal:
															- 1. You’ll be paid to do what you already love… learning new things.
															- 2. You’ll get to work in a fast growing online training and software company. And there are loads of growth opportunities now (and in the future).
															- 3. You’ll learn the ins and outs of online marketing, product development, and the psychology behind persuasion, salesmanship, self-help, and more.
															- 4. You’ll be part of a company that’s has one main goal: Helping people get more of what they want (whether it’s in business or life). We change lives – and we’re proud of it.
															- Plus, there are other cool perks like…
															- You’ll get paid vacation.
															- We cover your health insurance 100% (for individuals), and have a great family plan option as well.
															- You can work from anywhere.
														- Here’s What You Need To Do Next…
															- Thing #1
															- If you think you’re a fit for this position, fill out this short survey.
															- https://www.surveymonkey.com/s/5JTDFZD
															- We’ll be reviewing applications over the following weeks, and we’ll be sure to get back to you after we review your application.
															- Thing #2
															- Email your resume to careers@socialtriggers.com. Please note, use this exact subject line: “Resume for Product Developer.”
															- If you do not use that exact subject line, your application won’t be considered.
															- The deadline for this application is April 12th.
													- Product manager (affiliate marketing/eCommerce)
														- Bulletproof Product Manager Job Description
															- I’m seeking a kick-ass product manager to join the Bulletproof team. Work from home, but be in the Pacific Northwest, preferably near Seattle where our in-person meetings happen every couple weeks.
															- Help me bring the world’s highest performance supplements and foods to reality.
															- You’d be responsible for some new Bulletproof products, product rationalization, managing product life cycles and optimizing product mix. You will also be driving new product development processes, handling all elements of getting a product from concept through launch. And once a product is launched, manage occasional updates and changes, but day-to-day ongoing management is handled elsewhere.
															- Strategic Product Marketing
																- Ensure new products you manage are in alignment with Bulletproof mission and values. We don’t sell inferior crap, ever. If it can be better, it will be.
																- Write and implement product plan to identify market issues and untapped opportunities.
																- Conduct market research and make fact based decisions based on analysis.
																- Continuously monitor nutritional supplement and functional food industries by reading industry emails and magazines, attending select shows (i.e., Expo West), observing retail products, etc.
																- Identify product gaps and recommend new products and product reformulations to continuously upgrade the performance of our products and our customers
																- Continuously monitor competitor pricing, packaging, positioning, branding, advertising, marketing, online and printed communications, merchandising and overall effectiveness along with changes in the competitive landscape worth note, and recommend actions.
																- Develop and maintain expertise in select product types and health categories. Expertise includes a keen understanding by market of category sales, innovative ingredients and formulas, top-performing products, and segment drivers (market trends, economic issues, government/regulatory considerations, consumer trends, and segment user psychographics and demographics for both dietary supplements and functional foods/beverages).
																- Develop creative strategy (including positioning), claims, brand names, promotions, and other strategic and tactical elements for new products.
																- Provide information to aid in the creation of product pages, collateral, and promotions.
																- Work with Director of Marketing on new product launches.
																- Responsible for product pricing including recommending changes.
																- Conduct analysis of competitive products, current product performance, and the impact of promotions on performance. Match analyses with supplier price changes as well as corporate and distributor objectives to recommend appropriate price changes.
																- Follow a solid Product Lifecycle Management Process and manage product lifecycles.
																- Monitor monthly sales and suggest ways to improve performance where appropriate.
															- Market Expertise and Coordination
																- Develop and maintain expertise in assigned markets. Primary contact for marketing and content generation around the products you manage.
																- Coordinate new product initiatives with content team.
																- Work together with and gather appropriate market data from content and social media teams personnel.
																- Distributor Interaction
																- Work with key distributors and ambassadors to gather feedback on proposed new products and to collect input on desired new products.
																- When necessary, conduct field tests for new product concepts.
															- New Product
																- Develop and coordinate new products in the product review and launch process (formulation, feasibility, forecast, claims, unique selling proposition, pricing, content creation, and distributor pitch, etc.)
																- Coordinate as necessary with legal, manufacturing, purchasing, R&D, QA, and content team.
																- Manage new product process to ensure timely launches.
																- Act as project manager for your launches.
																- Review complaints and suggestions collected from content team and elsewhere and respond appropriately.
															- Desired Skills and Experience
																- BA or BS required.
																- MBA or Advanced degree in science preferred
																- 7-10 years marketing experience, with time in packaged goods or beverage and new product development
																- Grocery industry and coffee industry a bonus
																- Experience in health & wellness, specifically nutritional supplements is preferred
																- Be extremely proactive. Get things done.
																- Work well from home, a coffee shop, or an office.
																- Highly organized, detail oriented and excellent project management skills.
																- Have had at least one dream with a Gantt chart or critical path in it.
																- Flexible and comfortable with frequent change in focus and direction
																- Microsoft Excel makes you happy
															- To Apply
																- Send resume and cover letter to: hr@bulletproofexec.com and include in the subject line: Bulletproof Product Manager.
												- Strategic Partnerships Manager
													- If you are comfortable reaching out to people you don’t know, striking a win-win deal for both parties, and know how to successfully build strategic relationships…
													- …then I want you as my strategic partnerships manager.
													- This is a part-time position with hopes of making it full-time as soon as early as July 2015.
													- Details About This Position…
														- I launched Social Triggers back in 2011, and it quickly became a top destination for business owners and entrepreneurs who are looking to get ahead in business and life.
														- To date, we’ve reached more than 5,000,000 people around the world, have customers in 69 countries, and we sell online education products and software.
														- And that’s why we need you.
														- We’d like to reach more people with our free content, online training courses, and software products.
														- And your main goal will be to identify potential strategic partnerships for Social Triggers, build the Social Triggers partnership program, and coordinate partnership promotions.
														- The ideal candidate will be great at managing relationships, extremely detail oriented (it’s tough to manage partnership promotions while accommodating busy schedules), and will thrive in a position that’s closely monitored and linked to bottom line business results.
													- Here are your responsibilities:
														- Begin building the strategic partnership program for Social Triggers. You’ll need to find – and acquire – Strategic Partners who are interested in promoting Social Triggers online courses and software products. At first, you’ll focus on our software product, ZippyCourses.
														- Be comfortable reaching out to people we have a relationship with and also people we don’t have a relationship with. You’ll need to build strong relationships with people and businesses of all sizes – from the fast growing startups in Silicon Valley to up and coming bloggers. Some of these people will be familiar with what we do – others won’t. You must be able to recruit both.
														- You must be okay with starting from scratch. We’ve done very little with regard to attracting and managing strategic partners – and you’ll be responsible for starting (and shaping) our entire program from the ground up.
														- You must be comfortable with seeing a strategic partnership from start to finish. You must create a list of partners, decide who to reach out to and when, schedule the promotions, monitor the results of those promotions, and ensure the strategic partners get paid.
														- You must have the ability to think creatively about who we reach out to and why. Just because they have the ability to generate results doesn’t mean we want to work with those people. Our brand at Social Triggers is the most important thing hands down – and we only want to work with people who care equally about their brand.
													- Required Skills
														- Strong excel skills are a must. You will be tracking the results of the strategic partnership program across various channels. We’ll want to know which partnerships to pursue and which to ignore – and the data you collect will help us figure it out.
														- Strong project management skills. You’ll be working with several different partners with varying timelines. You must be able to manage the project and relationship from start to finish.
														- You must have the ability to multi-task. You’ll be working with several different strategic partners are various points in their partnership cycle with Social Triggers. You must be able to manage that – and ensure everyone has a remarkable experience.
													- Here’s Why You’ll Love Working for Social Triggers…
														- This position has high expectations, but here’s the deal:
														- 1. You’ll be paid to do what you already love… making deals that lead directly to bottom line revenue.
														- 2. You’ll get to work in a fast growing online training and software company. And there are loads of growth opportunities now (and in the future).
														- 3. You’ll learn the ins and outs of what makes a partnership GREAT vs just okay.
														- 4. You’ll meet a lot of people – both people we are currently connected to and people who we would like to reach out to in the near future.
														- 4. You’ll be part of a company that’s has one main goal: Helping people get more of what they want (whether it’s in business or life). We change lives – and we’re proud of it.
														- Plus, there are other cool perks like…
														- You can work from anywhere.
													- Here’s What You Need To Do Next…
														- Thing #1
														- If you think you’re a fit for this position, fill out this short survey.
														- https://www.surveymonkey.com/s/K357F6Q
														- We’ll be reviewing applications over the following weeks, and we’ll be sure to get back to you after we review your application.
														- Thing #2
														- Email your resume to careers@socialtriggers.com. Please note, use this exact subject line: “Resume for Strategic Partnership Manager.”
														- If you do not use that exact subject line, your application won’t be considered.
														- The deadline for this application is April 14th.
												- Growth Designer
													- The Giant Hairy Problem You’ll Be Solving
														- We’ve got some pretty aggressive goals for growing our business and we’re building a fully functional team to drive growth forward. We’ve already got world-class marketers and engineers on our Growth team. But there’s an absolutely critical skill-set missing: design. That’s where you come in.
														- You’re not just another body that makes things “pretty”. We need a much larger commitment from you. You’ll be responsible for the UX of the Growth team. For every campaign, app, and test, you’ll push to give our users a world-class experience that delights them at every step.
														- And this is your giant hairy problem. How do we meet the goals of the business while over-delivering on value to our users? You’ll tackle this head-on and find the perfect blend between the two. You’ll push the limits of your design craft to meet the needs of our users and the business.
														- You’ll Learn More In 3 Months Than You Did All Of Last Year
														- We’re not here to follow mediocre best practices. We want to deeply understand what drives a business forward. Which design variables matter? How do we persuade people to take action? When have we truly delivered the value that we’ve promised to our users?
														- We have a simple goal on the Growth team: learn faster than anyone else in the industry. The faster we learn, the faster we grow the company. You’ll be right in the thick of it. Your ideas will have a chance to prove themselves just like the rest of the team. Some of our ideas will shock us with their results. Maybe they totally crash and burn. Or maybe a random, boring idea throws us into an entirely new level. Either way, we force ourselves to constantly evolve our understanding of what actually drives growth.
														- We reach this depth of understanding by operating at an incredibly high tempo. Our iteration and feedback loops happen at a blistering pace. Testing combined with an intense tempo means you’ll learn more about what actually impacts customers than any previous project you’ve worked on.
														- Do you want to spend your design career following best practices or do you want to see what truly impacts people?
													- You’ll work on projects like:
														- Variants for A/B testing.
														- Launching new landing pages for campaigns.
														- Working with our engineers to build light-weight freemium tools for our audience.
														- Improving our student apps to show them the value of living a Rich Life.
														- New funnels and signup flows.
														- Other marketing odds and ends (social profiles, PPC ads, email designs, etc).
													- What We're Looking For:
														- Design skills that drive conversions and actions. This is not a branding team, this is an acquisition team.
														- Can take an idea all the way to coding by hand in HTML/CSS and JavaScript. Even better if you can do final front-end review and get the project shipped.
														- This is not an entry-level design position, you’ll need to know how to drive projects and hold our team to a high design standard.
														- Growth projects are HIGHLY iterative. Many ideas won’t work. This means you’ll put your heart and soul into projects which will flop on a regular basis. That’s the nature of the work. But you need to be able to tackle the next iteration with as much gusto and passion as you did the first time. The magic doesn’t happen on launch day, it happens in the countless iterations that follow.
														- You should love design but you should love results more.
														- Our team moves at an exceptionally high tempo, you’ll be shipping weekly if not daily. You’ll need to find that perfect balance between high quality design and getting a project shipped.
														- Comfortable descoping to meet deadlines. Short deadlines and a high tempo mean we can’t do everything. You’ll need to instantly see the most valuable piece of any project, make it as lean as possible, and find a way to get it shipped so we can see if it had any impact.
														- This is a 100% remote position, work from anywhere you like.
													- If you’re excited about this, we’d love to talk to you. Use the “Apply Now” button below to get in touch with us.
													- Perks Of Working At IWT
														- ♥
														- Great health, dental, vision, life insurance plans for US employees all through an easy to use online provider
														- 📊
														- 401k — We want to help our team save for retirement! We offer a 401k plan, for US employees, with top rated funds and a generous employer match.
														- 📅
														- Generous Paid Time Off including National Holidays (we want you to be happy and spend time with your family and friends rejuvenating)
														- 
														- 100% remote - flexible work environment (We expect you to work hard but give you the freedom to do it in your preferred setting)
														- 💻
														- Technology reimbursement — Each month we offer a stipend to help cover your technology costs related to your role. Pay your internet bill on us!
														- 📕
														- Education reimbursement — Learning is very important in our culture! We offer a monthly stipend to encourage you to buy books, listen to webinars and purchase materials that will help you grow professionally and personally.
														- 👥
														- Department and Team Meetups where we get to set goals, map out processes and brainstorm. Oh ya, fun is built in too!
												- Website Development, Testing,
												- Legal, Finance and Accounting,
													- bookkeeper for accounting cashflow
												- Affiliate Manager
												  For my affiliates + getting more
												- PUAs - female discipline school, managing girls etc

												- **Advanced (6 figure income)**
													- Media Relationships Manager
														- The Giant Hairy Problem You’ll Be Solving
															- We produce amazing content at IWT. It changes lives. Get rid of your debt, get your dream job, earn income on the side, start your first business, get healthy and fit. We show people how to live a Rich Life. Talk to any of our students.
															- So much of our best material is completely free. But there are millions of people that haven’t seen it yet. They’re still struggling with debt, not enjoying their job, and don’t know the path for moving forward.
															- Our content engine is running a full speed. But we need help getting the word out. Who else should know about our content? Who has an audience that should hear about it? Which journalists, bloggers, and partners do we need to build relationships with?
															- This is why we need you.
															- This isn’t a cold call blitz where we spam every “influencer” with an endless list of links. In everything that we do, we lead with value and we’re always authentic. This is about finding people that would be a perfect fit for the Rich Life philosophy, building relationships with them, and making sure our content is such a good fit for their own audience that they can’t help but share it.
															- You’ll build the relationships that get our content in front of audiences that are 10X the size of our own in an honest and authentic way.
															- This is your giant hairy problem.
															- You’ll Learn More In 3 Months Than You Did All Of Last Year
															- We’re not here to follow mediocre best practices. We want to deeply understand what drives a business forward. Which design variables matter? How do we persuade people to take action? Which channels and campaigns drive the most value for the business?
															- We have a simple goal on the Growth team: learn faster than anyone else in the industry. The faster we learn, the faster we grow the company. You'll be in the middle of everything. You'll see how the entire business works and it'll be your ideas that help us make it even more effective. You'll learn exactly how to build a world-class growth machine.
															- We reach this depth of understanding by operating at an incredibly high tempo. Our iteration and feedback loops happen at a blistering pace. Testing combined with an intense tempo means you’ll learn faster than any of your peers.
														- You’ll work on projects like:
															- Research a list of journalists and bloggers that are actively writing about a given space, contact them, and find out what content would help their own work the most.
															- Coordinate with our own Editorial team to make sure content is being produced that fills those gaps.
															- Notify journalists and bloggers when we publish content that we know they want to see.
															- Hit monthly goals on how many major mentions we’ve received from target publications.
															- Help our Director of Growth network into target companies for major partnerships.
															- Line up interviews on podcasts and publications to kick off major launches.
															- Take a specific piece of content and find multiple publications that want to feature or mention it.
														- What We're Looking For:
															- You’ll need media outreach experience. If we give you a target publication (like Fortune), you should immediately have several ideas for how you’ll network your way in. 1-2 weeks later, you should have a solid connection.
															- You know when to go for the “ask” while also building a long-term relationship. We’re here to build for the long-term but we also need to get results.
															- You know when to hold back. Anybody can spam and grab any opportunity that comes by. Instead of competing with volume, you compete with quality.
															- Owning a goal inspires you. You’ll have a target number of high-quality mentions of IWT each month. You always find a way to hit goal.
															- Our team moves at an exceptionally high tempo and there's always a high volume of projects in motion. And it's only going to get crazier. You should thrive in that kind of environment.
															- This is a remote position, work from anywhere you like.
															- You must be based in the United States.
														- If you’re excited about this, we’d love to talk to you. Use the “Apply Now” button below to get in touch with us.
														- Perks Of Working At IWT
															- ♥
															- Great health, dental, vision, & life insurance plans for US employees all through an easy-to-use online provider
															- 📊
															- 401k — We want to help our team save for retirement! We offer a 401k plan for US employees, with top rated funds and a generous employer match
															- 
															- 100% remote — flexible work environment (We expect you to work hard, but give you the freedom to do it in your preferred setting)
															- 👥
															- Department and team meetups where we get to set goals, map out processes, and brainstorm. Oh yeah, fun is built in, too!
															- 📅
															- Generous paid time off including National Holidays (we want you to be happy and spend time with your family and friends rejuvenating)
															- 📕
															- Education reimbursement — Learning is very important in our culture! We offer a monthly stipend to encourage you to buy books, listen to webinars, and purchase materials that will help you grow professionally and personally
													- Marketing Automation/Email Marketers
														- Email Marketing Analyst
															- We are looking for an ace Email Marketing Analyst to coordinate email communications for I Will Teach You To Be Rich.
															- You will be trained in world-class email-marketing strategy. You’ll send millions of emails. You’ll monitor, tweak, and test changes. You’ll master the frameworks, tools, and techniques we’ve created to change people’s behavior — and scientifically measure that our material works.
															- As Email Marketing Analyst, you’ll be accountable for the production and delivery of all our emails to subscribers.  You’ll advise our growth and editorial teams on email marketing strategies to help them achieve lead and sales objectives.  You’ll be responsible for managing and executing the email calendar, and ensure quality assurance and deployment approval.  You’ll provide executive level reporting and dashboards to analyze the performance of our emails and make recommendations for continuous optimization and testing.
															- This is rare, backstage pass into IWT and an opportunity to work closely with all our core teams.
															- We are a 100% virtual company - you can work from anywhere in the country.
															- Responsibilities:
																- Primary responsibility for executing a highly complex editorial calendar, from initial concept through performance tracking of email campaigns.  Perform formatting, testing, release and quality control of all emails.
																- Collect necessary information and feedback from cross-functional teams to produce campaigns thoroughly, accurately, and on-time
																- Test and validate email campaigns to ensure accuracy of content and links
																- Monitor email deliverability and ensure proper list hygiene, follow email marketing best practices including CAN-SPAM compliance, and maintain a high rate of deliverability. Propose expanded or improved capabilities of email best practices.
																- Execute email split tests, analyze results, and recommend iterative split tests
																- Perform analysis and build dashboards to report on email and campaign performance on a weekly, monthly and annual basis
																- Analyze performance metrics of deployed campaigns and circulate feedback to drive learning and optimization of future messages
																- Deliver executive email performance report package on a weekly basis
																- QA and publish regular blog posts
																- Create and prepare ad hoc reports and trend analyses
																- Respond to customer deliverability issues and troubleshoot resolution
																- Write and maintain SOPs to maintain the integrity and smooth execution of all email production activities
															- Desired Skills and Experience:
																- Bachelor’s Degree - Marketing, Business Administration, Mathematics, Science or other Quantitative field
																- 2-3 years experience with email marketing and production (including complex list segmentation)
																- 2-3 years experience creating and sending large scale email campaigns through ESP systems (prior experience with Maropost strongly preferred)
																- 2-3 years experience with Basic HTML, CSS and Wordpress
																- 2-3 years experience with online and email analytics and ESP reporting
																- Experience with A/B split testing and experimental designs
																- Working knowledge of Google Apps, MS Office products, Basecamp and other leading productivity applications
																- Strong analytic and quantitative skills - ability to do data extracts and manipulation using Excel and other tools
																- Multi-tasker, able to effectively prioritize and execute multiple projects at the same time
																- Self-starter / self-motivated, able to work independently with minimal oversight
																- Highly organized with exceptional attention to detail and able to provide oversight of copywriting quality
																- Must be able to perform in a fast paced, iterative, deadline driven environment and be willing to “do what it takes”
																- Able to maintain a consistent daily work schedule to align with email send and peak periods
																- Able to work remotely (with your own phone, laptop and high speed internet access in place)
																- Superlative written and verbal communication skills - essential for working in a remote environment
																- Must reside in the U.S.

														- Marketing Automation Manager
															- Are you an experienced direct marketer with a proven track record of growing revenue via email marketing?
															- Are you looking for a real opportunity to apply your marketing automation muscles to solve really complex but rewarding challenges? To join an all-star team?
															- If so, you may be a great fit for our team.  Read on.
															- We produce amazing content at IWT.  Get rid of your debt, find your dream job, earn income on the side, start your first business, get healthy and fit. We provide people with the daily tools and systems for how to live a Rich Life. Talk to any of our students.
															- The Marketing Automation Manager is ultimately responsible for ensuring that our content gets out to subscribers and customers.  The Marketing Automation Manager creates, optimizes and tracks high performance email campaigns and automated marketing funnels that drive conversions and grow the lifetime value of our subscribers.
															- You’ll Be the Best in the Industry
															- We’re using cutting-edge practices and setting new standards in the industry for data-driven marketing. We want to deeply understand what drives conversions and repeat purchase. Which variables matter? How do we persuade people to take action? Which campaigns drive the most value for the business?
															- We have a simple goal on the Marketing Automation team: push the edges more than anyone else in the industry. You'll be in the middle of everything. You'll see how the entire business works and it'll be your ideas that will help us make it even more effective.
															- We operate at a mission critical pace with a high degree of complexity.  You must be able to solve problems quickly with creative and sound solutions.  Fast cycle testing, iteration and feedback loops.  You’ll learn faster than any of your peers in our environment - guaranteed.
															- We are a 100% virtual company - you can work from anywhere in the country
															- Responsibilities:
																- Increase revenues and optimize conversions from our email marketing campaigns
																- Design, build and QA new email campaigns to support our launches and promotions, partnering with Editorial and Growth teams to ensure proper campaign flow, tagging, setup and segmentation
																- Create event- and behavior-based funnels (automated, multi-step and multi channel ie. email, retargeting, SMS, etc) using advanced logic and Sixth Division methodologies
																- Manage and optimize our batch / broadcast email sends and automated email marketing campaigns
																- Track and analyze campaign email and conversion metrics and other performance KPIs
																- Develop A/B test plans to test subject lines, content, sales copy, offers and more
																- Establish standard procedures for, and ensure company-wide adherence to, campaign development, testing, tagging, UTM parameters, tracking and reporting
															- What We're Looking For:
																- BA/BS - Marketing, Business Administration, Mathematics, Science or Quantitative field preferred
																- Proven track record in email marketing and marketing automation (minimum 4 yrs experience, length of time matters less than your ability to produce results)
																- Experience in working with large subscriber databases (250K+) with complex segmentation strongly preferred
																- Direct to consumer, direct response email marketing experience
																- Expert at designing and building highly complex but efficient logic-driven campaigns and automation sequences
																- Coding skills (HTML, CSS, Javascript) and knowledge of Wordpress a plus
																- Advanced database marketing skills (CRM, segmentation, targeting)
																- Data manipulation and analysis (Microsoft Excel, SQL, v-lookups, pivot tables, complex formulas, etc)
																- Hands-on experience with InfusionSoft and/or other leading CRM / Marketing automation systems
																- Training in InfusionSoft Sixth Division methodology a strong plus
																- Excellent analytical skills including campaign performance and trends and subscriber and customer conversion metrics
																- Able to manage multiple campaigns with different resources, priorities and timelines
															- Is this you?
																- Analytical - clearly define problems, identify trends, patterns, and create solutions
																- Optimizer - someone who can’t help but improve and optimize everything
																- Builder - you create processes and leverage automation for scale
																- Highly driven - relentless, passionate, self-starter, super determined
																- High energy - we work hard and play hard
