																	- Virtual Staff Recruitment
																	  collapsed:: true
																		- http://www.virtualstaffrecruit.com/
																		- VSR is one of the leading Outsourcing Company in Indonesia and Southeast Asia.
																		- VSR focus through its expanded business plan includes seeking partners to develop and market its business in field of work that required select skills.
															- Assistants
															  collapsed:: true
																- i.e. personal assistants, research assistants
																- General Virtual Assistants
																  collapsed:: true
																	- https://www.timesvr.com http://www.asksunday.com/ http://www.brickworkindia.com/ https://www.getfriday.com
																	- http://www.virtualstaffrecruit.com/
																	  collapsed:: true
																		- http://www.virtualstaffrecruit.com/
																		- VSR is one of the leading Outsourcing Company in Indonesia and Southeast Asia.
																		- VSR focus through its expanded business plan includes seeking partners to develop and market its business in field of work that required select skills.
													- Create an Advert for Job Role
													  collapsed:: true
														- Use the Operations Manual for this working role to give a summary of the job duties.
														- Some of our previous job adverts also can be used again as a template and are available here: http://siva-id.jobstreet.com/applications/listJobs.asp?JGRP=&&Orig=&Submit=1
												- Prescreen:
												  collapsed:: true
													- After 4 days send everyone the psychometrics tests
													  collapsed:: true
														- Overview
														  collapsed:: true
															- Description: In this stage candidates will take psychometric tests on their intelligence and personality.
															- Benefit: You will be able to quickly analyse the best suited people before even interviewing them.
														- Make a new Psychometrics test doc
														- Copy the Email Template and change parts in bold
														  collapsed:: true
															- Note:
															- 1) Email them all from your work gmail account.
															- 2) BCC everyone so no-one is in the 'To:' field. This is to avoid privacy complaints from everyone's emails being shared.
															- --------------------------------
															- Email Subject:
															- Congratulations You Have Passed the Prescreening Stage
															- Email Body:
															- In your application for the position of [Job Role] at CV Bsure, I would like to congratulate you on passing the prescreening stage.
															- I now invite you to continue the recruitment process by completing a few short psychometric tests. Please see attached link below which contains the links for these online tests.
															- [add URL hyperlink for this job role's psychometric test form]
															- Please note that you have to report the result by filling in your CBS scores into Google forms. You might take note of your scores, re-open the Google form link above, and put the CBS scores on blank form based on section of the test e.g. Reasoning Ability: 43.
															- When you are finished, click send form. Please also remember that once you are finished you must send the screenshot of your result to [enter your staff gmail address] to verify the result of your tests.
															- Those tests will take only less than 1 hour, and please note that you have to finish and report the result before [Day, Month DD, Year at 12:00 AM (give them between 2-5 days)].
															- PS:
															- These links will help you to take the screenshots.
															- Windows: http://www.wikihow.com/Take-a-Screenshot-in-Microsoft-Windows
															- Mac: http://www.wikihow.com/Take-a-Screenshot-With-a-Macbook
															- Warmest regards,
															- [Your name]
															- Corporate Recruitment - Human Resource Department
															- Besure, LLC. (CV. Bsure)
															- Jl. Terusan Surya Sumantri Kav. 11A Bandung
															- Phone: +62 22 8780 1148
														- Await email replies and highlight GREEN the name on the (Responses) sheet to keep track of whether you've received the correct CBS screenshots
													- Prescreen to a top 20
													  collapsed:: true
														- Example of complete Candidate Summary http://bit.ly/12gaekY
														- First make a copy of the Candidate Summary TEMPLATE http://bit.ly/1u9NUzY
														- Copy of TEMPLATE - Candidate Summary - ROLE MO/YR:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Only include the top 20 candidates with highest Reasoning Ability score on psychometric tests
															- Order them from 1-20 in order of years of experience, with the least experienced at the top (but N/A at the bottom)
														- Filling in the columns:
														  collapsed:: true
															- See examples for help
															  collapsed:: true
																- At the bottom of the template below the candidate #20 row is a row with an example candidate.
																- You can also see an example of completed Candidate Summary here http://bit.ly/12gaekY (Note: This is a sheet only it's been shortlisted down to 6 candidates, a later step)
															- Give them a letter score for each part of Skillset
															  collapsed:: true
																- From our Operations Manual for the work role + job description you should have a good idea of what skills we're looking for, and what level of expertise is expected.
																- Letter scores:
																  collapsed:: true
																	- X - Not Enough / No (they have no idea about how to do it)
																	- C - Enough (they know the basics)
																	- B - Good / Yes (they are somewhat skilled)
																	- A - Excellent (they are very experienced in the skill)
																	- N/A - Not Stated (they haven't mentioned their level of expertise)
																- After all their skills have scores:
																  collapsed:: true
																	- CTRL + click all of the letters which are the same e.g. X
																	- Once you've hightlighted them all, change the text colour e.g. red.
														- Copy the psychometric scores using transpose
														  collapsed:: true
															- Copy a candidate's scores from openess -> verbal intelligence
															- Open up the Candidate Summary:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Right-click their row underneath the 'Openness' column > Paste > Paste special > Paste transpore
												- Shortlist:
												  collapsed:: true
													- Analyse the top 20 to shortlist the 6 final interview candidates
													  collapsed:: true
														- Put candidates through progressively higher standards of screening criteria until only 6 candidates are left to be interviewed.
														- Note:
														  collapsed:: true
															- 1) If it at any level too many candidates are accepted (e.g. it's Level 4, there's already 3 accepted and 5 more get accepted), then evaluate those accepted at the current level (i.e. the 5) with the higher levels of criteria (in order).
															- 2) Highlight the candidate's name and fill it in red if they're rejected at any stage. If they're accepted, fit it in green. Otherwise leave it blank and they will be moved to the 'Kept for Reference' section on Jobstreet.
															- 3) In the Candidate Summary sheet put a comment on the candidate's name if they were rejected. In this comment state at which level they were rejected and why.
														- 12 Levels of Analysis
														  collapsed:: true
															- Level 1:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Rejected if their Openness OR Conscientiousness OR Agreeableness is below 2
																- OR Neuroticism above 4
																- OR JELA Score below 20
																- OR CELA Score below 12
															- Level 2:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Accepted if their portfolio of past work shows they can do exactly what we're looking for
															- Level 3:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Accepted if their Reasoning Ability is above 46 (top 10%).
															- Level 4:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Accepted if their Reasoning Ability is above 44 (top 20%).
															- Level 5:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Accepted if their Reasoning Ability is above 42 (top 30%).
															- Level 6:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Rejected if their Reasoning Ability is below 39 (top 60%).
															- Level 7:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Rejected if their Verbal Intelligence is below 39 (top 60%).
															- Level 8:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Rejected if their Short-Term Memory is below 39 (top 60%).
															- Level 9:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Rejected if their Neuroticism is above 3.
															- Level 10:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Accepted if their Openness is above 4.
															- Level 11:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Accepted if their Conscientiousness is above 4.5.
															- Level 12:
															  collapsed:: true
																- Look for the remaining candidate with the highest Reasoning Ability score.
													- Schedule interviews - 2 per day at 11am and 2pm slots
													  collapsed:: true
														- Copy the Email Template and change parts in brackets.
														- If you have called the applicants and they agree with the schedule you offer
														  collapsed:: true
															- Email Template 1
															  collapsed:: true
																- Note: Email them all from your work gmail account.
																- Email Subject:
																- Invitation to Interview
																- Email Body:
																- Dear [Applicant name],
																- Congratulations!
																- As a result of your application for the position of [Job Role], I would like to invite you to attend an interview on [Month DD, at 00:00 PM/AM] at our office in Jl. Ters. Surya Sumantri Kav. 11A Bandung (In front of Transline). We will tell you a bit more about the position and get to know you better.
																- You will have an interview with the General Manager, Aaron Sollesse. The interview will last in less than 1 hour. Please bring two references from your past employer, so we can contact them if needed. Also bring your portfolio and have ready any other documents which demonstrate your skillset.
																- Warm regards,
																- [Your Name]
																- Corporate Recruitment - Human Resources
																- Besure, LLC. (CV. Bsure)
																- Jl. Ters. Surya Sumantri Kav. 11A
																- Bandung, Indonesia
																- +6222 8780-1148
																- [your email address]
														- If you haven't called the applicants or they can't be called by phone
														  collapsed:: true
															- Email 2
															  collapsed:: true
																- Note: Email them all from your work gmail account.
																- Email Subject:
																- Invitation to Interview
																- Email Body:
																- Dear [Applicant name],
																- Congratulations!
																- As a result of your application for the position of [Job Role], I would like to invite you to attend an interview at our office in Jl. Ters. Surya Sumantri Kav. 11A Bandung (In front of Transline). We will tell you a bit more about the position and get to know you better.
																- Please let me know which of the following times work for you, and I can send over a confirmation.
																- 1. [Day, Month DD, YYYY] - 00:00 PM/AM
																- 2. [Day, Month DD, YYYY] - 00:00 PM/AM
																- 3. [Day, Month DD, YYYY] - 00:00 PM/AM
																- Make sure on the day of the interview to bring your portfolio and have ready any other documents which demonstrate your skillset.
																- Warm regards,
																- [Your name]
																- Corporate Recruitment - Human Resources
																- Besure, LLC. (CV. Bsure)
																- Jl. Ters. Surya Sumantri Kav. 11A
																- Bandung, Indonesia
																- +6222 8780-1148
																- [your email address]
													- When you receive a response
													  collapsed:: true
														- Add a comment below and make sure that the interviewer is a Follower of this task
												- Interview stage:
												  collapsed:: true
													- Interview applicants
													  collapsed:: true
														- Candidate has arrived:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Greet them, tell them the name of their interviewer and to take a seat in the front lobby whilst you inform the interviewer of their arrival
															- Interviewer open up docs for reference throughout the interview
															  collapsed:: true
																- This Interview SOP
																- Their Operations Manual
																- Candidate Summary sheet
																- Their job application e.g. http://siva-id.jobstreet.com/applications/
														- Turn on the voice recorder
														  collapsed:: true
															- Explain to the candidate that you will be voice recording the interview to transcribe it for later analysis
														- Read the questions from your phone (Asana app or webbrowser) or note them down before the interview
														- Interviews must be conducted in English to assess their fluency
														- At start:
														  collapsed:: true
															- 1-2 minutes of fluff chat to get them comfortable
															  collapsed:: true
																- Ask them where they're from
																- How long it took them to get here
																- And questions about things you already know from their job application e.g. their educational background
															- Describe work role
															  collapsed:: true
																- First off, let me tell you a bit more about our company. We are a fast-growing small business currently with a team of X (number of employees), mostly Indonesians.
																- We operate in the pharmaceuticals industry, 3D scanning and more but you will primarily aid us as a [working role title].
																- This will involve tasks such [SOPs from their Operations Manual] on a regular basis.
																- As an example, if you are accepted onto our team you will first be working on [list a a few of the highest priority tasks for that working role].
																- Does this sound like something you can handle?
															- Describe company - brief general operating principles
															  collapsed:: true
																- Our business is very different from others in 3 important ways.
																- 1) Constantly Evolving, Not Routine
																  collapsed:: true
																	- For one, as I've just mentioned we operate in a number of wildly different industries. This means that you'll be working on something different often so there will no chance of getting bored.
																	- This is a rapidly-evolving workplace thus it's fast-paced and the duties required of you may change from day-to-day. You'll be learning about new industries and skills on a regular basis so ongoing training is a big part of our organisation.
																	- If you prefer doing the same mindless task day-in and day-out then this isn't the right company for you. If however, you like a challenge and a place to use your full creative potential, then you'll fit right in.
																- 2) Systematic to Improve Quality and Be Efficient
																  collapsed:: true
																	- Another big difference is that we are very systematic. Every task that we have to repeat is extensively written down in a document template. This means that whenever something needs to be done like payroll, researching a location or putting together a project plan, there is a checklist document for how to do it.
																	- This doesn't mean that all your work will be easy because you know exactly what to do however. Your duty will be to constantly improve the documentation you use so that others can benefit from it.
																	- And the reason why we do this is because it results in high quality of work by ensuring nothing gets accidentally forgotten.
																	- It also causes high-quality work by freeing your mental resources to be more creative. When we make decisions it uses up our limited mental energy. By having a checklist for recurring tasks, you don't have to think too hard to make sure you didn't miss any steps out which means you can spend your precious mental resources on the more creative aspects of your work.
																- 3) Hands-Off type of Company
																  collapsed:: true
																	- Although we have extensive training, communication and support systems, we're a very hands-off type of company. You won't have anyone watching you over your shoulder to make sure you're doing your work.
																	- Most of our business communication instead occurs through our project management software, where you can get an overview of all tasks and can communicate with others.
																	- We hire talented people, and then get out of their way so they can do their best work. This is not a place full of policies and bureaucracy nor do we do hand-holding here.
																	- We'll give you all the resources, support and guidance you need, but you'll often be pushing new boundaries with high expectations for your work.
																	- The intention is to simply give you an environment where you can live up to your full potential and it's up to you to sink or swim.
																- Conclusion:
																  collapsed:: true
																	- Those are the 3 main differences - 1) Constantly evolving work, 2) Systematic procedures and 3) Hands-off environment
																	- This isn't suited for every type of person. What are your thoughts on how you would do in an environment like this?
														- Interview Questions:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Enough talking from me. I want to hear about you.
															- Tell me about yourself - what do you in your spare time? Tell me about your interests and hobbies
															- What do you like about this particular work role?
															- What did you like and dislike about your previous job?
															- Why are you leaving your current job?
															- Ask questions related to their expertise + see portfolio/docs
															  collapsed:: true
																- For example if the job role requires knowing a coding language ask them to explain what they can code, what coding languages they know, what they've done in the past etc
																- Basically try to gauge how much expertise they actually have.
															- Where do you see yourself in 5 years? / Describe your career goals
															- What skills would you like to learn in the next year?
															- What are your strengths?
															- What are your weaknesses?
															- What do you expect from a supervisor?
															- Tell me why you want to work here
															- What can you contribute towards this company?
															- If the people who know you were asked why you should be hired, what would they say?
														- Concluding:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Have you got any questions for me about this work role or company?
															- Handshake, tell them we'll be in touch and wish them a good day
														- After the interview:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Fill in the Candidate Summary sheet post-interview section
															- Transcribe their responses into the Interview Summary Sheet
														- Note: change AS much as possible of the explaining the business style and personality suitability from telling to questions in order to ascertain more accurately. (start with changing to easy either/or type questions, then work to morph them into open-ending questions. the goal is to not give them any hints as to what responses are "ideal"

														- On behalf of Besure, LLC. I am pleased to offer you the position of [Work Role]. In this role you will report to [Manager's name], [Manager's work title]. Your start date will be [Month, Day, Year] at [Time].

													  collapsed:: true
														- Decision based on A,B,C qualities compared across other position candidates - add this to maximum salary part - decided maximum when we see our potential candidates
														- Acceptance Meeting Preperations:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Compensation Discussion Diagram
															  collapsed:: true
															  Vertical scale with one axis - minimum to maximum allocated salary for work role
																- Note down
																  collapsed:: true
																	- 1) Maximum salary allocated for the position (decided when Job Acceptance Mail sent)
																	- 2) Our baseline salary (currently 3 million IDR for all job roles)
																	- 3) Previous Salary (actual received previous salary in similar past job role)
																	- 4) Pre-Interview Expected Salary (previous expected salary on their job application or interview)
														- New prospective employee has arrived:
														  collapsed:: true
															- First tell them they will go straight into starting tasks today, but first we have to handle the formalities, starting with compensation discussion
														- Compensation Discussion:
														  collapsed:: true
															- 5) What do you think is the minimum salary we're considering for this position?
															- 6) What do you think is the maximum salary we've allocated for this position?
															- Response
															  collapsed:: true
																- If they don't speak within a few seconds:
																  collapsed:: true
																	- I see that you have placed your expected salary in the (lower/higher) end of that range
																- If they do elaborate or justify their expected salary:
																  collapsed:: true
																	- If they give somewhat justifiable explanation, mental note to lean the higher end of their expected salary (but below maximum)
														- Break:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Okay. Are you thirsty? Can I get you some water?
															- Okay. Sit tight (then you leave the room and get them a water no matter their response)
															- (Set the water down on the table) We'll be back with you shortly, just sit tight.
														- Leave the room to decide the final figure:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Think about whether they adequately justified their requested placement on the salary range
															- Decide on the final salary figure and type it into the Job Agreement form for that job role, then print it out
														- NOTE - for next conversation upon entering room again:
														  collapsed:: true
															- If you've decided to give them below their expected salary then proceed to Probationary Period Discussion, then Salary Finalisation
															- If you've decided to give them above their expected salary then proceed to Salary Finalisation, then Probationary Period Explanation
														- Probationary Period Explanation:
														  collapsed:: true
															- Explain that we have a 3 month probationary period where we will closely monitor your work and analyse your fit within our team
															- You will receive frequent feedback via monthly reviews leading up to your comprehensive performance evaluation after 3 months
															- If you are deemed to falling short of expectations was result in the end of our working relationship
															- If you are kept on then we'll discuss your contract extension and reevaluate your compensation in line with your performance
															- Explanation - This is to ensure that the staff we do retain are of the utmost quality
														- Salary Finalisation
														  collapsed:: true
															- After careful consideration we have settled on the figure of [salary number] per month
															- We certainly think that's more than fair and by the way our compensation package also includes Prudential health insurance, pension fund contributions and much more.
															- All of the benefits are outlined in our Besure, LLC Benefits Handbook.

										- How to Train a New Team Member
										  collapsed:: true
											- _Overview_
											  collapsed:: true
												- Description: This SOP will teach you how to train up a new team member so that they can effectively perform all SOPs within their working role.
												- Benefit: You will be able to train anyone new to our organisation how to effectively use all of our systems and be a productive contributing team member.
											- Training period - 3 months:
											  collapsed:: true
												- Create a copy of their job role Operation Manual
												- Assign it to yourself and set the deadline 1 month from the current date
												- With help from their Project Manager, create tasks for them based on each SOP and tell them to use the SOP for guidance. It doesn't matter if the task purpose is not real and results will not be used
												- If results are not up to expectations then update the SOP then tell them to refer to that specific section again for guidance
												- Repeat for every SOP
												- 3 month Trial period: Fire fast
												  collapsed:: true
													- Keeping mediocre performers in an entrepreneurial, growing business hurts everyone else’s chances to create a great living for a lot more people and offer incredible value to your market. In other words, you aren’t doing your employees any favors by keeping them in a job they aren’t good at.
												- When all training is done, inform the Project Manager
										- How to Manage Team Members
										  collapsed:: true
											- Overview
											  collapsed:: true
												- Description: This SOP will teach you
												- Benefit: You will be able to
											- How to Delegate
											  collapsed:: true
												- Golden Rule #1: Each delegated task must be both time-consuming_ and _well-defined
												- All tasks must have deadlines
												- Identify the few critical tasks that contribute most to income (80/20) and create deadlines in less than 5 days
												  collapsed:: true
													- Parkinson’s Law dictates that a task will swell in (perceived) importance and complexity in relation to the time allotted for its completion
												- If your team delivers work you're not satisfied with, update their Operations Manual with improved SOPs
												- Praise them - e.g. show appreciation for tasks completed with words of thanks, compliments and 'likes'
												- Help them - e.g. add followers to tasks and comment that they'll help assist them, use @ and URL links to direct them to useful resources etc
												- Delegation Review
												  collapsed:: true
													- Look over each of your team member's tasks. Are they appropriate tasks for their current Operations Manual? They should be operating at the highest level of their abilities. If not then tell them to hire an assistant (if they've currently got overdue tasks and other big tasks coming up which aren't on schedule for completion)
												- How to Choose Tasks to Assign
												  collapsed:: true
													- First look at vision for the projects you're responsible for
													- Then short term vision (next year)
													- Then project progression plan (which should be the short term vision manifested, which comes from the long term vision)
													- Look at what stuff you're still doing that is better suited to a different work role
													- Then stuff that others would do better than you
													- Stuff that is time consuming for you
													- Stuff you've put off for too long
											- Systemising
											  collapsed:: true
												- Never do yourself what you can pay others to do
												- [EDAD] First Eliminate > Delay > Automate > before trying to Delegate
												- Create streamlined 80/20 SOPs to teach people how to do work themselves, don't correct their mistakes personally
												- Encourage initiative to create SOPs - they're expected to create a checklist SOP as they do the work, setting their own milestone due dates
												- Current employees:
												  collapsed:: true
													- Develop SOPs so they can work unassisted
													- Build initiative in team - spontaneously does new tasks and creates SOPs for it

													- Recurring Company Improvement

														- Make these subtasks for Hall of Fame, comments for passing reports etc. Rotate these internally with all staff and give attributions. Announcement-like? or as tutorial for how to do a report etc ? Maybe Hall of Fame type is best for tutorials (google doc - view on link, not original doc)

												- I need someone who I can trust and train to handle the website, interaction with customers, contacts, social media and who can do relationship marketing. This person must be in the US, in my area and English speaking. For this I always find kids straight out of college. I will pay that asset $30K/year.

													- see Asana OMs This is the Level 2 Operations Manual. --------------------------------------------------------- Create a copy of each SOP when you need to use it to complete a task and put it in your private Personal Project. (This is because more than one person will need to use the original SOP and these tasks are recurring., So you instead will be completing the checklist on the copy SOP.) Also make sure to remove the creator of the SOP as a follower whenever you create a copy. They don't need notifications in their Inbox for every task you complete. Handles one small business or project (directly manages a team of 1-10 people)

									- 2) Project Management
									  collapsed:: true
										- How to Train a Project Manager
										  collapsed:: true
											- For when hiring new Project Managers - they need their own workroom for new company.
											- Invite all new hires to the Dojo then move them onto another Workspac
											- e.g. CNC, 3D scanning etc
										- How to Create a PPP
										  collapsed:: true
											- _Overview_
											  collapsed:: true
												- Description:
												  collapsed:: true
													- This will teach you how to create Project Progression Plans (PPPs), which are similar to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
												- Benefit:
												  collapsed:: true
													- You will be able to create detailed and accurate PPPs so that projects can be effectively prioritised, adequate resources allocated, be completed quickly and live up to expectations of the project outcome vision.
											- Definition & Examples
											  collapsed:: true
												- Project Progression Plans are very similar to Standard Operating Procedures, the difference is that PPPs are our best guesses of all the steps in the SOP.
												  collapsed:: true
													- This is because a SOP is the detailed checklist of how we do a particular task in the present. A PPP is a detailed checklist of how we will complete a particular project in the future.
													- This means PPPs are less accurate because they usually can't be tested until we actually get to that stage of the project.
													- Though with thorough research, we can effectively plan out every major milestone linearly so that we can gauge just how much progress we've made and what is left to be completed.
												- Examples of past PPPs:
												  collapsed:: true
													- Notes about these examples:
													  collapsed:: true
														- Previous examples may use a different format than the current preferred format
														- Focus on how the underlying aspects of these PPPs e.g. level of detail, linear order, expected time til completion, assigning each subtask, suggestions etc.
													- Building a 3D Scanner
													  collapsed:: true
														- Analyse Project
														- Create 3D Design
														  collapsed:: true
															- Create 3D Design
															- Revised 3D Design (approval from the Boss)
														- Bill Of Material and Support Parts :
														  collapsed:: true
															- Bill Of Material and Support Parts
															- Revised BOM (approval from the Boss)
														- Booking Parts :
														  collapsed:: true
															- LED Stripe
															- LED Stripe Test to Power Suply 12V (Test Ok)
													- Upgrading to a Pharmaceuticals Company
													- Pharmaceuticals Approval
											- **SOP**
											  collapsed:: true
												- Research and add steps
												  collapsed:: true
													- Research and make notes on all steps along the entire progression
													- Add in as much detail as possible prior to starting the project. Ideally to SOP-level of detail. See ✓ How to Create a SOP
												- Major Milestones
												  collapsed:: true
													- Add due dates for all major milestones, to be able to judge if we're working on schedule.
													- At least 3 due dates, near beginning+middle+end
												- Mini Milestones
												  collapsed:: true
													- Don't need due dates on every subtask necessarily but:
													  collapsed:: true
														- Create at least one due date per week (break down each task into a subtask if necessary)
														- Assign these mini-milestones to each relevant person
												- Assign all subtasks if more than 1 person responsible for completing tasks
												  collapsed:: true
													- This is to ensure everyone knows what they are personally responsible for
													- Assign each subtask to each relevant person
													- OR - If working role is not assigned yet:
													  collapsed:: true
														- Then note on the subtask description which working role is responsible for completing this task
												- Add notes regarding all other potential obstacles
												  collapsed:: true
													- Make notes on where one task needs to wait for another to be complete (either put them as a subtask within the other or link using Asana @ Hypertext.
													- And any other obstacles that may arise, make suggestions in advance for preparation.
												- Assign main parent task will be assigned to the Project Manager (if not designated, then to yourself for the moment) and set the due date for 1 year from today
												- When finished:
												  collapsed:: true
													- Add your Manager as a follower to review the project and make a comment on the parent task
									- 3) Business Developer
									  collapsed:: true
									  AKA growth hacker / marketing director
										- _Intro_
										  collapsed:: true
											- Mainly for implementing Greg's new ideas
											- i.e. research + reports (has their own research assistant too)
											- Responsible for hiring new employees & project managers
											- identifies business needs, hires permanent staff and helps develop operation manuals.
											- reports to evaluate business needs (monthly / weekly surveys of team?)
										- Vision (Medium-term)
										  collapsed:: true
											- You must keep in mind the medium-term vision (up to 5 years) of what the business will look like at that stage.
											- This will inform all actions and decision-making so that the vision will be achieved in that time period.
										- One novel Project Manager pipeline:
										  collapsed:: true
											- Internships for Westerners
											  collapsed:: true
												- Process:
												  collapsed:: true
													- How to decide when to look for a new intern
													  collapsed:: true
														- Interns generally 3-6 months to hire from the point of the marketing message so need to plan ahead and already have good candidates always in the pipeline
														- Bring on new interns when cash flow is good enough to support it e.g. At this point in mid December I'd say an extra $5k per month may warrant a new intern
														- necessary if business growth is slow due to not enough self motivated entrepreneurs in the business to execute all business projects
													- Application process for interns
													  collapsed:: true
													  Example copy blog post
														- Location - Remote. Our entire team is remote, and we stay in close contact via online channels like Basecamp, Skype, and email. We also meet in person every few months! We think the flexible schedule is a huge bonus, but you should be comfortable working for yourself in your own location.
														- PayWe believe in highly competitive compensation for top performers
														- Imagine this. You’re working from home. Or a coffee shop. Or Bermuda. You’re researching the recent rise in autism in the population. Or you’re writing about that time you were in a car accident and how it changed your perspective on death. Or you’re creating a bad ass picture of a dragon torching a city of townspeople as a metaphor for how our primal nature corrupts society.
														- And thousands of people are going to see your work. Thousands are going to be touched by it and be better people for it.
														- Oh, and you’re getting paid to do it. Full-time. And your work schedule is flexible. And you can work from anywhere. And your boss is like, the coolest boss in the whole world (ahem).
														- Yeah, that would be pretty awesome…
														- I’m hiring. Most people don’t realize this, but underneath this sleek-looking blog and these potty-mouthed articles there’s an entire publishing apparatus — two people full-time and a number of contractors and freelancers. It’s like, a legit business and that business has seen a lot of growth the past two years.
														- There’s a lot in the works behind-the-scenes right now. The new book is just one of them. The book will be coming out this summer, but on the back end of that, this site will be scaled in a way that I’m not sure any blog has ever been scaled before. More articles. More quality. More creativity. More horsepower. More everything.
														- And to do that I’ve decided that I need to hire two people.
														- The Two Positions
														- To take the site to the next level I will be looking for two people to work closely with me and collaborate on a variety of content. The two positions are: a content curator, and a digital artist.
														- What I’m Looking For in a Candidate
														- Interested in one (or both) of the jobs? Then the next question is how do we decide if you’re a good fit.
														- The following are absolute musts — they’re non-negotiable:
														- 1. You must be a fan of this site and of my work. I know that sounds egotistical, but you’re going to be working for me and supporting my content. If you have no idea about who I am or what I do, then you should read this, this, this and this. If you love the ideas presented in those articles (not like, love) and identify with them big-time, then sweet, hop aboard! If not, well, then it’s just downhill from here.
														- 2. You must be familiar with internet technologies and various internet platforms. This is absolutely non-negotiable. That means you must know and be comfortable with: MS Office, Google Apps, Facebook/Twitter, Skype, and Paypal. WordPress experience is a huge plus. As is knowledge of SEO.
														- 3. You must be at least 22 years old.
														- 4. The content curator must be a fluent English-speaker and able to read and write English at a high academic level. The digital artist must have at least basic English skills.
														- 5. You must be interested for the right reasons. Last time I put up a job posting on my site, I got tons of applications from people where it was really clear that they weren’t nearly as interested in the position or the business as they were getting the opportunity to travel, or to learn skills to help them start their own business, or to be my super best friend, or to be internet famous or whatever.
														- Let’s be clear straight out: I’m looking for people who are in this for the long haul. I’m looking for people who are in love with the work they will be doing and believe strongly in the message this business is putting together. If you get this job, this is your full-time job. It’s not a gig. It’s not a stepping stone to something better. It’s not something cool to do until something better comes along. It’s part of your career and your life.
														- This business has averaged insane growth year over year and this is an opportunity to get in and be a part of it pretty early on. The readership of this site, as it stands, is huge. I’m in a unique position in that my blog is one of the only ones of this size online that is a) not part of some larger corporation and b) completely internally monetized — no advertisers, no affiliates, no publishers, no agents.
														- There’s real opportunity to create something new and take internet publishing in a new direction here. The same can easily be said with the self help industry. If that’s something that excites you and you can get behind, then let’s do this. If not, and you want this for some more superficial reason like you hate your job or you always wanted to live in Tahiti, then please, don’t waste my time or yours.
														- How to Apply
														- You may only apply for one position, so please choose the position that best suits your skills.
														- Send all applications to the email address: jobs (AT) markmanson.net
														- The subject line should read: “APPLICATION: [Your Name] – [Position You’re Applying For]”
														- Send a brief cover letter with the following: 1) Why you want this position and 2) What is the stupidest thing you’ve ever done and why. (Maximum 500 words)
														- Attach your resume/CV.
														- Content Curator candidates: Attach a DOCX file with the answer to the following: “What are your five favorite books? Why is each one your favorite book?” (Minimum 500 words; Maximum 1,000 words)
														- Digital artist candidates: Include your portfolio. You will be judged almost entirely based on your portfolio.
														- The Application Process
														- If applying, please read below carefully. The application process has three phases.
														- Phase 1: All applications are due by Wednesday, April 16th, 11:59 EST. Any applications received after that will be deleted without being opened.
														- Phase 2: Candidates who seem qualified for the positions will be notified no later than Sunday, April 20th. They will then be invited to participate in phase two of the selection process. Phase two will consist of a few short-term assignments that are similar to the work the jobs entail. These assignments will be judged for both quality and turnaround time. Since this is a blog that will be updated regularly, it will be expected that you can produce high quality work quickly and consistently.
														- Phase 3: Phase three will be a Skype interview (if necessary) for any remaining candidates who performed well in Phase two. Job offers will then be made over Skype. There may even be balloons…. and a clown or two.
														- Employment will begin on Monday, May 19th.
														- Other Important Information
														- There are some important details that we should get out of the way so you know exactly what you’re getting yourself into, and I don’t find myself answering the same questions over and over and over again.
														- These positions are likely to be highly competitive. My last job opening received over 70 applications and that website had a fraction of the readership this one has. When you send your application in, please add the letter ‘X’ to the bottom of your email to be considered. Yes, that is exactly what it sounds like, don’t bother asking me why.
														- Once hired, there will be a six-week probationary period from May 19th until July 1st. During this time, candidates will receive $375 per week (paid via Paypal). The probationary period is to make sure we are a good fit and you are reliable and actually like the job. If, for some reason, one of us decides that it is not going well, we can end the arrangement and I will hire one of the other successful candidates from phase three.
														- Beginning July 1st, if all is well and good, full employment will start with a starting salary of $3000 per month.
														- You will be paid as an independent contractor, so you will need to square away all of the tax/bank stuff before you start.
														- Applicants do not need to be from the US.
														- And although this was a men’s site and much of the content on the site is still directed at hetero men, all future content will be gender neutral. Therefore women are encouraged to apply.
														- Please leave any questions in the comments section so I can answer them publicly.>
													- Interviewing Western Interns

														- Which business do you find yourself most excited about?

														- What other questions do you have about what life and the businesses are like out here in Indonesia?
														- When do you plan on making a decision, and when's the earliest you'd be able to come Indonesia?

															- (2014-09-27 23:43) myself: Well, it will take some time to explain all that I've got going on here, but here is one project we are starting now:

															- (2014-09-27 23:43) myself: It's 3-d scanning. I already have a 3-d printer and we built our own 3 axis cnc wood cutting router
															- (2014-09-27 23:44) myself: But there are quite a number of other projects in different industries
															- (2014-09-27 23:45) myself: including pharmaceutical sales, epoxy-granite flooring and couter tops, and especially we want to get a modelling agency and camwhore business thriving
															- (2014-09-27 23:46) myself: Oh, and I've rented a villa in bali for a year, and put a deposit down on another, and plan to rent a parcel of land, and then develop that land with some bungaloes.
															- (2014-09-27 23:46) myself: We'll get the wood cheap as I build a portable bandsaw mill.
															- (2014-09-27 23:46) myself: Built. Actually it's in the latter stages of construction right now.
															- (2014-09-27 23:47) Aaron Texas: Ohh man all of these things sound incredible.
															- (2014-09-27 23:47) myself: ya. It's so much fun. I'd never retire.
															- (2014-09-27 23:48) myself: Some of our projects we'll want to put on kickstarter or similar
															- (2014-09-27 23:48) myself: Some will be for export, some for the domestic market
															- (2014-09-27 23:49) myself: I'll be making a factory for cnc carvings and use the cheap 3d modellers here for exporting custom carvings
															- (2014-09-27 23:49) myself: and we will of course also make and sell various cnc equipment
															- (2014-09-27 23:49) myself: Our first machine works fine, and I've ordered parts for the second. I plan to collude with a local university to R and D and build other machines
															- (2014-09-27 23:50) myself: And I've already tested out camwhoreing with two models, and on the best days they can earn quite a lot. So that's going to be a big focus
														- 3 month trial
														  collapsed:: true
															- (10:43:58) myself: Awesome. We think that a 3 month trial period where both parties assess how it's been like working together would be the best plan of action. Since this is going to be a long-term venture, only by working together for a while would we each be able to assess the chemistry and see if there's a match, and if either don't think the partnership should move forward then we can stop there with no hard feelings.
															- (10:46:58) myself: In terms of responsibilities, I'm very interested to see your capabilities with systemising our business processes across the board and it would make sense to have you be the primary manager for several businesses
															- (10:48:05) myself: I'm not sure which businesses most interest you but one of our related ones to the CNC has huge potential for growth and systemisation so I think you would be suited well there
															- (10:49:29) James Skinner: I am a big fan of trial periods myself. Removes a lot of risk for both parties.
															- (10:52:05) myself: [delayed] In terms of what we're offering, all lifestyle expenses would be paid for (accomodation/food/phone etc), you'd be living a fun ass lifestyle in Bali/Bandung during for the first 3 months with us and meeting other cool like-minded guys, assuming you are an effective manager then there would be profit sharing after and of course you're learning how to be a successful entrepreneur and all the benefits that entails
												- Flight booked:
												  collapsed:: true
													- Travel Prep Advice
													  collapsed:: true
														- Btw some info for Bali:
														- You don't need malaria tablets (touch wood), very very low incidence and no-one uses it. Plus doxycycline is an antibiotic so will fuck up your gut bacteria
														- Get a Microfibre towel for the beach. Lightweight, compact and many of them block UV Rays
														- You'll be riding bikes out here which is fun as hell
														- Get pocket-size Aloksaks or any other waterproof bags and you can bring your phone or wallet into the water at the beach if desired
														- Travel underwear like ExOfficio is useful for saving space. 2 pairs is all you need, wash the one you just wore whilst in the shower and leave it to dry whilst you wear the fresh one.
														- You can find any meds you need here easily but I like to keep some on me just incase. Indigestion and diarrhoea tabs plus aspirin spring to mind
													- SIM cards & contact
													  collapsed:: true
														- I just wanted to ask you what is your mobile phone number?
														- It should be fine to contact you via that number when you land but as headache-prevention you can always reach us via the messaging app 'LINE' if you have a smartphone (even if you decide to get a SIM card at the airport we can still contact you via the same account on this app, unlike WhatsApp).
														- You don't have to get a local SIM card as soon as you arrive, but if you want to then Telekomsel do a cheap pay-as-you-go prepaid SIM card called SIMpati which I used when I first arrived.
														- You can also get a Skype Go-To number which you call at any time, then type in any international number (mine is +62 812 20299785) and it will use your Skype credit to make the call much cheaper.
														- Maybe over the top planning haha but I know the worst feeling would be being in a foreign country with no contact to your hosts so this would be a worst-case scenario if your phone refuses to connect to a carrier here.
														- If there's anything else you want to know before you fly out let me know.

											- The business development executive may do any, or all, of the following: work with the existing products/services to find new channels and markets for distribution; research and recommend new products/services; explore and negotiate acquisitions of other companies; analyze and present joint venture concepts (working with other businesses or brands that may be synergistic); find ways to sell more products/services to new and existing customers by adjusting current systems and marketing approaches; explore and stay abreast of competition in an attempt to find and maintain a competitive edge.

										- How to Create an Organisational Chart
										  collapsed:: true
										  AKA Org hierarchy / org chart
											- _Overview_
											  collapsed:: true
												- Description
												  collapsed:: true
													- This SOP will teach you how to create an Organisational Chart, which is the diagram for all roles in a business.
												- Benefit
												  collapsed:: true
													- You will be able to identify the essential job roles for a working business.
											- First create a new task named '#PROJECT Organisation Hierarchy' and add your Manager as a follower
											- Create a Draw.io Diagram Organisation Chart
											  collapsed:: true
												- Examples
												  collapsed:: true
													- PMM
													  http://i.imgur.com/p7AR5ai.png
													- E-Myth example
													  http://i.imgur.com/8gwR4kF.png
												- These diagrams are for creating a simple overview of the finished state of a business.
												- Principles
												  collapsed:: true
													- Every position on the chart reports to one (and only one) manager. Giving two or more managers the power to direct the activities of one employee is an invitation to miscommunication and chaos.
													- Make sure you divide up the work according to what the business needs. Don't try to design a position to fit the particular talents of one individual. If (when) that person leaves, you'll have to start all over with a new chart because you won't be able to find a replacement.
													- Instead of using titles, designate positions in terms of the results they will obtain.
													- Once you have a working organization chart, the next step is to figure out how that chart is going to serve the future of the business. We refer to this as Double Vision: keeping in mind how the present is going to serve the future. As you build your business toward your Strategic Objective, think about how your organization chart needs to morph and evolve in order to serve that vision.
													-
											- collection of the Operations Manuals
											- http://blog.emyth.com/overcome-org-chart-writers-block-0
											- Create the Operations Manual for each working role
											  collapsed:: true
												- Why
												  collapsed:: true
													- What we mean is that a lot of business owners inadvertently create ‘systems’ that
													- completely depend on themselves, or their employees, without even realizing it.
													- For example, if your accountant Mary is the only one in your business who knows about
													- your cash flow and your key financial indicators—then Mary
													- is
													- your finance system. If
													- Mary goes, your system disappears.
													- But what if Mary wrote down her daily routine and all the information she knows to keep
													- your business running? Now, you’re suddenly creating a
													- documented system
													- . Think about
													- it this way: what would happen if Mary left? What would you need to do to ensure that the
													- business keeps running?
												- For existing working roles:
												  collapsed:: true
													- For every working role we have already developed an Operations Manual for, create a subtask named '#PROJECT #WORKINGROLE'
													- Change '#WORKINGROLE' to what the role title is and in the description use @ Hypertext to link to the current Operations Manual
												- For new working roles:
												  collapsed:: true
													- How to Create an Operations Manual
													  collapsed:: true
														- Start with multiple subtasks each with a basic description of all tasks they would be involved in.
														- Once you've listed all their possible tasks, turn these into as detailed SOPs as you possibly can (see ✓ How to Create a SOP).
														- Purpose
														  collapsed:: true
															- Boost Self-Efficacy:
														- Operations Manual TEMPLATE
														  collapsed:: true
														  This is a training program for all current and future tasks I want you to take care of. Think of it as your employee handbook or job role.
														  
														  Refer back to these whenever you need to complete a task. There should be a SOP to cover all of your recurring duties.
														  
														  [Your Working Roles]
														  Level 1 - Basic Training (link to SOP)
														  
														  [Your Manager] Aaron
															- Operations Manual Setup
															  Create a private Project:
															  
															  In the left pane press the '+' next to Projects to make a new project.
															  
															  Left-click to go into that project and click the bright blue '1 Member' button at the top of the middle pane then 'Make private'.
															  
															  At the top of this 'Operations Manual' task where it says 'No project' type in 'Personal Project'.
															  
															  You now have a private project called 'Personal Project' where you can easily access your Operations Manual from
												- After all Operations Manuals complete:
												  collapsed:: true
													- Write a comment on the Organisation Hierarchy parent task that the Operations Manuals are now complete

											- How to Create a Vision Statement (Short-term)
											  collapsed:: true
												- _Overview_
												  collapsed:: true
													- Description
													  collapsed:: true
														- This SOP will show you how to create the short-term vision goal (up to 1 year) of what the business will look like at that stage.
													- Benefit
													  collapsed:: true
														- You will be able to make better decisions in alignment with the vision throughout the project in order to achieve it in within the year. .
												- Create a copy of this task and move it to the 'PROJECT Organisation Hierarchy'
												- Now rename the copy ''PROJECT Short-term Vision'
												- ''PROJECT Short-term Vision':
												  collapsed:: true
													- Set the due date as 1 year from now and assign the task to yourself
													- Add notes in all empty subtasks below. Use the present tense (imagine you're talking like the business has just reached it's first year of operation)
													- When you're done filling in the empty subtasks, delete all sections: and subtasks ABOVE including this one
													- VISION STATEMENT:
													- Monthly profit in $:
													- Monthly profit in $ in another year's time:
													- Total number of team members:
													- Ideas for how to increase income + size of team:

										- Vision (Long-term)
										  collapsed:: true

										- How to Manage Multiple Projects
										  collapsed:: true
											- _Intro_
											  collapsed:: true
												- There are the businesses you will manage. This means sole responsibility for business growth will be with you ultimately which means you will have to hire, fire as well manage your teams and tasks so that they are effective and efficient.
												- Also there are other businesses you will merely oversee. This means ultimate responsibility lies with a different person, but you are to aid that person with business growth. This means that much less frequently you'll receive and give feedback on these businesses.
											- Regularly refer to 'The Vision' for each of these businesses
											  collapsed:: true
												- The Vision is the overview of what the finished business will look like according to the ✓ Chief Visionary Officer - Aaron.
											- NAME - Monthly Reports
											  collapsed:: true
												- Monthly Reports
												- The first thing you'll do is use Jing to copy an image of all Burndown Charts. You'll then briefly state what steps you are taking to ensure that the project stays on target for it's deadline.
												- state at least one thing that can be done to improve the business or speed of progress. (Maybe that should come from each stage including the RA?)
												- Monthly report should be many words but weekly can just be numbers, graphs and brief summaries of tactical next action (+ obstacles and recently completed tasks). Monthly report will get a Strategic Review
												- How can I assist you
											- How to Setup Weekly Reports
											  collapsed:: true
												- Weekly Reports eg Shintia - Weekly Reports
												- Purpose:
												- Gives me a reminder of what is happening within project so I can share that info with other managers
												- Increased accountability for project manager
												- Increases sense of personal responsibility for project to the project manager
												- Suitable for long-term projects (>2 months)
												- Google Sheet with questions for all businesses in one doc (so they can see what they wrote for last week in order to not write the same thing twice if confusd). Google sheet best to easily search past history. Asana comments can't easily search back. Google Doc also not as easy to go back
										- Project Manager job desc
										  collapsed:: true
											- enforcing timeboxes, and responding to the impediments people explicitly report
											- http://scrummasterchecklist.org/pdf/ScrumMaster_Checklist_12_unbranded.pdf
											- Chief Operating Officer
											  collapsed:: true
												- Intro
												  collapsed:: true
													- This is a key role for IWT. As our COO, you'll work directly with me to help build our team, plan our projects, and “keep all the trains running.” This position is unique in its scope: On one hand, this is a highly operational role, and you should have extensive experience hiring, firing, and managing multiple projects at once. On the other hand, you'll use your in-depth knowledge of the business to make strategic recommendations for staffing and projects.
													- If you're the kind of person who ends a conference call saying, “OK, what are the action items?” this role might be right for you. If your your friends joke around about how you have 50+ filters/folders to manage your email…we should talk. And if you can talk to an entrepreneur and identify key strategic recommendations (or oversights)…this role is for you.
													- In general, this is NOT a highly creative role. I'm looking for someone highly experienced in executing complex project plans, setting up reporting systems, and “making sure the trains run on time.”
													- We'll work together extensively, so a fun personality is a must.
													- If this role is right for you, I hope to hear from you!
													- - Ramit
												- Tasks and Responsibilities
												  collapsed:: true
													- •Manage a number of important processes including resource allocation, project prioritization, project reporting, and project and process management
													- •Take over the day to day processes and tasks from the CEO.
													- •Manage a staff of top performers. Identify areas of personal growth for each team member
													- •Identify holes in staffing and work with our internal recruiter to identify key hires
													- •Create a fun work environment for outstanding performers who want to be the best
													- •Identify areas where we can automate and scale our work and build systems and processes to minimize repeated work
													- •Build reporting systems for staff performance and project updates
												- Education and Experience
												  collapsed:: true
													- •Management experience required. You should have operational experience in hiring, firing, and building teams with measurable success
													- •The ideal candidate will have a mix of project management, operations, start-up experience, finance, strategy, and sales/ business development.
													- •Ability to assess business objectives, diagnose problems and understand high priority issues.
													- •Ability to clearly communicate objectives across a range of stakeholders and to articulate the rationale behind those goals.
													- •Results driven individual with a high level of attention to detail.
													- •Capable of managing multiple complex projects at the same time.

												- Daily Activities
												  collapsed:: true

													- Every morning (to get them excited to come to work)
													- e.g. tongue twisters, yoga balance,
													- E.g. Monday = Theatre warmup, Tuesday = body eg twister/stretches, Wednesday = pingpong (4 in a line on each side who only get one hit each), Thursday = poetry/singing/karoake,
													- Riddles, quick board games eg connect 4/
													- Each day a different positive mindstate e.g. playful, excited, mindfukness guided meditation
													- Educational days e.g. body, mind, habits, - and everyone can take turns e.g. Show & Tell workshops/seminars, possibly at lunch

													- Focus:
													  collapsed:: true
														- Early morning coffee
														- Focus tests
														  collapsed:: true
															- http://www.sleepdisordersflorida.com/pvt1.html
															- http://www.cogmed.com/working-memory-challenge
															- Use these periodically to check your focusing abilities
														- Meditate
														  collapsed:: true
															- Most traditional meditation techniques are simple to practice.
															- Practice is the word.
															- Here is a very simple instruction:
															- Set a timer for your desired length of meditation.
															- Sit upright on a chair, cushion or rolled up towel, with your back straight.
															- Close your eyes. Breathe through your nose.
															- As your breath rises and falls, bring your mind gently to the feeling of the air moving in and out around the tips of your nostrils. Keep your mind there.
															- As you do this, other thoughts and feelings will arise.
															- Maybe your foot itches. Maybe you've got something you want to do immediately after you get up, or two days from now and it keeps coming to mind. Doesn't matter.
															- Note these thoughts and feelings as they come up, try to avoid judging them as good or bad, just notice they happened and gently come back to your breath.
											- Team meetings and culture
											  collapsed:: true
												- Team meetings - though unnecessary time suck, distracting, uses huge amount of productive time (no of people * duration)
												- Daily 1 hour call with team leaders
												- Then group call with every single employee mentioning what they're working on today - on mute til their go
												- this is useful for catching mistakes early + builds team culture. + Skype open throughout day to stay connected
												- this CEO stopped offering 1 on 1 with clients to spend more time with team - training them up, building purpose, fun, etc
												- Get employees to make a quick Friday report for what they've done. What they need from me to get the next step done.
											- Linchpins?
											  collapsed:: true
												- Systemise for the lowest common denominator, but then hire managers or skilled workers for new elements eg virtual girlfriend, expanding into new markets, biz consultants etc. The danger is otherwise having average employees and customers
												- Cheap drones are better than linchpins when working with commodities, especially in highly competitive markets with little creativity or customer service needed. Linchpins better when ideas are needed on a moments notice, when it's high trust-based work with customers, when they need know deeply about the business history and values to be effective or when experts are needed on a constant rather than intermittent basis (like business consultants)
											- Desktop popups
											  collapsed:: true
												- https://www.google.com/search?q=popup+desktop+notification+on+employee+pcs&ie=utf-8&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:en-GB:official&client=firefox-a&channel=sb
												- Configure it for a certain time each day e.g. stretch, meditate, lunch time, daily report etc

										- My Personal:
										  collapsed:: true
											- Daily Reports Mastersheet
											  collapsed:: true
												- Every morning when you log-on to Asana your Inbox should have 'Daily Report completed' from every member of your team. Go to https://drive.google.com to see their Daily Reports.
												- Giving Feedback for Daily Reports
												  collapsed:: true
													- Go into the (Responses) sheet and in the top-right corner 'Share' it with the team member
													- Consider each team member's Daily Report (Responses) spreadsheet as a chatroom. Write down comments for feedback to the team member on a daily basis and keep in mind all management skills
													- Click on the cell in the 'Obstacles' column and click 'Comment'. Add a comment and tell them how you're going to help remove the obstacle out of their way. Reply back and forth until the issue can be 'Resolved' and the obstacle is completely solved
													- If you want a different specific task to be the focus of their next day then tell them in a comment on Asana in their assigned 'NAME Daily Report' task
												- TEMPLATE Daily Report
												  collapsed:: true
													- This is a daily task to complete a report which will be seen by your Manager. This is a way to increase feedback and communication between yourself and your Manager.
													- Please complete this form at the end of the day.
													- (INSERT LINK TO DAILY REPORT FORM HERE)
													- Delete completed subtasks:
													- Completed Daily Report
